user_comment: Nice design, Wish size X-Small was offered Generous cut. She’ll need to grow into these. I ordered in size small but she’s at that in-between stage of moving from teen clothing to women's. She wears a size women's small in Old Navy brands, but these were not flattering on her 5’ 3” (and growing!), 115# frame. They hung on her. I realize they should drape, but the pleating at the waist, and the length in the leg, gave a look like she was dressing in her mother’s slacks. Worn with a high heel (she doesn’t), and given a press, these would be much better. But out of the package, see photos. Here’s what she liked… She said they were comfortable and airy, but not so airy that you don’t get the coverage you need. She liked that the crotch was not skin tight but thought it could have been a little more fitted because the way it fit on her, it would be creased 24-7. She did like that the waist was fitted but not super tight. And, she liked the style details - flared bottoms, paper bag waist, the button closure, pleats and side pockets. She thought the slacks would make a design statement when you don’t want to wear a dress, and she liked that the bow belt was removable, depending on the occasion. The way she’s growing, it won’t be long before these will fit and drape nicely. Currently, her waist is 29.5”, hip 37”, inseam 28-29” and she is wearing the size small in the photo.

LOVE! I am 5'7" and 148 lbs. I actually measured myself before I ordered, referred to the size chart, ordered a large, and they fit perfectly. I have a very long torso, so these are a little longer on me then they would be on most. They would also be adorable at ankle length, if you have longer legs. I actually liked these so much I got on and ordered a grey pair. In the photos I am wearing the mocha brown. The fabric is heavier than I expected which makes the hang beautifully. They are well made and a steal at the price. Dress up or casual. Machine or hand washable. I steamed mine, but think they could be lightly ironed inside out. Love this purchase.

The drape is real What you see is what you get with these. The pleats stay intact with wear, and they are a relaxed straight leg. Very confortable This is my very first review, so I hope this isn't messy hahaha. I love these workout pants. They are high waisted as expected. They have 3 pockets. 2 for phones, keys, things of that sort. One on both sides of your thighs. The other one is on the waistband and it fits a credit or debit card perfectly. Works great for someone like me who doesn't like carrying around a purse and a wallet. I grab my phone, card, keys and am on my way. I attached some pictures in case that helps. I apologize if the quality isn't amazing. When I pulled them out the bag, they looked way too SHORT. I was worried they would fit like kapris on me. To my surprise, they fit all the way down to my ankle. They look smaller than they actually are! They're very stretchy and give me tummy compression as well. They passed the squat test. I always wear nude no-show underwear when working out, so i'm not sure if other types of underwear would affect that or not. They appear to be a thick enough fabric to me. I even measured the pockets and they are about 5 inches deep. I used an iPhone 6s to show how it fits inside. For reference, my measurements are 33-27-37. I weight 126-130 and am 5'7. A size small fit perfectly. I am normally a size small so I'd say they are true to size. If you like form fitting clothes, go with your usual size. I love that as a size small, they actually fit my legs. I usually have issues with smaller sizes looking like high waters on me. I can imagine that larger sizes will fit taller women with no issue. I've never owned high-end workout clothes so I can't compare these to other brands. For the price point, these are pretty damn great. I'll try to update in a few months to see if they still hold up a 5 star rating. Hope this helps!

I always wear nude no-show underwear when working out, so i'm not sure if other types of underwear would affect that or not. They appear to be a thick enough fabric to me. I even measured the pockets and they are about 5 inches deep. I used an iPhone 6s to show how it fits inside. For reference, my measurements are 33-27-37. I weight 126-130 and am 5'7. A size small fit perfectly. I am normally a size small so I'd say they are true to size. If you like form fitting clothes, go with your usual size. I love that as a size small, they actually fit my legs. I usually have issues with smaller sizes looking like high waters on me. I can imagine that larger sizes will fit taller women with no issue. I've never owned high-end workout clothes so I can't compare these to other brands. For the price point, these are pretty damn great. I'll try to update in a few months to see if they still hold up a 5 star rating. Hope this helps!

Def recommend this for Amazon clothes I’ll give the essential info..... • I’m 4’11” and 124lbs • 29”/30” waist, 39” hips with a pretty flat tummy • I purchased a small based off of what other women were saying their measurements were and what size they bought • Def wash these inside out PROS: • It fit nice and snug but not extremely tight that my thighs looked like sausages busting out • the waist is really snug. If my tummy wasn’t more on the flat side I’d probably have to get a medium so I wouldn’t get a high muffin top. • the material is super smooth, love the feel! • I bought 4 different colors and they all look amazing. • The lines/patterns do well to shape you rump • Decent price and quality of product. Especially for amazon clothes. I feel like they never are what you think they are • Colors are shuttle and not as vibrant as they are online but that’s okay. I really like them • Great side pockets for putting your phone or whatever. • Small pocket to put a credit card or a key. CONS: • the material is super smooth, However, I feel as if it could develop those little balls on it after a bunch of washes but we will see. I’ll wash it inside out • I know I’m short but these are super long. I’m going to have to roll these suckers even though I hate doing that.

Perfect for squats and leg workouts For reference I am 5' 3" and 145lbs. I run between a 8-10 in pants. And medium-large. Sometimes leggings fit me too tight in medium or too loose in large. So i started out with a medium, I have a bit of extra meat in my thighs and bum so it did fit a little tight and would kinda slide down when squatting. I switched to the large and it was perfect, i feel the waist a little loose but nothing too crazy. If you are on the thicker side on the thighs and bum and run around my weight/size range, i recommend a large. The length on me is pretty long but can't complain since I am on the shorter side and it is normal for me to find pants longer than i prefer. These are super soft, comfortable for workouts, perfect for squats because of the thickness but not so thick since they are still very breathable. Had 1 pair for over a year and got another just recently. Bought 2 pairs for my friends to try out for their workouts and they love it too. Love that it comes with 2 pockets, holds my phone securely, they are deep enough to hold my Galaxy Note 10. I've had some leggings from other brands where my phone would just slip out of the pocket, because they were too small. Overall excellent product. Do not pass up on these.
Perfect fit Super soft, fits snug and looks great! Vibrant color, not too thick, or thin. I'm 5'2", 132lbs, ordered a medium.
Perfect fit Super soft, fits snug and looks great! Vibrant color, not too thick, or thin. I'm 5'2", 132lbs, ordered a medium.

I love them so much but they arnt perfect First let me say these are amazing. Really I absolutely love the fit of these and the way they feel, I love the that they actually stay put and im not constantly tugging on them. Im 5'7'' 115lbs and I got an xsmall and a small I had to return the small size. The small size seemed a little baggy on the ankle and just lose fitting. I like my legging tight.. So for the reason I gave 3 stars.. I washed these on delicate and air dried them, then I wore them around the house for one day. Just to see how I liked them, with no working out in them or any excessive movements at all. Just normal around the house chores of the day. By the end of the day the leggings looks liked they were pilling already. all over them it just looked like I had worn them many times. I think the material feels great, but I dont want my clothes to look aged so fast. Such a shame because I was willing to buy a ton of these if every color because I really love the way these feel. They feel really high quality and just the most comfy leggings, but I think this will deter me from that.. I may buy a black pair to see if you can see the pilling, but the light pairs are out of question for me..

Hides all the jiggles great. I purchased several to try on and these were my favorites. Still not 100% perfect but they work. Pass the squat test and I do love the pockets. I’m not sure yet if I’m a fan of the little elastic in the top of the band but I do love that on the inside leg where I normally chafe, there is an extra patch of fabric so your legs aren’t rubbing together on a seam but fabric. Brilliant. I’m about 170lbs and 5’4. These do a good job of sucking in my post baby belly. I got a size large and it’s working great for me. I’m sure if I did an x-large it would be a bit roomier but not too loose. My only qualm is with one of the seams, the threads are coming apart after only wearing them once.

Comfy but not great for running Pretty comfy, the fabric feels really nice. The way they're designed they kind of squeeze you out though. Not too tight, but they feel like they want to creep down. Did high knees and they dropped pretty far at the waist, but squats and up-downs went fine. Probably better for yoga or weightlifting or just leisure but not so much running or high impact movements. Pockets along the legs are nice but I'm not sure I'd ever use them, also one sewn in the front of the waistband. Came all the way to my natural waist with come coaxing but settled at my hips. Fabric doesnt seem seethrough at all. Ordered XXL, I'm 5'6" about 250#

Stand apart from the rest. Excellent quality. No camel toe! In the last week or so I have ordered most of the main/best leggings and running tights from Amazon (including Colorful Koala, occfy) and these really may be the best! Excellent high waist, nearly long enough for my 5'11" frame (close to a 7/8 length), and greatest of all--no camel toe! I'm 5'11", 115lb, and the xsmall fits perfectly. No sagging in the bum or crotch and really the most "seamless" looking crotch of all of the leggings. I think that front seam and the gusset on the inside make this the case. Got the gray color and they are definitely pretty and buttery soft. Dying for the "Bordeaux" or maroon pair next! Would recommend 10/10. I have a small butt and these work the best of any leggings so far--no flattening effect at all. True to size. Arrived quickly and in the standard nice zip lock bag. These really are great. I'm in love!

See through, not for hard workouts Soft like one of those really soft pj shirts. Light and silky. These are not compression. My waist to hip ratio is nothing dramatic but the waistband was not strong enough to keep these up more than 1/4 through my HITT leg day work out. The pockets are not strong enough to hold in my iphone 8 plus. I would not put my phone in them or really anything. The seam under the waist across the butt is annoying and honestly creates an uncomfortable bulging that had me constantly trying to hike them up. If youre looking for something to lounge in, 100% buy these. If youre looking to buts out a hard work out, i wouldnt. They are absolutely see through. I will not post a photo of my butt proving so. But i can see my knees through them. The ankles are loose and though this isnt a big deal, it bothers me. They look nice enough. But i wont be buting more. If youre looking for the impressive VS workout leggings dont buy these but possibly something a little pricier or even high waisted fablitics leggings. Yeah. I prefer being trapped into the scheme that is Fabletics to get averagely priced leggings that keep me feeling confidant over these. Im 5’8” 140lbs and these are a small. If they were thicker i could probably fit great into an XS

But very happy with these leggings overall, especially the price point!

Super comfortable and soft My daughter loves these leggings! She says they have just the right amount of compression, and the fabric is thick enough, but super soft. It’s matte, not at all shiny, but still doesn’t pick up pet hairs or lint like some do. She ordered her regular size small, and they are true to size, and even long enough for her 5’8” height. Love that there are pockets! There are also cute stitching details, like at the back of the knees, that make them look unique. We will definitely be buying more of these in different colors, especially since the price is so great.

Great pants for a great price I just bought these a week or so ago and have already worn them at least 3x. These pants are soft, stretchy enough to move around in (squat proof) yet snug enough to hold everything in. When I first tried them on I noticed the ankles had some space, and occasionally I catch myself pulling them up so they stay high-waisted. Those would be my only “dislikes.” For reference, I’m 5’2 and 120lbs, ordered a size small.

I'm 5'4" and 208 lb.

Absolutely 100% approve of these leggings. I work out 6 days a week and these were better then expected for high impact...the best part is they are squat proof!! This is a huge deal for me. I am 5’8” and 178 lbs. I ordered a large and they fit perfect not to tight not to big. The fabrics is soft and oh my super comfy. I will definitely be buying another pair!

These are ok but not for tall ladies These leggings are ok but for sure not for tall girls. After reading other reviews that said they were too long, I was really hopeful that these would work for me. I'm 5' 11" and they are too short for me. I could easily have used 2 more inches. Other then the length, these leggings seem nice and are very soft. I probably could have went with a medium instead of a large but then they would have been even shorter. I will be using them too work out and I'm usure if they will stay up while I run. 🤷 The do seem squat proof. For reference I'm around 185 lbs and have alot of my height in my legs.

Love them, worth the money! I don’t normally write reviews but I really love these leggings! I was hesitant because i wasn’t sure if the material would be how I wanted but they feel just like fabletics leggings and they’re great for working out. I also really appreciate that they have pockets and they’re not see-through. For reference I’m 5’3” at 120 lbs and I got a small. I’m thinking about buying another pair in a different color so I highly recommend!

If I could change anything.. 5’3 and 225lbs. Usually size 18/20 in jeans. Ordered a 2x, kinda expected them to be more loose at the waist, but they’re fine for compressing. Don't pay full price These are super comfy and the pocket holds my phone nicely while I'm cycling. I washed these once on delicate and let them air dry. As soon as I put them on I noticed they had started coming apart at the seams after just one wash. Very disappointing but I got them on sale & only paid $13 so I don't mind having to sew them. I'm not sure how many washes they'll hold up for, even on delicate. My advice is wait for them to go on sale, they are not worth more than $13. I am 5'3" & 135lbs, the Medium fit perfect.

Butter soft legging good for a good workout or just wear daily as sports wear! I love these leggings! So soft and flexible. High waisted and do not roll. I feel legging roll when they are worn too small most of the time or dont have enough flexibility. Im 43 and they hold in and make my csection baby lower belly look great! I got the capri length also and love them also. I also love the length and I am 5’8 195. I wear a L.

Ordering a 2nd pair!! I’m in LOVE!! I have issues with legging that let your buns jiggle. These are perfect. They don’t show my underwear, the don’t let my thunder thighs jiggle and they smooth over my cellulite. The pockets hold my IPhone 8 Plus just fine. Will order another pair and wish they offered a pair without pockets. I looked on the sellers amazon store and couldn’t find any. *5’8” 230# and ordered an xl. Everything you need in a workout legging! I was skeptical at first but will be buying more!!! These are flattering and shows off every inch of muscle definition in legs! Holds Up Over Time Super comfy they hold up well even after many washes and wears. Im 5’4 and 168 lbs and got a size large. They are a little larger than I prefer. I’ll go with the medium next time but still a great set of leggings, the pockets are awesome.

Not that weird Shiney material Material is thicker and not that Shiney stuff like some leggings. Im 5'3, 107 lbs. I got a size small and they fit great except a little loose n the butt area. But tbats all me...im working on that! Haha definitely recommend this brand. Will buy again.

super comfy and cute! I'm about 5'7 and ordered a small. as soon as I took them out of the package, I knew they'll easily be my favorite leggings. the material is strong and very stretchy and soft to the touch. I was however under the notion they would lift my butt more, but oh well. Get them!

Great leggings for a great price! These are great leggings for an even better price! They’re soft, comfortable and NOT see-through! They do show sweat, at least the color I bought (mauve), but I don’t care bc I only practice yoga at home. They’re true to size, I got a medium, I’m 5’8” and 150lbs.

Short curvy girl approved I am 5’2”, 142 pounds and very pear shaped. I ordered a medium. Length is perfect! I didn’t want cropped pants, so these are the perfect petite length. I was worried about these fitting weird around my waist- I deal with IBS bloat and am horribly self conscious about waist lines. The fit is comfy given my large hips, thick thighs, and will be comfy and flattering around my IBS flare ups. Fabric is actually flattering! I tested a bunch of linen pants at various shops recently and didn’t like the thin, see through linen (again, sensitive about my big hips.). These are a little thicker, and while the fabric is a little rougher (I suspect they’ll soften in the wash with some fabric softener), they’ll wear much better. And, the pricing here really can’t be beat for the quality. I ordered the striped navy blue. Navy is super dark (my lighting in pics is bad, sorry!), so the pinstripes pop nicely. I’m considering ordering another pair before my upcoming trip! These will travel so well, I can already tell. In a nutshell- yes, buy them.

Finally found my linen pants! The pants I received are 50% linen / 50% cotton, so there may be some shrinkage. Keeping this in mind, I had ordered a large and a medium. I went with the medium because I felt like I swam in the large and if I wash in cold and line dry, shrinkage should be at a minimum. Great value for your money, not super soft, but not uncomfortably scratchy either. I’m almost 5’2” and 175lbs, plus I’ve got hips! For those fun size gals whose hips don’t lie, I think depending on the look you want, either size would look great on you!

Size up! Love these!! I got them in a large but am usually a medium, and the large fits perfectly. I’m 5’3, 140 lbs and usually size 6 or 28/28 in bottoms. One thing to note — I wear these high waisted unlike the model pictures where it’s at their hips. Wearing them high waisted, they fit perfectly around my waist and hips and are also the perfect length, usually pants are too long on me but these are the perfect full length. I also love that they are 100% cotton! The material is soft and light but not thin or staticky. It’s just 1 cotton layer so it’s not super thick but I feel like it would be warm enough for any inside wear.

Waist is tight, they run small and short, they shrink! I'm very disappointed in these pants, as they are a cute design and looked like they'd be comfortable. But I can't really wear them after washing them. For reference, I am 5'7" and 133#. I ordered size Small. My photos were taken BEFORE I washed them... 1) There are many loose threads hanging off these pants, due to low quality control. 2) The elastic waist is very tight. Too tight for a supposedly comfy pajama/lounge pant, especially one worn mid-rise. I think buyers should size-UP. 3) These pants are 100% Cotton, and definitely shrank after the first wash in cold water and tumble dry on Low heat. Now they feel even smaller and are too short for me. In general, I recommend that buyers SIZE-UP by 1-2 sizes, and either hang dry or dry on low heat for very short time. Too bad the quality is low, because the navy/white gingham design and "boxer" look is cute.

I accidentally ordered these in the medium (I meant to select L but it changed when I opted for a different colour) so I didn't expect these to fit. I wear a UK size 16-18 and although I did manage to squeeze into them, realistically the M stretches to a UK size 14. These would be great for summer nightwear, they're very lightwight, however, they're also quite opaque (and not from stretch). The legs are quite long and wide, which I like for sleepwear but I'm 5'7" - the length was perfect on me, shorter wearers might be tripping on them. Mostly well-made, no loose-cotton and even stitching. They have pockets which is a bonus. Wrong size was on me, but I think for a comfortable fit, I'd have needed the XL.

Super high waist but very little shape I’m 5’6”, 129 lbs., and wearing the medium (8-10) in my photo. I’m normally a consistent size 4 in US pants/jeans but I’ve learned to order up from Amazon. I’m happy with the fit of the medium. The fabric is lightweight and feels like a thick chiffon. The pants have a button/zipper fly and deep hip pockets but the back pockets are fake. There are belt loops on the waistband but the back is elastic so the belt is optional. The color is a deep navy. The legs are long enough that I have to wear at least a short heel to keep them from dragging the ground. What I like: 1. The super high waist and wide leg style. 2. Lightweight and flowy, good for summer work pants. 3. Real (and deep) pockets. What I don’t like: 1. The material has a nylon pantyhose feel, just scratchy enough to be noticeable. 2. Profile of the pants is kind of shapeless. The fabric just hangs and does very little to flatter the figure.

Ugh Amazing! I'm screaming at how perfect these pants are! When I first felt them out of the package I immidiately noticed the thickness of the fabric. I'm usually a size 8/10 and these are a little loose at the top of my waist, but these pants fall really nicely on my thigh and leg area. They have some stretch, but aren't body con. I'm 5'7 and these fit like proper cropped pants on me. These are great for work or for going out. I haven't been so happy about a pair of pants in a long time!

Runs big These jeans are so cute! Initially when I ordered, I went with amazons suggestion (which is usually pretty spot on), and ordered a medium. They were huge, but I loved them so I reordered in a small. Honestly, after getting them and wearing them, I could have done an extra small. They are a mid-rise fit, super stretchy, and comfortable (like no joke ladies if you’re still dropping it like it’s hot, these are for you). They are the perfect cropped length (I am 5’7), the white is not see through (I’m wearing magenta underwear in the pics), and I am seriously obsessed with the flared bottom detail. I was really hoping to just pop these in the dryer for some shrink action, but the label specifically says no dryer so I’m rolling with the looser fit. These definitely look more high end than they are, and I’m probably going to order the Jean version because I like them so much. I definitely recommend, but would suggest sizing down. For reference I am pretty universally a size 3 or small in most pants.

Tall girls wanted Here I go again, ordering pants I know will not suit a vertically-challenged chick as myself (5’1”). Sometimes it works out considering how you wear it, yes? These jeans, size small in medium blue, were not the case. I had to wear them below my waist/belly button. Otherwise, they would be too loose, requiring a belt.There is some stretch to these pants so I could pull them on and off without unbuttoning. Not that it matters anyway since there is extra fabric everywhere. I think if the hemline didn’t flare, these jeans would have been ok on me. They remind me of another pair I ordered here on the Amazon. But those worked better being wide-legged.I’ll keep these for now and hope I grow five inches overnight. The denim is medium weight. The quality is comparable to Gap. In the meantime, I’ll pair them with anything but flats or flip flops. Fake it til you make it as the fashion world says it.

I would recommend buying these!

Stretchy and Comfy I bought these to wear for work. I was touring a new cruise ship and needed something stylish but easy to move around in. These hit the spot. I do agree with others that the waistline is tapered but, for me, they worked ok. I have an hourglass figure so my body tapers at the waist naturally. If you have an apple shape, you will need to size up. I’m 5’4”, 160 pounds and the medium fit well. If I weighed a smidge over 160, they wouldn’t work. The fabric is soft and wasn’t see through with my skin tone underwear. The pockets sit high on the front and while functional, are a little awkwardly placed for truly holding items. There are pockets on the back that work great for normal use. This will be a staple in my wardrobe. I really enjoyed wearing them.

Super comfortable! I'm a guy who's recently discovered the joys of yoga pants. They are so comfortable! Unfortunately, as a 6'3" person, I literally can't find male leggings in my size. To boot, guys' clothes will always have tight ankles, which makes it look more like long underwear rather than pants. These Marika pants are great. The material is high-quality, mostly cotton and very breathable. The fit is flattering -- tighter around the thighs, flaring out past the knees. The impression is very much 'pants' and not 'ballet tights,' though I do wear longer shirts with these to cover my waist and rear. Some pilling on the inner thighs, but that's happened with every cotton pair of yoga pants that I've purchased. Additionally, I've yet to see shrinkage in the wash or the elastic waistband wearing out -- the elastic band seems VERY thick and durable: it seems to be much better quality than cheaper pants I've bought. All in all, these are great, and the color choices are excellent too. Both the Ponderosa Pine and the Zinfandel look great :)

Surprisingly nice When these arrived, they looked liked they'd run small or be too thin of fabric since they are really light. I waited a while to try them on because I didn't anticipate being able to wear them out of the house. Happily, they fit really well and don't show through! I'm 5'9" and inseams often run short for me, but these were just fine. Both standing and bending over did not show the print of the underwear I had on under these, as you can see in the pics. They are really smooth, so if you wear them with a sweater or other cotton top, they won't cling. I wish they had pockets, but for the number of maternity pants I am finding that are transparent, I can live without that as long as they cover my butt! Adequately stretchy and comfortable, even over the baby bump! Very happy!

Comfortable Easy Going Fit & Style The Maxbee Capris are the most comfortable pants. The material is soft and lightweight with decent stretch. They're breezy even on a hot day and go with almost everything. My wife has another Maxbee piece and so far, their clothes deliver on comfort and style. These capris have a drawstring and stretch waist. It conforms to the body without any pinching and it falls loosely around the derriere and legs. There's no bunching at the thighs or wedgie. They're also easy wash and hang dry in no time without wrinkles. ~ Kort

Comfy lounge pants These are decent capri pants but, for me, they feel more like pajama bottoms or lounging wear. They are thin -- not see-through thin -- but lightweight pants. I have a size 30 waist, am 5'8" and weigh about 155, give or take a few pounds. These fit very well but I think I could have actually sized down for a more trim fit.The cut of the pants are different from other workout capris I own. There a lot of stretch and a baggier fit to the legs. I'm used to workout capris that fit snug to the body. These are much more looser in fit. I think I might have been happier with a size medium as there is plenty of room in the stomach area. If you carry a bit of weight, these will not disappoint. The material is very stretchy and soft.

I was hoping to wear these to work, but will probably just use them for casual wear instead.

Awkward proportions but good workmanship These are every bit as good as Smartwool 200 gsm merino leggings although a bit scratchier, and the elasticized cuff is way better than the Smartwool’s for petite people, keeping all the excess length above your ankle. As another reviewer noted, the proportions are bizarre. The medium has a 28” long inseam paired with a weirdly short rise (7-8”), so it ends up being BOTH too long in the legs AND almost too short in the torso. I’m built like a boy and have a super short torso, and it’s still barely up to my bellybutton. I can’t imagine how it would work on anyone with a long torso or bigger than a 40” hip unless they want to stretch these things to their limits. Highly recommended if you’re a 5’7”, 130 lb, 37” hip, built-like-a-rail Nordic swimmer who likes low-rise wool leggings. Still recommended if you’re a short, slightly overweight rectangle who is going to hide them under snow pants and drysuits. .

Warm, cozy, opaque and breathable. These are equally comfortable lounging around the house (68-70F) and out for chilly walks (35-43F). They are nicely opaque, and wear well. No signs of pilling to date. I’m a pear-shape, currently ~145lb and bought the large. They’re a wee bit loose; could likely have bought the medium for a more fitted look. Because I’m short (5’2”) with short legs, I fold up the bottom cuffs. If worn multiple days (I use them for traveling), the knees do stretch out a little, but return to shape when washed. I wash them on gentle cycle and line dry, or dry on delicate and there’s been no sign of shrinking. Bought two pairs; black and the white/fawn stripes (which are really fun).

A LITTLE HIGH IN THE CROTCH, BUT NICE! I was looking for a wide-legged pant to go with my top. I purchased these in Orange and Red in sizes L, XL and XXL. I ended up going with the Orange pants in XL. At the time, I was about 210 lbs and I am 5'5".These are nice pants. They are a bit thin, so when I wore them, I put on a boy short underneath to smooth out the look. The crotch area is a bit high for my taste. So, even though they fit great, the crotch area can look a bit tight, giving the perception that they are too small, which was not the case. With a slight heel, I was great to go without having to hem them.I felt the Orange color 'popped' better over the Red, but both are nice colors.

Want to love them: sizing down and will review again Updated review: I returned both sizes. They are just awful. I've lost a lot of weight and switch between anywhere from M,L,XL depending on the brand (not weight fluctuatation). I have larger breasts, wide hips and butt. Pros: good quality, comfortable, elastic band and button/zipper, big pockets, high waisted but could be worn lower Cons I saw another reviewer ask if these pants give you "sad butt" and I'm here to say and show the proof.... yes... yes they do give you sad butt. My butt is not happy 😆🤧 Length is kind of weird. I'm 5 foot 4" and they definitely don't fit me cropped even pulled up as high as they'll sit without a belt. They are hitting my ankles and top of feet in a way that evokes memories of childhood pants that had shrunk too short. Conclusion I'm going to order a pair in medium and update. I gave the pants a good review because it's not the pants fault I'm in between sizes and short. And I knew what I was getting into with the "sad butt" which hopefully will be remedied by a smaller size. We shall see.

My new favorite: a lovely, natural fiber, comfortable pair of pants for fall and spring weather Overview: These pants are comfortable yet elegant. Ignore the part of the listing which says cotton: they are entirely of linen. The pants are a great weight for fall and spring- a bit warmer, yet still lightweight. The grey color is a lovely neutral tone which matches a variety of colors and styles of tops.Sizing and fit: I am a size 8-10, 5'4" with a high waist. I ordered a size medium. These pants fit me perfectly, with the legs hitting exactly at the ankle (!!!). The cut is a baggy or comfortable fit. The ankles on the pants I received are tapered, more so than I expected from the pictures, and I prefer that. I would NOT consider these plus size by American standards. I used the sizing chart on the listing, so make sure you plug your measurements into that chart- from my experience, it seems accurate.Pros:-These are so, so comfortable and fit really well- not too short, nor too long.-The relaxed fit is really nice for working from home, or a dinner date. They dressed up well with a nice shirt, and also work for casual situations.-The fabric seems to be of a good quality with a fairly tight weave (this is why I recommend the pants for spring/fall weather).-The seams are sewn with what looks like a cotton basting or hemming tape. This means that none of the seams are exposed. This probably makes them more comfortable, and will also increase the longevity of the pants. The fabrication of these pants looks very high quality to me. (See picture)Cons:-Because these are entirely linen, I find them to be slightly scratchy in warmer temperatures (above 70*), so my personal preference is to wear these in mild weather, around 65*- 75*. At cooler temperatures the fabric does not feel scratchy/ prickly to me. However, if you are someone that needs their clothing to be super soft, these might not be the right pants for you.-Because they are entirely linen, they wrinkle easily. If you want to wear them for a night out, I would iron them first. I've included one picture of how they look after ironing, which is marked; the other pictures are of the pants which were initially ironed after wearing them for a while.-The taping in the crotch seams made that area seem a bit bulky at first, but as I wear them it does not bother me.

Sizing NOT Right. 1st time I bought a grey in small, inseam (it's only 28.5inch) isn't as long as described on size chart (whixh I still holding on to, still not sure if I would keep it or not), so I bought the Khaki in medium, hoping it would longer - Wrong! For the price seller is selling is not worth it at all with all the wrong measurements. I bought few pants with similar material from Singapore for only US$16 - $17 a piece (only its a little thicker, which is popularly called as scuba material pants). I thought I could find something similar on Amazon US, and only if only this Amazon seller gives the correct measurements it could have been less hassles and frustration! For ref I am 5ft 4 - 5 inch, 128 - 130 lbs, waist 27"-28", hip 37".

Mizuno, our go to for softball pants Mizuno is my daughter's go to brand for softball pants. They fit her so flatteringly..(is that a word?!) She wears a small and is and a size 2, and is 5'2", about 108lbs. 2 Are fairly thick, but have nice stretch and usually last more than one season!!

Really classy pants These pants are really nice, especially for the price. There really classy looking and quite comfortable (fabric is 100% polyester). It feels like you’re not wearing anything as they are so lightweight. These would go great for work or even to wear casually. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and ordered a large because I do not like anything at all tight on my waist, and these fit well, but I would have been fine with a medium because they do stretch. I would say they are true to size. Overall I think these are totally worth it.

Pleasant Mistake I blame myself for not reading the title more closely. I was looking for hiking pants with good reviews. I saw the photos with long pants. I got the package on time and was wondering where the lower half of the pants went when I pulled them out of the package. That's when I realized I ordered the Capris. Honestly, feeling the material I would have bypassed these in the store. I am 5'6 and weigh 150-152 pounds. I ordered the large size. They fit just right. I worried about chaffing and overall quality. I was going to return them but hate the return process. I decided to wear them on my 7 miles in, 7 miles back overnight 1st backpacking trip in years. I was pleasantly surprised. Absolutely no chaffing and had multiple river crossings knee to mid-thigh water deep. Within 20 minutes the pants were completely dry every single time. A companion wore jeans and they never fully dried and he was miserable, especially when the temperature dropped at night. I expected snags from all the desert brush along the trails. Not one snag. I would absolutely recommend these Capris. Now I just need to find these in pants. I was not paid for this review.

Nice pants made of cool material I ordered my First pair very large 3x because I thought they would either shrink or be small and they are very large and didn’t shrink. I ordered a smaller pair, XL and they fit great . They are made very well. They are definitely cool. I wanted them because it is very hot where I work and it did help. they have a drawstring so I’m able to adjust the waist so they don’t fall down all day and they have a back pocket and a front pocket on the leg that I can put my large phone in. They also have two other pockets in the normal place on the front, all of the pockets have a zipper on them so you can close them. I have been very happy with these pants. They’re very lightweight and thin. They fit my 5’6” 175 to 180 bottom heavy frame.

Be aware and check all your pockets on this pants mine came sewn shut on the leg pocket I attached a photo to show the stitched close pocket Ordered a second pair and they were so small on the thighs versus this pair. I have a black pair and those fit great. Really wanted the green but I’m afraid to order another pair since the two problems with the pocket stitched closed and second pair was way smaller. Update ordered another pair different color and those are perfect. I have 4 pairs now size med and they do stretch when you wear them length and size perfect for my body 5’2 155 I have big thighs ,big but and a small waist

I needed capris for therapy. I went cheap-er rather than going with the brand I really like. I’m only 5’3” 110 lbs and purchased a small. These look like old woman capris on me because they are long and bulky. I should have sent them back. I’ll wear them to work in the yard. There are some nice features like lots of pockets. The one weird issue is that the packaging lines in the fabric will not go away. I’ve washed, and washed again. I think these are listed as water repellent? Anyway, I put them in the dryer even though it says not to. I thought the lines would steam out. Nope. Lesson learned. I’m going back to Mountain Hardware. Expensive, but work for me.

I’m 5’10 female with 36” inseam I am a 5’10 female with a 36” inseam, and I weigh around 110lbs- I am very slender so I purchased the small. The length is fairly good for me- they sit at about kapri level for me. These are much longer than I expected because for me women’s pants are always shorts! The waist is perfect for me I have a 23” around the waist so it is a perfect fit! I can’t imagine being shorter than 5’7 and these fitting. And I can’t imagine being over 150 lbs and these fitting for you. Definitely made for people with athletic builds. They are very cool and it is in the 90s here and I wear these outside walking or biking. Highly recommend for ya taller folks out there!!!

GREAT PANTS for Travel/outdoor/rain I recently went to Alaska and bought these Navy, super comfortable and practical pants for outdoor excursions. I am 5 feet, wear a size 6; "small" fit perfectly (I tried the medium but too big). They are a little stretchy & lightweight, the zippers are good and definitely repelled the rain on a whale watch. I highly recommend them for walks/hikes/campfire or every day; good price too!!

Extremely comfortable, water resistant, and windproof! I bought these to go kayaking in FL in December. They absolutely are water resistant, as well as super comfortable. The size chart was spot on. I'm 5'6", 145 lbs, 29" waist, 41" hip and the medium (8-10) fit perfectly. The material is a bit stretchy too. I also wore them to hike in WI. They absolutely are windproof. It was 35F and windy on the shore of Lake Michigan. I wore these over a heavy base layer and was very comfortable. The 4 very large mesh zippered pockets (see pictures of pants inside out) easily hold the largest of cell phones. The option to cinch the hem can give these more of a "jogger" look.

Comfortable with lots of pockets These pants have a nice stretch to the material and the back half of the waist is made of elastic, which means that the pants will stretch to fit any body type. I am very curvy so the pants are form fitting in the top but loose below the thigh. They don't appear as wide legged on me as they do the model. I really liked that they were wide leg but on me they just look like regular slacks. I'm not sure of that's just because of my body type or because the style of the pants that I received doesn't quite match the style pictured. There are a ton of pockets. One pocket closes with a zipper while the other 7 pockets either don't have a seal or close with velcro. I would've preferred that all pockets close with a zipper (for cleaner lines), but these cargo pants are the traditional style with flap pockets all over. The length of the pants is nice as well. I'm 5'3"but I have long legs. So I believe these pants would fit most people. Very tall ladies might end up in crops. The material is thin but durable, and quick dry, so these pants would be good in hot weather. I haven't had an opportunity to wear them on a bike yet but I've worn them around the house and they are very comfortable.

My wifes first pair of cargo pants & she loves them! I ordered these for my wife as she fancied something different to wear to her normal type of pants. I ordered her normal size which is a small (size 4). She is 5'4" and around 130lbs just to give you an idea with regards to the fit. She was really surprised with the material. The fabric feels very durable, smooth and crispy but comfortable. She loves the cut and said that these fit her perfectly. The waist is fastened with a popper and Velcro with elastic around the back for a nice snug fit. The bottom of the legs can be rouched or left wide which gives these pants great versatility. The toggles to rouch the bottoms tuck in nicely without getting in the way. The pockets on the thighs are fastened with Velcro. The pockets near the waist, one has a zipper and the other is an open pocket. The two pockets at the back are fastened with Velcro. It is worth noting that these are machine washable on a gentle cycle and low tumble dry. My wife is totally in love with these pants and cannot recommend them enough. The quality is faultless and she thinks the price is great.

Feel super comfortable! I love the feel of these leggings. They're very soft. The waist reaches above my belly button. There's not much compression to them so don't expect them to change your figure. I'm 5'5" and there's a tiny amount of bunching at my ankles, which I prefer to them being too short. I'm not a fan of the 7/8 styles and I'd consider these to be truly full length. My biggest complaint is that the outline of where the pockets lay is very obvious. Pockets easily fit my phone, which is nice, but I'd prefer if they weren't as noticeable. I'd wear them with long shirts only unless I really don't care that day. I also workout frequently and suspect that sweat will be obvious and my legs might feel too warm. In conclusion, I'd highly recommend these for comfort and lounging but not necessarily for activewear or styling with normal length or cropped shirts

My biggest complaint is that the outline of where the pockets lay is very obvious. Pockets easily fit my phone, which is nice, but I'd prefer if they weren't as noticeable. I'd wear them with long shirts only unless I really don't care that day. I also workout frequently and suspect that sweat will be obvious and my legs might feel too warm. In conclusion, I'd highly recommend these for comfort and lounging but not necessarily for activewear or styling with normal length or cropped shirts

If they would of made allowance for sewing on the handkerchief panels on then the pant would of had a better fit also .. giving it two stars because I plan on keeping it to repurpose it in another costume but If quality doesn’t matter to you and you are petite it should work for you the costume will look good on stage especially the lower half

I wanted to love these! I wanted to love these leggings! The leggings were packaged very well which I appreciated. They were folded very nicely and placed into a little zip pouch that was clear on one side and sort of opaque on the other. Feel of the leggings were...prerty okay. They were kinda soft, but had a bit of a 'glossy sheen' to them -if that makes any sense. I bought the ones with the owls on them. My intention was to use these for workouts. I couldn't wait to try them on! BUT, when I did, the poor owls were stretched to the max, and looked a bit distorted. Also, after I pulled them up they immediately began to slide back down. They were too low on the waste and came a couple inches or so above my ankles (I'm 5ft5). I am a true Medium in leggings, and can sometimes wear a small. In pants I wear from a 7 to a 9 (juniors, and just depends). I have thick muscular thighs from lifting weights, and a pretty decent sized booty. So if you are curvier, these likely may not work for you. If your more on the slim or petite size, then your probably good. These shipped very fast, and I've had no interactions/issues with sellers. Not sure I will bother trying any of the other designs as I'd planned.

I would give the leggings 4.5 stars mainly because the material feels like cotton (the title in my order list also says cotton) but the label says nylon and polyester. However, I really like the fun color of the leggings which is very different from my other leggings. The material feels not as stretchy as some and I guess because it contains some cotton. The leggings fits as expected though. I ordered size small and I’m 5’4” and 115 lbs for reference. There is not much compression around the waist but the leggings don’t really ride down during my 7 mile run. This is an important factor for me because it really frustrates me when I have to constantly pull my leggings up during a long run. It is sweat wicking but like most cotton materials, it doesn’t get dry fast and basically sweat weighs down the leggings a bit. Overall, I like the leggings a lot. I only wore them once and very happy to run in them. Only wish the label description was more accurate.

Fits awkward body type! I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

Bring on the cold weather!! Be still my cuddly-fleece-loving heart -- these fleece lined leggings are amazing!! I have never looked forward to walking the dog in the cold weather more than I am right now! I practically live in leggings 24/7, and I have some thicker cold-weather leggings, but nothing like these. These are fantastic!⛄ These are fleece lined all the way from just below the waistband (the only part not fleece-lined but still very thick) to the ankles. And I am talking sherpa blanket type fleece -- cozy, cuddly fleece. They are pretty thick (about equal to me wearing an under layer + normal thick leggings) but they are very soft and stretchy -- literally like they took a sherpa fleece blanket and made it into leggings. They are also super easy to get on (sometimes the thicker leggings are stiff and don't give as much, but not these) -- they are like slipping on comfy PJs.⛄SIZING: I went with my normal legging size (8/10-Large). For reference I am 5'5", slightly fluffy mid-section. These fit very comfortably. They have a little extra length in the ankle (the way a 24-26" inseam legging normally fits on me) -- but I like that in the cold weather so my ankles aren't exposed to the wind. The waist does have an enclosed top elastic band and I would consider these high waisted (above my belly button), but I do not find these restrictive or compressive at all and they do not roll. I don't think the crotch area is particularly flattering as there is a bit of extra material there along with the central seam-- but I am not wearing these to a fashion show, but to stay warm so I can't really complain. I would recommend staying true to size on these. You can certainly size down if you want compression, but these aren't really for body shaping. I definitely would not size up.⛄There are no seams on the front, back or outside of the leg (only up the middle of the leg so it doesn't show) -- so I would consider these pretty much seamless.I cannot express how cozy and comfortable these are. Right now, I am torn because I don't want to take them off, but I am also melting in the summer heat. I don't usually relish being out in the cold to walk the dog or being at really cold ice arenas for hockey -- but I absolutely cannot wait to see how much of a difference these make! 100% recommend. ❤

Superior Leggings for Winter These pants are truly superior to any warm leggings I’ve worn in the past, and I only wish I had discovered them years ago. They are perfect for those cold New England nights when I need to shovel snow or brush off the car. I’m also planning to take them with me on my upcoming travels to northern Europe during the winter. They are incredibly comfortable and soft making them ideal for any outdoor activities. I carry my weight in my hips and thighs, and these pants fit perfectly without feeling restrictive. There's just the right amount of stretch, so I don't feel like I’m being squeezed into them. At 5'9" and 160 pounds, I often struggle to find pants that are long enough to reach my ankles, as many get pulled up by my hips and thighs. These pants, however, offer the ideal length, which is a rare find for my height and shape. The fleece lining keeps the feel of these pants soft and comfortable, making them even more perfect for cold weather. At this price point, it's truly a product not to pass up! The warmth, comfort, and quality make them an absolute must-have. I honestly wish I could give these more than five stars—they’ve exceeded all of my expectations!

Size up if you are a between size I am 5’6.5”, about 143-145 lbs, with a slightly athletic/muscular build. I tend to wear a 26/27 in pants and the medium fit me nicely. I also tried a size small (also pictured) but they were too tight in the mid thigh. Quick review version: pant material is nicer and thicker than expected, love that the waist band is smooth and not elastic. Nice length and overall shape. I can wear these with a pair of sneakers and the ends won’t drag. Longer/in depth: as mentioned, I have a bit more muscle, especially in the thighs. I first bought the small based on reviews and size guide. The waist fit me well, but the actual leg part was a bit too tight around my mid thigh. My pictures show me in the medium and small. You can see that the small is more tight in the thigh which causes a bit of buckling/a pouch to form at the top. The medium stays flowy through the leg. The waist on the medium is not too much larger and still fits me fine. These pants are good for cold weather or cold offices, but you could also probably wear them with a t shirt and not be too hot.

Linen-Like, Casual Summer Pants For reference, I am 5’2” and a size 4 (27”) waist. The length of these pants were great on me, but seeing as how they were a perfect fit length wise, I could see that being problematic for anyone more than a few inches taller than me. The material is lightweight (linen-like) and the waist is elastic. The fabric itself is not stretchy, so I would say the waist is only moderately stretchy (limited by the actual fabric itself). I love the look of paper bag waist pants, but just have not found them to be that flattering on my body type (straight, no hips). Not an issue with these pants, just an overall life gripe! I will enjoy these in the summer with a cute pair of casual sandals and maybe a white or black casual tee on top.

SIZE UP! I am 60 y.o., almost 5'1", about 118 soft lbs. Carry most of my weight in lower stomach (30") and butt/hips(39"). The fit here totally depends on your preference. They were too baggy but I washed on cold and dried on low and they shrunk just enough - maybe an inch everywhere (inseam is now 25"). The rise is still a bit low which makes them hang longer. They came out of the dryer just the way you see in the 2 "after" pics - no need to iron any serious wrinkles. Love the fabric for summer - light enough but not too thin or see-through. Very comfortable and DO have 3% stretch all around. Getting more colors... pics are before and after wash/dry.

Great jeggings! Will definitely buy more! Being a larger lady I have always hidden myself behind baggy tops and pants. Someone advised me to try cropped jeggings for a change and I’m so glad I did. These are lovely! They look and feel like proper jeans (not just like denim-look fabric) but are so stretchy and comfortable. Worst fit ever I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

Good fitting jeggings. Look like real jeans. My jeans are all a bit tight after I gained 10 pounds so I have been on the look out for jean-like leggings that didn't look like too much like fake jeans. Not an easy thing to find. These No Nonsense Women's Denim Legging actually look like jeans. They are not thick and stiff like jeans but they have good texture and coloring. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. The length is good and they seem to be made well. I will probably buy a pair or two more. They're not warm but definitely not see through or flimsy. If they were just a bit thicker for cooler weather, they would be just about perfect. They are the most realistic looking jean leggings I have found so far.

Tears easily I was actually surprised this was my fav pair to try of the 3 different brands I ordered. They’re super comfy. I’m muscular in the legs and am 5’0 tall. I do NOT put my hands in my back pockets much with an occasional phone, which is in my right back pocket. The left has started to tear at the corner already, and I’ve worn these a handful of times, mainly at work for casual Fridays. I can sew them back with reinforcement, though it’s only been 6 weeks since I bought them. I’ll be ordering another pair. The hole is the only reason it’s a 4 star.

Not bad I’m 11 weeks post partum and struggling to find clothes I feel comfortable in. Pre-pregnancy I was 115 lbs and a pretty standard size 4 in bottoms and small in tops. I’m right at 135 now and unable to fit in most of my clothes, but have been hesitant to spend too much knowing ultimately I’d like to lose some weight. I’m probably between sizes 6 and 8 now depending on the brand and material. I am 5’4” for reference. Best guess on my current bra size is a D. Sharing all this just to make it clear, I don’t really have a “normal” to use as a size comparison right now. Also offering up having a newborn as an excuse as to why my mirror and room are such a mess lol. After reading reviews I ordered a medium, and I’m glad I did. I think I would have struggled to squeeze into a small, and a large would have been too big. I know not to expect top of the line quality from most Amazon clothes, especially in this price range. The quality was about what I expected, not terrible but obviously not top of the line.

The small is too loose for xsmall wearers I'm 5'2" & at the time of the photos, I was 26 waist 36 hips & around 107 lb. I bought this pair in 2019 & wore it for about 3 years before it became very faded. It is very stretchy, it's flattering except the pockets are kind of narrow & longish? makes your butt look like it is slimmer but longer lool. It's not durable, don't expect to last more than a couple of years, depending on how much you wear & wash it. Back then when I wore this pair the most, I was around 115 - 125 lb & it became my go to pair for everything. I would say, I only recommend this pair for people over 115 pounds, otherwise it would be too lose so if you wear xs, the small will probably be too loose. I hope it helps.

Here's the scoop on these jeggings I'm 5' and 127 lbs, waist 33" and hips 38", and I ordered both medium and small. The small was way too small, but the medium fits very well. The waist of the jeggings comes up to my belly button, with no muffin top. Even when I lose a few pounds, they'll still be nice and snug. I just got them, so I don't know how they'll wash, but I'm not worried about it, even if they end up shrinking a little. I'm planning to dry them on medium heat, because I don't want to weaken the spandex. Unless they end up being flimsy, or have other unexpected issues, I recommend these jeggings. To answer some questions that other shoppers asked: - The blue jeggings look like jeans and have the orange stitching in the seams to look like regular jeans. The black jeggings have black stitching. - They both have rear pockets, with false front pockets. - Yes, they are long on me by several inches but, so what? It won't matter if I'm wearing boots, and I'll just tuck the bottom under with other shoes. - Both colors have decent fabric thickness (not real thick, but good enough), and it feels soft and comfortable. They are not see-through (don't know about the white jeggings). - I tried them on with different types of underwear, and I think that if you wear properly fitted panties (not too tight), there shouldn't be a panty line.

Perfect affordable pregnancy pants! These are seriously the best "pants" ever. I usually only wear dresses and leggings (blue jeans are SO uncomfortable), but I wanted something to wear tshirts and sweaters with. I have these in a medium prepregnancy and they fit perfectly (for reference I was between 135-140 pounds, 5'5", and a size 6). I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

NOW THIS... Is a home run buy!!! LOVE LOVE LOVE!!!! THESE ARE FANTASTIC!!!! Totally different from the"classic" pair I bought and gave away ,, in the light denim. There is a HUGE difference in the CLASSIC and the this one that just says " no nonsense woman's legging" . Beautiful stretch denim material. Light and nice texture... They sit a tad below my belly button ( I am short waisted). FIT IS PERFECT...Hug you in a all the right places. No uncomfortable wad of fabric In the crotch. Ladies you know what I mean! No rubbing. I am 5' 4 106 lbs. , I got the smallest. I was SO HAPPY right out of the box. :):) The length is perfect, and if you want to roll the bottom up a bit with a casual shoe it looks like real jeans, except you won't want to take them off the first chance you get. LOVE THEM> The waist band is the perfect size too... doesn't dig in . Very smooth silhouette. These can be casual or dressed up . They have a deep and generous back pocket. My IPhone 7s fits nicely without it popping out. Compared to the 'classic" ones...these have no brass button ( which i prefer) and I still can't believe the difference in the fit. I LOVE THEM>>>>going to get more as they have a great selection of colors and patterns! I also bought a pair of the capri's in pink and they too are awesome. Same great fit and no button.Nobody would know these are 'jeggings"... they look like jeans , and I am sick of spending big bucks on uncomfortable jeans!SO CUTE!!! *addendum I wore this today in the car , 90 degree heat , runner no around doing errands etc. I am happy to NO SAGGING OR BAGGING ! awesome!☺️

Get the sizing right! I ordered a large and should have ordered a medium. I am returning these but for nothing that is wrong with the legging itself, just a misjudgment in sizing on my part. I am 5'5 154lbs and usually wear 30/31 or size 10 pants. I have belly and hips but not really any booty or leg meat SO! that being said, I can't speak for other woman in the same height/weight class as I, but for me, these fit loosely with a bit of sag that just didn't look appealing. I think getting the size right makes all the difference in this particular brand of legging, so hopefully this short description and pics will help. No other issues - nice material, very comfortable, the waist didn't pinch the skin, seemed well constructed and I may just order these again except in the right size. ;-)

Great Quality and Looks Like Real Jeans I have 41" hips and a 37" waist. Ordered the Large in black. The fit well. A little baggy around the front fly area and the knee and lower leg, so I might have even been able to go MD but didn't want to risk that "too stretched out" look - where you can see the glimmer of spandex - not a good look. These are very very long. As you know, all women are supposed to have the same length legs, so that's why we don't have different length pants, but I unfortunately have very short legs. I'll have to have about a good 6" chopped off of these, but that's nothing new. These are nice quality, thick, REAL BACK POCKETS!, nice stitching that looks like real jeans (deep yellow thread). 100x better than ones I had purchased at Walmart (Faded Glory brand maybe?) where they almost always have that stretched out look where you can totally tell they're not jeans.The black is borderline black - could almost pass for just a really dark wash denim, which is fine by me.Absolutely worth the price!

Happy! I'm happy with this purchase. These are medium weight and don't feel thin or flimsy. Color is classy dark denim. Comfortable! I'm very happy with the value for the price. I am buying these because I need more stretchy clothing for my post partum body for the summer. I think the only thing I might not like is that the waist is rolling down, and then feels a little tight, but I think that's mostly because of my baby belly. I"m 5'-10", 190 lbs, and measure 44" around my butt, and the Large fits perfectly. They are also nice and long! I rolled them up for more of a capri look, but they roll down very long. With no time to shop, I'm turned to amazon for my summer wardrobe, and I'm excited to wear these with some of the cute tops I've purchased on amazon!

Called my sister immediately! As soon as I got these, I tried them on and as soon as I tried them on I called my sister to rave about how great these are! I got a medium. I got 1 black 1 dark denim (that one has not arrived yet, black is in picture). For reference, I am 5'4", 140lbs (and have a bubble butt). They come up to just below my belly button. These are so comfortable. They feel and look just like jeans on the outside but ohh baby they are leggings on the inside. Perfect amount of stretch without being see through. Hugs your curves in all the right places. I can sit, stand, squat, all with ease and comfort! I have longer legs and they just start to bunch near the ankle, but just like jeans do, so if you are shorter, they may need to be folded over. I would absolutely recommend these to anyone who loves jeans looks, but leggings comfort!

They're okay, not the best, but okay These are not as I expected. I have a pair from another retailer that look like actual jeans, but feel like leggings (and of course they don't sell them anymore), and I was hoping these would be the same. These are clearly leggings, they have a white cloth cast underneath. The pair I received had a pinhole kind of... run? You can see that in the picture. But they are very comfortable! I won't ever wear these out for an event or day out, to work or anything like that, but I will wear them around the house or for a quick run to a store or something, and look somewhat put together. And so for that I'll probably order another pair. They're also very long on me. I'm about 5'6 and they would go all the way to my toes if I didn't pull them up. I'm a 12/14/16 (depending on the pants) and ordered a an XL. They're slightly loose, but I would be afraid to do a L and risk them being too tight.

They're ok. I thought I ordered a large but evidently I ordered a medium. It fits though! I'm 5'5 and about 180lbs. I typically wear a size large at like Walmart or Target and a size 10 in stretchy jeans. I'm at that weird point where a 12 is too big and a 10 is kind of snug. I will say I do like these but but honestly $18 is too much. I will keep these because I am satisfied enough. They just feel like cheap $8 leggings from Walmart. They are flattering though and they do have pockets in the back. It is a little misleading to just say they have pockets though. I wish they had a wider waistband.

Really nice but not a great fit for postpartum These are surprisingly thick for a legging. I wouldn’t be concerned at all about them being transparent. Also I feel like they would be pretty sturdy and warm (once again considering that they are leggings). However I used the size chart and other reviews to determine that I needed a medium. Clearly they are too small for me. I’m 9 weeks postpartum. So a lot of my weight is in my belly. This isn’t high enough or low enough to accommodate my belly. It hits at a terrible spot, I don’t really feel like these are a high rise. Maybe a midrise. They are also extremely tight in the belly and crotch area. I was going to return them but I decided to keep them in hopes they fit when I’m thinner. Currently I am 145 pounds. I’m 5 foot 4 inches. I would say a majority of my excess weight is in my belly. So maybe a large would have been better. I’m really disappointed that they don’t fit now.

I’m not a fan of the muffin top myself and the medium is tighter on my middle than flattering.

Black is not black but still nice pants If you liked the Walmart brand of jeggings by Faded Glory that got discontinued, this is the replacement you are looking for. Pros: Very soft and comfortable, good amount of stretch, not see through at all, real back pockets, reasonably priced. Con: The Black is not a true black, it is a very dark denim. This is a problem for me because I am required to wear all black at my job. I am including a photo with the “black” pants against an actually black t-shirt. Fit is as expected, as a reference I am 5’6’’, 150lbs and a medium fits great. Would order again, just not in black!

Great look and fit I'm 5'8" around 165lbs Was looking for a good comfy pair of jeggings as I've gained around 15lbs. I didn't want to spend too much because I can't wear these to work, so only once a week would I want to wear these casually on my days off. Love the color. The rest of the reviews are correct, they run long, even for me, so I cuffed them at the ends which is fine. They have the appearance of a real jean, strechy, high-waisted and functioning back pockets. Definitely going to buy these in another color. The waist band doesn't roll down like some jeggings and leggings. Fits as expected. I'm a 12 in pants depending on the waist height, so I chose large and they fit great. Hope this helps!

Incorrect sizing chart Sizing is difficult to fully comment on because ... I ordered these based on the sizing chart, XXL indicated as 18 and 20, displayed on Amazon’s product page (image attached). I received an XXL, tried them on, and wondered why the fit was off. The sizing chart that the jeans/leggings come packaged with shows an XXL as 20 and 22. Leggings seem well made, soft jean material, but gathers at the knees. Length is great, I’m 5’ 7”. Waist band falls naturally about an inch below my belly button. These feel like they will slide down if I bend over, maybe my butt’s too big. LOL! If I ordered an XXXL (not available), would fit too large. As a woman, I know that just because these were too snug fitting for me, they may not be for you. Completely obsessed with these pants!!!! First the specifics… I am 5’8.5”, 145 lbs, 24” waist, 35” hips, and long legged! I’m 45.5 years old and I love to deviate from my typical all black aesthic! I ordered a small. Definitely needed an XSmall if they came in XSMall. I wore a 2.5” heel with these. Quality: too good to believe with the price! They are thick satin, not that thin mess. They are heavy, thus why they seem to sag. They are incredibly comfortable. They are sexy! The color is everything! I often worry when wearing satin, that they will stain easy or attract spots quick from lotion, or whatever…nope not these. The stitching is quality and appears to be durable. The fabric is so soft to touch and gives designer. I love the pockets as well. I love my pants long, and these are definitely long. If you are shorter or do not like your pants extra long, have a seamstress hem the pants. Trust me they are worth paying for alterations. I ordered these for a quick date night to a beautifully designed lounge. I ordered the hot pink trench coat last year from Amazon as it was featured in “The Drop”, and I have been waiting on the perfect pants to wear with the gorgeous trench. When I saw these pants, I knew I wanted them paired with my coat. What I would do different or what I intend to do with the pants….have alterations done to fit my waist perfectly, hem them up about 2 inches, alter the crotch area, for a more tailored look. Pair them with my sneakers, and either my Tupac graphic t’s or my 80’s Rock band tshirts (for this quick look, I wore a Leon Bridges tour t-shirt, and tied in the back to show my waist) Other pros-These pants shipped quickly. I used my steamer and they were unwrinkled within 20 minutes, and guess what??? They stayed wrinkle free all night even with a lot of sitting. That’s it, that’s the review…I want every color.

Great pants & VERY comfy but run SMALL! I really like these pants! Ordered a Large to begin with, but DANG! BEWARE! They run SMALL! For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & apx. 140lbs with 30" waist & 40" hips & the large was SNUG in the waist! For better comfort I went with the XL & I'm happier! LOVE the length! That was one of the BIGGEST reasons I went with these! The reviews said they WERE longer, and they ARE! LOVE that I can wear heels! YEAH! Only issue I have is the material! It's more a thinner cheap polyester so I took off a star for that! Would have expected, for the price, for them to have been a higher grade material! The pics made me think they were too! But, all and all, I like them & will keep them! LOVE the pockets! Just be aware that if you want that look they show in the pictures, I'd go up TWO sizes! You can tell from the pics, even with XL they are a wee on the tighter side showing ALL! So, I think I'll be adding some shape wear!! But, for the price and the LENGTH I recommend them!! Hope all this help!! Thanks much! ENJOY!

To get the look in the photo go 2 sizes above your Amazon suggested size The fabric and color are great. I am a suggested Amazon M and I’m exchanging for an XL to wear with drapes camisoles and jardigans for work, travel, or anything where I need to look out together without anything binding, clingy, or that I would have to peel off of sweaty skin. Great lounge, athletic or yoga pants Awesome pants, good quality material, breathable, suitable for all year wear. I wore them in a very cold, windy environment and was feeling great, warm but not hot, very comfortable. Material is off medium thickness, not see through or clingy, doesn’t accentuate any skin flows, stretchy, feels pleasant against bare skin. Great design-definitely not leggings style more like yoga or lounge pants, slightly baggy through the thighs and thinning out down the ankle with a cuff at the bottom. Looks exactly as presented in pictures posted in this listing even the color was captured better by their photographers than by my phone’s camera. In regards to the fit-for some reason Amazon recommended me to get XL, but its 12/14 and my regular size is 8-10 so I choose to go with Large and it fits me perfectly. Waist has a wide stretchy band than can be easily adjusted according to your personal preference whether you like it high waisted or wear a bit lower. Length is perfect for me as well-not too short or to long, just right and I’m an average height woman-5’5/5’6. All in all like these pants, would probably get another pair in different pattern and recommend it to anyone looking for casual, comfortable pair of leisure wear 👍🏻

Flattering on my mom bod I'm 8 weeks postpartum and none of my prepregnancy pants are comfortable or flattering anymore. I have some loose skin on my belly and a little pooch. I was looking for something form fitting but not skin tight, and these are it! I ordered this style of pants from several different sellers and these by far are the best! 1. They have a seamless waistband in the front, which makes them both comfortable and more flattering, as they don't dig into the belly. 2. They are made of a silky, buttery fabric that seams to smooth over any dimples and bumps. 3. Perfect length. I'm 5'6 and they hit right at the bottom of the ankle. 4. They aren't awkwardly tight in some areas and then loose in others, like some pants I tried on. 5. They hug the butt just right. My husband even said they made my butt look good! The only downside I noticed: inconsistent sizing. I ordered a small and a medium in the exact same pants. I went with the small. The medium was strangley much shorter than the small and looked more like long capris. For reference, I'm 5'6 and 128 lb. I got the charcoal color.

Great Lulu dupe, comfortable I bought this jogger because I was looking for an alternative to the Lulu Align jogger. This is the best dupe that I have found. I bought the Vintage Violet Purple and it is a gorgeous dark, dark purple bordering on purple-burgundy-black. I have already washed them once in cold water, and dried in the dryer on low, and they came out great. The only downside is that there were many loose threads on the inside (see photo) but I was able to stitch those down myself. For the low price, I am willing to do that. For reference, I am 5’5”, about 147lbs, and I bought a medium for a more fitted fit. I likely will be back to buy more colors.UPDATE: I bought 2 more colors, charcoal and light grey. I am including a photo for color comparison.

Light color is not forgiving I want to like these, but quite honestly I’m a bit nervous to wear these out to the gym. I’m 5’6, 28 waist and 34 hips. Size 6 pants small leggings. I ordered my normal size small. I feel like they fit a bit big through the waist and hips. I know they’re joggers, but I feel like compared to how the legs fit the hips should be less baggy. The material is like yoga or athletic leggings, comfy and buttery to the touch. They fit alright, but I think it’s the color that’s throwing me off. I love the color itself, but because it’s so light it’s not very forgiving and shows my lumps and bumps on the backside. And that’s in my dim bedroom light. Maybe I’ll try them jogging outside or on an upper body gym day if Ifs not crowded... but I think I should have ordered a darker color because of my backside imperfections.
