Decent pants I got these mainly for the pockets for when running errands and traveling. Pockets come in handy on these pants for stuffing things in them on the spot as opposed to having to opening and closing purses/backpacks repeatedly. I personally appreciate the fact that the thigh pockets have flaps that hook /adhere to the pants and not just flaps without a way to secure the pockets. This way things don't fall out of them as easily. The zipper pockets everywhere else are great, too. You have options whether you want to secure your items in there or not. I'm not a fan of cargo pants that just have pockets but no way to secure them. The fabric is thin but opaque. It's smooth and comfortable enough. When walking, the pants make a bit of sound reminding me of parachute pants. I'm not sure how I feel about the sound they make. Fortunately, it's not too loud. I don't love the pants but I don't hate them either. For reference, I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 35 hips, 119 lbs, and got size small. They fit comfortably.

Size small worked for me I'm age 62, a little over 5'6", weigh about 132 lbs. In general I need pants that are "roomy" in the butt. I first ordered a medium, but they were too loose in the butt and thighs. The size small works well. I didn't want skin tight so I'm happy with the fit. I plan to use these for travel. Made for TALL people! These pants promise a blend of professionalism and comfort that seems too good to be true - but for the most part, they deliver! The versatility is their main appeal. They bridge the gap between workwear and workout gear, perfect for my yoga-instructor daughter who also holds a day job at a marketing agency. The idea of a pair of pants that can effortlessly transition from a business setting to a yoga class is incredibly appealing. One of the first things that struck me about these pants is the quality of the material. It's sturdy and durable, suggesting longevity. You can tell they've been made with care and attention to detail, which elevates them from your standard pair of dress pants. However, the length of these pants is where we encountered an issue. My daughter, being a petite 5'4", found them to be excessively long. I ordered a medium size to match her usual 9/10 size, and while they fit perfectly around the waist and hips, the length was a significant setback. It's important to note that if you or the person you're purchasing for is on the shorter side, these pants will likely require hemming. Despite this hurdle, once these pants are altered to fit correctly, I have no doubt they'll become a staple in her wardrobe. The combination of comfort and style is not easily found, and these seem to have nailed it.

So comfortable and SOFT! Did I buy these because I heard they were a Lulu dupe? Absolutely. Am I disappointed? NOPE. Full disclosure, I don’t have enough disposable income to even know what the Lulu item even is or feels like, BUT I do know what cozy feels like—and this IS IT. What I love: 1. The weight of the material—it’s lightweight, but it feels like it would still keep you warm on a chilly spring morning 2. The pockets! They’re real sized pockets that will fit your keys and/or phone! 3. The fit: I’m 5’5” so I went with the longer inseam and I’m glad that I did! My height comes from long legs (as opposed to a long torso), so be sure to choose an inseam that will work best for you! I feel like you could style this with athleisure tops or even a nice sweater and it could still work. I’ll be wearing mine while coaching!

My favorites! These ODODOS leggings are my favorites for workouts and pickleball. I have five of them! The fabric is silky smooth and soft; they slide onto your legs and are extremely comfortable to wear. They are lightweight and stretchy, but not at all see through. NOTE: The patterned fabric is different from the solid fabric. I prefer the printed one, which is a polyester 76%/spandex 24% blend. (The solid fabric is not as silky.) The patterned fabric also hides imperfections like cellulite! The 19” inseam is great for summer and the 23” is perfect for any time. In the photograph I am wearing a 25” inseam, but I am quite tall. (5’8”). I machine wash mine and hang to dry. These are the only leggings I’m buying from now on!

Yes! (Fine Art). Have you ever bought clothing and it felt like it was custom made to fit your body??? These are like that for me. I’m 5’9 200lb and working on my weight loss journey. My hips are smaller than my waist/stomach area. Now usually that means my leggings will roll in the stomach area and fold to my hips. Not these beauties. They also go down to my ankles and for a tall gal, that’s tricky. I feel like taller ladies would be ok with these too. They are a thicker material but buttery soft. I’ve washed them multiple times and hold up well. I’m so impressed, I’m buying more. These are my go to leggings. I wear either a light yellow, different blues, orange, red, darker green, white, or black sports bra with them. Super cute workout outfit. Update: I’m down 33 lbs (so a little baggy in the crotch area. I just pull them up a little higher) but they still fit great. No pilling. I just used them on a 3 mile run in the treadmill and they didn’t roll down. My favorite leggings to date. I got the wine art print also.. also so comfy. I’m down to 166 and a large fits great. I probably could have gotten away with a medium but oh well. They look amazing with the Running Girl sports bra in Wined Red. A forest green, black, white sports bra would be so cute too.

Love all of my ODODOS yoga pants! I own a few pair of ODODOS yoga pants, both in capri and full length, and I love them all. These, however, are my favorite and I have them in both capri and full length. What I love about this pair in particular is the pattern. I like to have fun, patterned yoga pants, but sometimes I have a difficult time finding matching tops. Not so with these pants - they match with light blue, turquoise, bright orange, white or black and the colors are true (for example, not a weird shade of blue that only seems to exist on these pants). Also, the pattern is vibrant, bright, clearly defined AND the pattern doesn't lighten due to the stretch of the fabric in certain areas. You know how with some yoga pants the pattern is brightly colored on your calves but then lighter in the butt and hip because of stretching? That will not happen with these pants. Which brings me to why I love ODODOS yoga pants overall. They are a heavier, high-quality fabric that stretches with you without looking or feeling thin, and I find the high waist flattering. Another reason I love their full length is that I am 5'8" with about a 31-inch inseam and their full length actually goes to my ankles - fantastic and rare! I also love the pockets - a must for me when it comes to exercise. The pockets are deep and fit my phone or smaller water bottle comfortably. These fit well, wash well and LAST. While I just bought these, I have other ODODOS yoga pants that I've been wearing for 3-4 years. I feel these fit true to size. For reference, I typically wear a size 10 or large pants, again, I'm 5'8" and I weigh 157 pounds.

Good leggings for strength training, but not for cardio I'm currently building my own fitness blog and trying on products and clothing that will do well with photo shoots and videos, as well as 3 to 4 days of cardio training (spinning), and 4 to 5 days of strength training a week. This means that they leggings need to be resilient and put up with all the constant wear and tear. I have several leggins from this company and it has become one of my favourite brands. I'm tall, they cover me pretty well for my height, and the flexibility and durability are pretty good. However, unlike the other style of leggings this brand offers, the fabric has less breathability and is not convenient for spinning classes where you sweat a lot. I bought a small and they do fit a tad large on my hips, enough that I get that bulky look when I sit down but it might be because the fabric is kinda thick due to the type of fabric you need to use for prints. Here are my measurements for reference: Height: 5'9'' Weight: 134lbs Waist: 26in Hips: 8in Thighs: 21in Calfs: 15in Body Fat: 16%

Great fit, but slips at the waist. Overall, these are great! When I put them on for the first time, they fit in all the right places. The problem was when I started to move. The waist band slides down very easily. I think I can resolve this by stitching a ribbon or something into the waist band. I’ve done that with similar pants in the past. The way the material is folded at the waist, it shouldn’t be that hard. In the meantime, I can wear them as loung pants or tuck my shirt into them to (hopefully) create some traction and prevent them from slipping. I don’t think I could’ve fit comfortably into the next size down. I am 5’ 6” and wear size 20, and I purchased the 2X. Also, love the pattern! Colors are true to what you see on Amazon. Pockets are spacious. My iPhone 8 fit into the pocket with plenty of room to spare.

These are “Meh” if you have a booty I gave these leggings a 3star rating because “High waist” to me means it covers my belly button. These leggings sat just below the belly button and seemingly have the same height in the back...where my big booty is....meaning, not enough coverage when I decide to sit down, much less squat. I ordered them in a Medium. These leggings might fit the hips and butt perfectly, if you don’t have much of either, but my waist is 29”, hips are 43” (190 lbs). I considered going up to a Large, but the waist would no longer fit snug and would gape in the back. On a positive note, I am 5’10 and these actually hit at my ankles, which is a rare find in leggings. Also, the red is cherry red, vivid, pretty and there are no see-through issues with these leggings. I’ll wear them for light active days (walking) , but probably wouldn’t order another pair.

Inconsistent Sizing? Otherwise, perfect! I originally ordered these pants in Navy, 3X to be on the safe side as I have large thighs and a butt which makes finding cute wide leg pants tricky. I love them! I do wish the color was more of a true darker navy, but they work for what I was wanting. The 3X was on the large size, so going off other reviews and the size chart, I figured a 2X would be the perfect fit for the next pair I wanted to order. Unfortunately the 2X is more like 2 sizes smaller and oddly pulled to one side in the rear almost as if an XL leg panel was sewn to a 2X leg panel. These are a little long, but I wear them with a wedge or boot with a 3" heel and I'm good. I do love these pants, the material is great and flowy, not see through, the waist is cute, the pockets are perfect and the wide leg is greatly appreciated! For reference I'm 5'2" (typically do a 30" inseam) 230lbs. 37.5" waist, 49.75" hips, 52" butt circumference, and 32.5"/thigh.

Pros and cons / honest review For starters, I am 5’8 and 242lbs. First glance when I took them out of the package, they looked like they could run small as well as the length . They looked like they would not fit my legs, ( I have long legs) but to my surprise, they were very stretchy, length wise was perfection for me. I will say they do run small, I got all XLs. The green and maroon pair were very tight especially in the stomach area. The black pair I couldn’t even get over my calves, but for the green and maroon pair I could definitely get them on. If they had a 2XL, I could see that they would fit better. They are very soft, but also have a thick material which is nice. The colors are true to what you see on the photos. The scrunch in the booty is definitely there but I will say for me personally the scrunch was a little uncomfortable because of how tight the scrunch was. They are SQUAT PROOF, but I will say in the maroon pair especially, it’s not that noticeable on the green pair, right underneath the butt, right where the legs start there is a small area where it is very shear once you do a squat.( look at the other photo, where the blue circle is, that’s where it was shear) I personally don’t like that and don’t see the point of it being like that, but that’s me lol. I am definitely returning these. Hopefully this helps anyone.

BEST MATERIAL EVER Zero see through. The material is stretchy but super strong and it feels like a soft cloud against my skin. I'm not often wow'd but the combination of soft/strong/stretchy on these leggings that is AMAZING. I recently gained 32 lbs in a short period of time makes me a newcomer to "tummy control" but I'm definitely a fan of the construction of the entire leggings. If i could change anything it would only be that the front had the compression band come lower over the "fupa" area. The booty synch requires the right size. I usually buy a large and so i stuck with large. I'm 5'2" hourglass shape and 170lbs. I hope this helps!!

Pleasantly surprised! I purchased these in the color B-BLACK in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say they run a tad small if you have bigger hips/butt. I sized up because I was concerned about them being too tight, and I'm glad I did! I could do a large but I really like the looseness of the XL, as it's comfy but still flattering, and with a bodysuit, the fit is perfect in the waist (this one is from Pumiey, btw; I have a review of it as well). They are loose in the tummy area without being too baggy. I'm glad the pleats are super subtle. This style does not have the elastic back, as I don't love that, but I do wish they had some little fake slit pockets in the back, like other slacks do. I'm a little on the shorter side and had some extra length at the bottom, even though my legs are longer in proportion to my body, so I could wear a pair of heels and still be fine, but I don't often wear anything but flats, so I'll probably end up getting them hemmed by an inch or two. The only small complaint I can make is that I wish the button was darker, as I feel the tan one cheapens the look a bit. The material is a bit hard to describe, but I'd say it's pretty standard Amazon loose slack material, if you've tried others. It's thin and a little slick, but not very stretchy, if that makes sense. They feel kind of cool on the inside and they move nicely. I couldn't tell you how well these will hold up in the long run, but I'll try to update after I've worn and washed a handful of times. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these, but maybe size up if you have bigger hips/butt. I've been looking for some cheap slacks to try out because I haven't tried to dress nicely for the office in so long, not since I gained weight years ago, and these are a nice intro back into it. I actually purchased this exact style in B-BEIGE in the same XL size, so I'll post pics of those. I may grab the C style next, since they have tonal buttons and slit pockets in the back, even if they have elastic in the waistband too.

The length on my black ones small verses medium exactly the same the length on the colored pair... well unfortunately my medium gray pair ended up being almost an inch shorter then the others. Both are going to give you nice compression are very comfortable moves with you and are thick. For reference I am at time of pics 127 pounds 5' 6" and work out every day so have in my opinion a good amount of muscle. In the pic i am wearing the medium gray pair. Hope you enjoy these pants as much as I do and the full body suit is absolutely amazing too!

Sizing is questionable but I think I found a solution The quality is nice, very thick and warm. I’m 5’4 and the pants come past my ankles, unlike a few reviews I read who are around my height or shorter. I will say, I could’ve gotten a medium instead of a small, I’m 127. The small is a great size for somone who doesn’t have thighs, a butt or muscles (and that’s ok) but if you’re the toned/have more muscle, thighs and a butt kind of 5’4, get a medium. The sizing. I think if you want a longer pant leg, go up a size. Now, this is just s theory based off of reviews. Also, if you have a high butt or big butt, your pant legs may be higher (or rise up past your ankles). You can size up but you don’t have to. They have enough give but also don’t be delusional. Think about your body and the way clothes fit in you and whatever look you’re trying to convey, give it. Just be comfortable and don’t get too tight clothes, you’ll end up with stomach issues.

For size reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 175 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in a large, which fit snugly (see my pics). Overall, these are comfortable wide-leg pants, and I would order more clothing from this brand.

They look used after first wash Wanted To like these pants as I worked through my pregnancy. The material of the pants lose their shape throughout the day and end up looking really warn and wrinkled. I attribute that to the thin fabric. They have a little stretch to them, but would recommend to size up if you have some junk in the trunk as they will pull awkwardly from the front otherwise. Weird Crutch. Deep pockets I ordered a size small. I’m 5’5 and 135lbs. They fit fine length is ok and they stop just above the ankles. They have a really large/long crutch area, which to me, makes them feel a little awkward. Deep pockets, maybe that why crutch is so big?? What do I know? When I first tried them on, I had to double check the listing because I thought for sure I had ordered men’s joggers by mistake. I could get past all that, it’s an awkward fit but whatever I’m not going anywhere anyway. Three stars because they pill supppeeerrrr bad. If you buy these be prepared to shave them constantly. I’m honestly not sure that they’ll hold up all that well in that area between the thighs. I don’t have much faith that I won’t be seeing massive holes there in a month or two, but we will see, I guess. It got even worse if you can imagine it. DONT BUY WALMART PANTS ARE BETTER QUALITY

Great for petites, probably. I'm 5'7", not long legged at all, and got these as a gift. They are so short! Unwashed they have a 24" inseam. I will probably wear them to knock around the house because they're soft and stretchy and I don't live in a cold climate, but buyer beware if you want them to reach your ankles.

Buy a Clothing Shaver, You'll Need It! I'm really disappointed in these sweatpants. Does anyone make good, cotton sweats anymore? Everyone gave these such great reviews, so I bought them...and now I'm filled with regret and constantly picking off balls of fabric. Thank goodness I bought a fabric shaver! I mean, I have worn 2 pair, and BOTH started pilling within hours. And not just in the usual spots...rear, inner thighs, but everywhere! And I can't forget to mention that on the pink pair, the opening to the right side pocket is about 2-3 inches smaller than the left. The fabric will snag dry or calloused skin and it's just not good. Sure, they fit alright (I bought a L and I'm 5'2" and 148 lbs) and they fit like joggers, NOT sweatpants...so snugger through the rear, thighs and legs. I need to stop ordering clothing online because all I am finding are things that are made from fabrics that are cheap and just ICK. How can one polyester garment be silky soft and these pants are rough and stick to everything, snag and pill? Okay, I'm off to shave my sweatpants. Be warned...but if you like picking off little fabric balls, these are definitely for you! Of you're picky about fabrics, spend the extra on better sweatpants!

Sizing is way off I bought these because I have knee replacement surgery coming up and wanted loose, comfortable pants. I went by my measurements on the size chart (47” waist and 55” hips) and ordered the 3X. They are really long and way too big. I’m 5’10” and the crotch hits mid-thigh and the hem puddles around my feet! I can roll the waist four times to make them fit, but it’s a pain because they don’t stay rolled. I’m going to cut the hems off so I won’t trip. I wouldn’t buy these again, which is too bad because they really are very soft, have cute patterns, and are a good price. I can’t recommend.

Love these sweats! Soft and cozy *Honest Review* Originally ordered these in size small black and they didn’t give that baggy look, so I ordered again in medium and they fit exactly how I wanted. Now one thing I always try to comment on is the colors!!! The cyan color is not as pictured! It’s a very dull grayish blue. Now I have the avocado green and they’re pretty close to what’s pictured just more muted. Like a pastel lime green. I like them just not sure if I’ll keep or get different color. They’re definitely worth it. Cotton material so no yucky gross shiny polyester 👎🏽 I’m only 5’3 and these give the perfect amount of baggy. After a few washes they do shrink up a bit so I recommend washing(inside out)and hang drying. My weight fluctuates so currently my waist measures 26in hip 35in weight 108lbs. If you want these to be fitted than get usual size or size down but if you want more of what’s modeled than size up one or two! These are not super thick but they’re soft and comfortable. Last pic is of me wearing the black pair cuz I haven’t tried these on yet but they’re identical to the black pair except the color obviously 🙄 lol but just to give you an idea on how they look on someone with similar measurements. Good luck 👍🏽 Hope I was

Cute wide leg palazzo pants I just received these pants and my sister tried them on. They are so cute! She is 5’9 and they are long enough to fit her. I am shorter and can wear them as well because I actually like my pants to drag the floor a little. I love the flowy fabric and the dark grey shade which will match so many different things. Soft & Stretchy I ordered the black and blue yoga pants. I just got them in the mail today! They are soft and stretchy. I am 140 lbs and I ordered a size Large. Very soft and comfortable; wish fit were a bit different I've been looking for casual pants to wear around the house. I would have preferred something that did NOT have elastic bands the bottom of the legs, but these looked good otherwise, so I decided to try them. My usual pants size is medium (5'4.5", 150 lbs), but I ordered these in large because I didn't want them to be tight. On the one hand, it's a good thing that I got the large - you can see in the photos that the waist fits snuggly without adjust the loop closure at all. On the other, these pans are pretty baggy on me, which is surprising because I have large thighs - despite this, they look MUCH baggier on me than on the model! The material of these pants is very soft and comfortable. The interior is NOT fleece-lined like regular sweatpants. There is a black lining that looks like it would be heavy, but even though the pants seem pretty substantial, they also feel quite lightweight on. I like the pockets and the front seams, and I like that the closure is elastic with a sliding toggle rather than ties. There is a deep pocket at the back that has a small zipper closure - I get that this is supposed to be for a phone, but since I plan to mainly wear these at home, I could have done without the extra bulk. Overall, these are good quality comfortable lounge pants, and I would recommend them.

Not fleece material These pants weren’t quite what I expected. I thought they would be made of the normal sweatpant/fleece material. I didn’t look closely enough at the description to see they were primarily polyester and rayon. Despite the fabric being different than what I expected, they are very comfortable. The pants have two roomy front pockets and a zippered back pocket. There is a thick elastic waist band. I couldn’t tell from the pictures on the product site, but there is a line of stitching that goes down the front legs of the pants. It’s a style choice whether you like this or not. I’m 5’2” and these pants are a little long on me. Honestly, these pants aren't my favorite style, but they’re incredibly comfortable and have become my go to pants for lounging around the house. Washing: the care label suggests washing in cold water, and do not tumble dry.

Thickish material, not summer joggers. Stretchy, comfy, tapered. Nice lines I have these Pinspark tapered, cuffed, sweatpant joggers in the Navy blue, size small and they fit me as I expected. I chose the size small after checking the merchants size and measurements chart. I typically wear a size small, 4-6, weigh 125# and 5’2” tall. These fit me comfortably and loosely. The legs are tapered and the ankles have knit cuffs so not too baggy. The fabric is smooth, stretchy and kind of thick and heavy. I would not call these summer sweat pants. They have a casual, smart look to them and are good for every day wear. The navy navy is deep navy, almost black. I find the shoelace drawstring at the waistband completely unnecessary and will pull that out (with one easy tug) as the waistband is stretchy and my waist is never going to be too small to hold these on. It is just in the way. Nice comfy pants.

Silky Soft and Comfy These yoga pants have a comfortable loose feel and I really like the silky-like fabric. They seem well-made and have a nice cooling effect that’s perfect for summer. They're great for hiking or walking, and as someone with fair skin, I love the UPF 50+ protection. There are a few minor issues though. The fit is perfect in some areas but a little baggier in others than I'd like. For some reason, the pockets don't lay as flat as I'd prefer, but I do appreciate having four usable pockets. I got my usual Medium, which I think worked well for me. For reference, I'm 5'6", 145 lbs with a 28" waist and 38" hips. I like the high waist, although I didn't feel they offered the tummy control the description necessarily suggested, and the waistband is a bit tight when pulling them on, but feels comfortable once they're on. Overall, I'm happy with the comfort and think they look really nice, even if they're a tad baggier in some areas than I'd prefer.

Comfortable, flattering, true to size These jeans are super cute and great quality for the price. I did not want to spend $200+ on such a trendy style that I wasn’t sure would work for me but glad I took a chance on these Free People dupes, they did not disappoint. I am 5’9” and 155 lbs, age 47. The 10s fit perfectly, however I am extra thick around the middle and if you are more of an hourglass type the 8s might be a better fit. The 8s were great but a little too tight in the waist for me. I will echo some other reviews that these are definitely high rise, not mid. Totally fine by me but be aware if you care. Lastly, there was a huge difference in length between sizes so maybe consider that when you’re selecting a size. The 10s hit my ankle, the 8s were more like a cropped length.

Wonky sizing. very unimpressed I carefully used the size chart before ordering, which said that for a 29.9" waist to get a size 12. My daughter has a 30" waist, so I got that size for her (while crossing my fingers, because she usually wears a size 8). Well, I wish I had just gotten her regular size and ignored the size chart, because these don't come close to fitting her. They're way too big at the waist (and as for the length, forget about it). My waist is currently a 32", and these jeans more or less fit at the waist (although they are baggy and frumpy throughout), and even I maybe should have a size 10. Bottom line: IGNORE THE SIZE CHART. Either order your regular size or maybe one size up. And that said, the length is ridiculous. I'm 5'4", which is the exact average height of a woman in the US. and these are too long for me to even wear. Possibly if I wore them with stilettos they wouldn't drag on the ground, but guess what, I'm not wearing them with stilettos. So the entire fit of these jeans is off, the size chart in inaccurate, and the jeans don't look good on. These make me look chunky and are very unflattering. Not a fan at all.

Okay Capri Pajama Bottoms These pajama bottoms are capri, which is okay for me since I am short, 5'1 , I sized up to medium and they fit nicely. I chose the flamingo print which is cute. I like that they are lightweight. The downside is the material which they claim is 60% cotton and 40% polyester. I suspect these are actually 100% polyester due to the feel, rough and they did not shrink even a little, for 60% cotton they should have shrunk a little and the fabric should have soften up with washing. Although they are thin, polyester is too warm and rough for sleepwear.

Runs big I am usually a size 27 waist, which is a US size 4. According to the chart that would be a medium. I was afraid if I ordered a small that the waist would be too tight and uncomfortable. However, I normally wear a size small in adult women’s clothing. If you follow the chart, the fit may be a little big, so I would trust your usual size. I fit in the medium, but they definitely are on the bigger side. These look like they wrinkle…I know I am not somebody who will iron them, so I guess I’m wearing them wrinkled. I ordered the top, but it hasn’t come in yet, but it is nice that you can make a set for a very reasonable cost. The fabric is lightweight and seems to be a polyester blend. They’re not super stretchy. There is a drawstring and elastic waist. There are two back pockets, and two leg pockets, which I appreciate. I prefer to keep my phone in my right leg pocket where it doesn’t get in the way or fall out if I have to bend down and it’s secured with a zipper, so it doesn’t fall out.

Vivid, comfortable & stretchy These pants have such a vivid color. The green is very solid green. I feel it’s almost not true to the photos in the description but pretty close. They are stretchier than I thought they’d be and fit well. I am 5’ 5” and about 186 lbs. I hover between a 12-14 depending on the brand and the day. I went with a 14, and they’re spot on. I definitely would not have gotten away with a 12. Sometimes 14s tend to be long on me, but these are perfect bootcut length for my proportions. The rise is like a high mid. I carry my weight in my legs and belly, and while they don’t have tummy control I still feel they are pretty flattering. They are also on the thinner side which avoids feeling bulky like corduroy tends to, but it’s not the choice if you’re looking for something super warm. I would buy these in more colors and likely will, soon.

VERY flattering and soft/comfortable It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. I did not notice any lint or fading after I washed them.I would say these pants are quality made, cute, cute, cute, very soft and comfortable to wear and will flatter just about any body type with it's cut. You do have to button them up. I'm glad the style is coming back so all shapes can look good, which to me means flattering to your body type. I would say the price to me is on the high side. I've always been thrifty and look for bargains or discounts, especially on a Sr fixed income. If you're willing to pay this for 1 pair of pants, go for it! They will not disappoint.

Comfortable, flattering, easy care I really like these pants. I put them on and drove for 2.5 hours for a graduation. Fought crowds. I'm 5'3" and was wearing a small heeled shoe.I like that they are machine washable and tumble dry. With this type of fabric though, I generally do let them air dry and then pop them in the dryer to fluff and de-wrinkle.I also like the shade of white these pants are. They’ll do Thank god I just found these pants! I had purchased ABCWOO’s pants back when they were selling them and they stopped making good pants. I found these and they are fantastic!!! They’re dressy without feeling uncomfortable. The 2xl is a little tight on me but I’m working on that. They seem very nicely made. Ordered one pair and just placed an order for 5 more! Update: After only 2-3 washes, the band ripped out of the one pair and another has the seam coming out in the butt. I do want to like them, but they are a bit short if you’re tall. I’m 5’7” Another update: More of the band seems are tearing as well as I’ve had belt loops come off. I really wanted to like these pants cause something like it was hard to find. Also, since I’ve ordered so many, the length DRASTICALLY varies. Some are way too short and others are almost too long. No attention to quality I suppose. How unfortunate.

Poorly sewn. Will be ruined upon first wear. I bought 2 midnight black boot cut pairs only for both of them to rip at the same seam (a few inches from the same belt loop) on the FIRST wear. You don't even know it happens it's like the thread wasn't secured after sewed through and simply putting the pants on opens it right up. PERFECTION in a pant! I simply don't have enough adjectives to describe how absolutely ELATED, ENTHRALLED and DELIGHTED I am to have found these! I don't think I have EVER been as happy with a purchase. Wish I could give them 100 stars!I am 5'4" - probably around 170 "or so" at this time, have fairly thin legs but carry weight around the midsection. I normally wear a size 10 pant (sometimes an 8 if it's a stretch waist- sometimes a 12). I might have done w/a size medium in these but I'm glad I got the large as the medium may have been too tight in the waist.An upcoming December trip that includes a dinner where we're supposed to wear white sent me on a frustrating quest for something appropriate yet comfortable. I couldn't find ANYthing in the right color in any of the local stores. The white is more of a "winter white"/cream, but really all-season. In the photo, I've got black undies on- and you can see that they show through a little- but with the proper light-colored underwear, they'll be fine.These are VERY high-end. Classy, comfortable, easy to wear, and even have actual usable front pockets! THE BEST perfect weight fabric, and no wrinkle which makes them ideal for travel. The elastic waist will accommodate a few pounds up or down and yet doesn't look at all frumpy- and in fact is even sexy! What's not to LOVE? I think they'd look great on anyone, and are even flattering to this 67-year old body! What's more, I FEEL fabulous in them. Seriously, I'm moved almost to the point of tears LOL.I've already ordered 2 more pair in the navy & the black, and I've got another 3 in my cart in size medium, just in case I lose a few lbs and God forbid the manufacturer stops making them.THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU, dear manufacturer! Please consider making these in SHORT length (not petite, just "short"). That's the only way they could possibly be improved upon. And please, please, please, NEVER stop making this pant!XOXOXOI

Stylish trousers for work I'm very happy with these pants. The trouser style is great for the office. The elastic waistband is super comfortable, but the pants still have a tailored and professional look. They look sharp with a belt and a fitted shirt. The material is an interesting waffle knit. I wasn't sure I'd like it, but I do. It has a nice weight, and I'll be able to wear these even in the colder months. The pockets are nice and deep. I'm typically a size 14 pant; I ordered these in Large and they fit great. I'm 5'6" and these hit just below my ankle. I can still wear them with flats without them dragging on the ground. I've gotten lots of compliments on them, and a couple of women at work have asked me where I got them. Great business casual pants that I'll get plenty of wears out of!

Peekaboo... Everyone Can See You The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. In the image attached to this review, the silhouette of my hand is on the right and the rectangle retail tag to the right of the drawstring. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

Silky, comfortable, flattering. Shorter than expected length These silky high waisted palazzo pants are a timeless classic. The fabric is a silky feeling synthetic material that drapes well, and they are very flattering. When taken straight out of the box, they were a little wrinkled but that is to be expected. With a few minutes in the dryer they flattened right out. The waistband falls at the smallest part of my waist -these are very high waisted, and plenty long in the crotch. The pockets are not too shallow, and can fit my phone without slipping out. The waist band is both elastic and drawstring, so they are comfortable, functional and adjustable. I wish they were longer though- I am 5 foot 6 and with completely flat sandals they were about two inches from the floor (and I prefer a floor length pant). I think if you are shorter than me, the pants would hit the floor or perhaps allow a higher heel if you're close to five feet tall. If you're taller than me (for example 5 foot 8 or more) then they would be more of an awesome Culottes length. I ordered a size Medium, which based on the chart fits a size 8 to 10. I am a size 10 and these fit nicely in the waist (meaning the elastic was a perfect comfortable fit and did not require additional cinching with the drawstring). Overall these are a breezy daytime pant, the type that you see on European women with strappy sandals, a form fitting body suit and a great pair of sunglasses.

Silky, comfortable, flattering. Shorter than expected length These silky high waisted palazzo pants are a timeless classic. The fabric is a silky feeling synthetic material that drapes well, and they are very flattering. When taken straight out of the box, they were a little wrinkled but that is to be expected. With a few minutes in the dryer they flattened right out. The waistband falls at the smallest part of my waist -these are very high waisted, and plenty long in the crotch. The pockets are not too shallow, and can fit my phone without slipping out. The waist band is both elastic and drawstring, so they are comfortable, functional and adjustable. I wish they were longer though- I am 5 foot 6 and with completely flat sandals they were about two inches from the floor (and I prefer a floor length pant). The length is good and I usually need a long pant length…they are just right for me but could be too long for anyone under 5’ 5”. I am super excited to have the khaki color because I’d love to have some lounge pants I can wear with brown sweatshirts. The fabric is going to be very warm so I suspect these will be best for the cold months of winter. Overall I like them and will wear them but they aren’t true sweatpants to me.

Great dress pants, style and quality I was hesitant to purchase these pants based on mixed opinions in posted reviews and overall score. Being a Latina with curves and big thighs, it’s sometimes challenging to find the right fit. For context I wear size 10 or 12 depending on the style, material, cut, etc… I bought these in a 12 to ensure they weren’t too fitted! The material is great quality, very soft and flowy, they are roomy in the thighs, fabric not overly thin so we’re not transparent as you can see in the pics (I zoomed in on all so you can really see the details), and the cut fit very pretty. The waist has some elasticity in the back and the zipper is in the side. I’m 5’5 and length was perfect. I can wear with flats, kitten or regular 3”heels without a problem. Finally, classy pants that fit right! I plan on getting them in more colors.

Great date night pant! For reference. 5’6”, #185, wearing XL. Great fit, excellent style, washed fantastic in a garment bag. I received so many compliments on these pants. The satin looks and makes them feel high end.

Seems to run big. Nice lightweight pant. I am 5'7" and weight 155lbs, I have a 31" waist. Based on the measurement chart I ordered an XL, I normally wear between a M and a L. Based on their weight/height chart I probably should have ordered a L. These don't look big on me in the picture but they were, the slightest amount of weight in the pockets pulls them right down because the waistband is very loose on me. I got these for review so I can't trade them for a new size...instead I think I will thread a drawstring through the top so I can cinch them tighter.So, I would recommend going off of their size/weight chart for sizing...not the measurements they list.These are incredibly lightweight pants, I think they will be amazing in the summer but they don't work for the winter at all here in Minnesota. They have nice sized hand pockets and big cargo pockets as well. The cargo pockets do not fasten shut. The ribbed material around the waist is very stretchy and comfy.These are very comfy lightweight pants and seem to be good quality, a drawstring would have made them perfect...as is, they slide down too much when I wear them. They are very thin, not see through, but you definitely see underwear lines through them.For the price point they are very nice. I think they will be amazing in the summer. I just wish I had sized down and gone with the size/height chart for sizing and not the measurement chart. There is no way the XL have a 30" waist as listed...it's more of a 32-33" waist.

Enthusiastic Approval. Triumphant! I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

More legging than jegging I'm 5'2 and 160 lb (which i carry all in front, not in my hips). I bought a large. As you can see, they are a little long on me so they bunch up a bit at the ankles. They are also really more legging than jean - clingy, thin cloth (but not see through for me). My husband said he didnt like the shape of these pants and I was like "Uh, thats the shape of my legs!"But I do love the choices of colors. I love the way this pair coordinates with the tunic I'm wearing and they arent too warm for warmer weather. I do wear this outfit to work, so I may consider getting more to coordinate with other tunics, but I'm not sure.

So comfortable!! I bought these for work, where I am required to wear khaki pants/shorts. The fact that they have functional front and back pockets was a HUGE selling point for me. I am 5'3 and between 125-130lbs. My waist was 30" at my time of purchase so I could not decide between small or medium because according to the size chart, I am at the upper spectrum of small (25 - 32) and at the lower spectrum for medium (28 - 38) and I really do fluctuate between those sizes depending on the brand. I ultimately went with small and they fit nicely with absolutely no squeezing of my body. I am so comfortable at work and because of the texture of the fabric, they look like form fitting pants rather than leggings. As you can see in the photo, the pants are too long for me, so I will stick to the capri style. I've worn them once a week for the past month and although I have followed the directions for washing and drying, the fabric is starting to look like its going to pill so I am not sure how much longer they will look nice. If I order more to wear outside of work, maybe I'll try hanging them to dry and see if that helps.

If you are wondering about the waist, YES: there is a little elastic band at the top. Welp, here I am again. Wrote a similar review on a similar pair of wide leg yoga pants only a couple weeks ago. Sigh! Here’s the verdict- these are overall cute yoga pants with good options on color and inseam length. They could really use a size XS though, the size small was just a touch big for me (jeans size 24/25 waist). My real gripe here is the lack of description of the waistband and the actual waistband. I hate HATE having the little elastic band at the top of the waist in leggings/yoga pants. It is not comfy, not flattering. I want a nice wide seamless band that lays flat. I read every word of this item’s description and could not find a clear answer. I then read all the reviews, fruitless venture. I then posted my question to the Amazon community, fruitless venture. Answers were literally like 4 people said no, doesn’t have the elastic and 2 said yes. Baffled by this, I decided to just give them a shot on a lightning deal sale. They arrived today and are going back. I decided to post this review to help anyone who shares the strong hatred of skinny elastic and I decided to include a photo to help show the Amazon community what it is. (I should note that I bought the khaki color and because this is Amazon I cannot rule out the possibility that some colors may have it and others may not. I will not be purchasing 8 pairs in order to find out).

Not office wear, but great anyway… I bought this size Medium in 2 colors - I am 5’6” and 150 pounds and the fit is perfect. I bought them in hopes to “dress them up” as I’ve seen other reviewers mention, but I definitely won’t be able to wear them in my office setting workplace. The side seams (see pic) look like leggings/athletic wear which unfortunately won’t work for me at the office, but I will definitely get use out of these! They are a high rise (almost too high for me) with a loose fit thru the legs. The flare is gradual and flattering, and they hit right at the top of my foot (almost too short with shoes). They don’t do anything for my fluffy middle section, but I usually wear a longer top to cover that. It washes well - I did not dry it but the tag says dry on low. I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!

Comfortable but the waisted and… These pants are very light and comfortable, perfect for the hot, miserable weather we have been having lately. Be aware that they come with a waistband that could stand the Leaning Tower of Piza back up. This waistband is NO JOKE. It is about 2 inches wide and not sewn into the pants so when you wash the pants, it twists all around with little hope of ever being orderly again. I cut the damn thing out and thus now have even more comfortable pants minus the muffin top that any two inch waistband is bound to cook up. For reference, I am 5’6”, 145lbs (more athletic build than squishy). The pants also have a lot of extra room in the crotch, which is hard to explain because they are women’s but all the same, they are cute, comfortable, affordable and I’d recommend them (with a band-ectomy). Hope that helps :)

Good fit and comfort I am very pleased with my purchase. I am 5'3" (160cm) and 105 lb. (47.7kg). The small size fits very well. The material is soft, but also durable cotton. It has a nice relaxed fit without being overly baggy and not fitted like skinny jeans. The length fits good. The cuffs come all the way to the ankles. I mostly wear my pants on my hips so the crotch tends to sag a bit, but when I pull them up onto my waist, the crotch seam fits as pants should with just enough room. I have a long waistline. I have NOT washed them in hot water and line dried only due to the high cotton count. I have not put heat to them so I don't know how much they will shrink. Some pants, when you bend you knees, they creep up at the ankles, but these have enough play and stretch in them that they stay where they are, even flexing the joints.As for any cons, the pockets are a bit bulky, but I can deal with a little bulk if it means I get pockets! I am using these for work. The zippers are a nice feature, but I can see the small plastic grips may be problematic. I read some reviews where they fell off in the wash. They have been through one cycle already. So far, so good!I included pictures for reference.

Loved them…until I realized they ARRIVED W A HOLE IN THE BUTT OF THE PANTS I tried these pants on as soon as they arrived & instantly fell in love w them so I decided to keep them on & wear them over to my friends house. Our plans included relaxing while we lounged around binge watching a show on Netflix & I had a less than 2 min walk to her house, so these were perfect. Unfortunately, less then 10 minutes after I put these on I arrived at my friends house, bent down slightly to take my shoes off & as soon as I did, my friend piped up & said “there’s a hole in your pants”. I wasn’t even halfway bent over when she said it. The hole is not in an easy spot to cover either. I plan on sewing it up bc I really do love these pants & they’re not only cute & comfortable, but they’re also the perfect weight for the weather this time of year (early fall). W/o the hole these would have definitely been 5 star pants, easy. The fit is absolutely perfect on me although they do run a bit large bc the small fits me amazingly & I’m typically a medium in pants. The measurements in the size chart seem to be accurate though so I absolutely recommend using those to choose your size. I decided to order a small opposed to a medium using the size chart measurements. I’m short waisted so I often have to roll the waistband of pants like this. I was so excited when I rolled this waistband & saw that the string used to tighten the waist was now on the part of the waistband that’s visible once it was rolled. It’s like the waistband was meant to be rolled. The pockets are still easily accessible w/ the waistband rolled as well. Not to mention that they lay flat w the waistband rolled or unrolled unlike so many other pants this style. I often find that the pockets bulge out at each side making you look bigger than you are. The fit is perfect imo…loose w/o being too wide legged & the length wasn’t so long that I’d end up stepping on them either. With sneakers on the length looked just right. The material used is also insanely soft, almost silky, breathable & just super comfortable. I was a bit worried about the feeling of the inside of these pants bc it’s described as a “a comfy looped interior…” & the close up image of the interior showed the looped design opposed to the soft fleece I usually try to get. I decided to take a chance after reading the glowing reviews stating that the interior was super comfortable. I was pleasantly surprised to find that I thought the interior was just as comfortable, if not more so, than I’d hoped based on the reviews I’d read. It has an almost silky like feeling against the skin that actually feels quite nice. In the past I’ve gotten pants & tops w a similar looking fabric that in some cases has caused irritations from rubbing against my skin it really is a shame bc these pants are absolutely amazing & I fell in love with them the moment I put them on, but I can’t in good conscience give them more than 3 stars bc they arrived damaged. W/o the hole in them, these are 5 star pants all day. For reference I’m 5’2, between 135lbs &140lbs w/ my weight fairly evenly distributed & a 29.5” waist. Also, I’m typically a medium in both tops & bottom & the small in these fits me perfectly.

Not to mention that they lay flat w the waistband rolled or unrolled unlike so many other pants this style. I often find that the pockets bulge out at each side making you look bigger than you are. The fit is perfect imo…loose w/o being too wide legged & the length wasn’t so long that I’d end up stepping on them either. With sneakers on the length looked just right. The material used is also insanely soft, almost silky, breathable & just super comfortable. I was a bit worried about the feeling of the inside of these pants bc it’s described as a “a comfy looped interior…” & the close up image of the interior showed the looped design opposed to the soft fleece I usually try to get. I decided to take a chance after reading the glowing reviews stating that the interior was super comfortable. I was pleasantly surprised to find that I thought the interior was just as comfortable, if not more so, than I’d hoped based on the reviews I’d read. It has an almost silky like feeling against the skin that actually feels quite nice. In the past I’ve gotten pants & tops w a similar looking fabric that in some cases has caused irritations from rubbing against my skin it really is a shame bc these pants are absolutely amazing & I fell in love with them the moment I put them on, but I can’t in good conscience give them more than 3 stars bc they arrived damaged. W/o the hole in them, these are 5 star pants all day. For reference I’m 5’2, between 135lbs &140lbs w/ my weight fairly evenly distributed & a 29.5” waist. Also, I’m typically a medium in both tops & bottom & the small in these fits me perfectly.

Loved them…until I realized they ARRIVED W A HOLE IN THE BUTT OF THE PANTS I tried these pants on as soon as they arrived & instantly fell in love w them so I decided to keep them on & wear them over to my friends house. Our plans included relaxing while we lounged around binge watching a show on Netflix & I had a less than 2 min walk to her house, so these were perfect. Unfortunately, less then 10 minutes after I put these on I arrived at my friends house, bent down slightly to take my shoes off & as soon as I did, my friend piped up & said “there’s a hole in your pants”. I wasn’t even halfway bent over when she said it. The hole is not in an easy spot to cover either. I plan on sewing it up bc I really do love these pants & they’re not only cute & comfortable, but they’re also the perfect weight for the weather this time of year (early fall). W/o the hole these would have definitely been 5 star pants, easy. The fit is absolutely perfect on me although they do run a bit large bc the small fits me amazingly & I’m typically a medium in pants. The measurements in the size chart seem to be accurate though so I absolutely recommend using those to choose your size. I decided to order a small opposed to a medium using the size chart measurements. I’m short waisted so I often have to roll the waistband of pants like this. Quality Material and Comfort These sweatpants are certainly well above average in their quality, and have a nice fit. I ordered small and I'm honestly not sure if an XS may be a better fit? I like the fit from the front, but as pictured from the side, these are definitely a baggier loose fit-which isn't bad, but just not sure if I could have gone with XS? For reference, I am only 5'1", a heavy118lbs today, and 28.5 waist. These are only slightly long for me but do work best with at least an inch platform underfoot at my 5'1“ height (or lack thereof). These photos are taken right after their first washing. I through them in on "warm" wash temp and dried them and don't really believe they shrink much, so don't count on that. If you like that looser baggier fit I'd recommend you order yournormal size. My 83 year old mom has also worn these, and while she weighs the same, her waist is smaller and hips slightly bigger (why didn't I get that shape?!) Interestingly, they fit my mom similarly to myself. Stated another way, I think the extra room in the seat/upper thigh area of this cut will accommodate both the larger waist/smaller hip and more shapely figures about the same. The blue color on these was great and they are very comfortable. Very good quality. Thought they looked like a nice cut to them in the advertising and they actually are as pictured with great quality fabric/weight to boot. Recommend!

Nicest stirrup pants I have had in years. I LOVE THESE! I have 3 pairs and can't say enough about them. They are not sheer at all. The fit is fabulous. The stirrup is GREAT. These are comfortable as they could possibly be. They are amazing for tall gals. I am 5'6 1/2. They are affordable and come in a variety of colors. Thank you for making these available. I will wear them for years. They were delivered in 2 days. I love them.

The most amazing scrub pants I have ever worn! I hate scrubs. For 30 years, I would run home to change before I was seen outside of work in them. They never fit right, they’re uncomfortable, the material sucks and is so thin that I’m always cold. I found these and gave one pair a try, without having much hope. Well, let me tell you, they are amazing. They are so comfortable and feel and look so great that I’d wear them outside of work, too! I will never wear anything else. Every time they go on sale, I buy another pair or two because I’m afraid that someday, they’ll disappear and I’ll never be able to find anything like them ever again - they are that great! Some stats - I’m 5’8 and 185 or so, somewhat curvy with a belly and the kind of waist that lends itself to muffin tops. I wear a size L for scrubs and a 12 for jeans. I bought these in both XL - which is a wee bit too long and slightly loose, and a L - which is the perfect size for me and fits like a glove, makes my legs and butt look good, and doesn’t pinch or pull anywhere or give me the dreaded muffin top. LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

Design seems blurry My first thought when i opened these up was "why isn't the image sharper?" My son and husband didn't think anything looked wrong with these, but it bothered me, and reading the reviews, someone else mentioned this as well. It's hard to tell based on the photos in the listing-- i went back to compare to see if i had been mistaken with my expectations or what, but i decided that it's kinda hard to guage from those pictures. I just assumed it would be more fine lines up close. The seems and the way the pattern changes in the middle of my legs is not something i like, not something i think looks good on me. I think it's looks funny, and wondered if i should have ordered a different size, but given that I'm about 165lbs wearing a size L, i don't think i was wearing a pair that was too small for me... they seemed to fit well, but i just don't think the legs look nice on me. Quality of the sewing and materials seems good. Pockets are well built. Pants feel nice, no pilling. I don't love these pants, but that's because of the way they look on me. If i had ordered a solid color, i think i would be very happy with these pants. If you don't see anything wrong with my pictures, then you might really like these pants.

On the fence So, I’ll start off by saying these are probably the most comfortable “scrunch” leggings I’ve purchased from Amazon. I probably have about 10 pairs from all different brands. I have a big issue with these types of leggings because I do have a little Fupa and most seamless leggings really push it down and emphasize it. These leggings are actually “tummy” control. They aren’t all the way to my sports bra like most others and they fit right over my hips/love handles. They are compression but NOT see through. The CONS, the scrunch is very excessive. Not only visually but comfort as well. The scrunch goes the entire way down your butt and it’s very deep I guess you can say. I can also very much feel it when I wear them, which I don’t care for especially since sometimes I don’t wear underwear 😂 but to each their own. They are also very good with not showing a camel toe. I mean they are seamless so naturally it’s kinda there but man, some of these brands just display 😐 I am typically a L/XL and the Large was perfect. I am 5’7 160 lbs. I’m typically an 11/13. True to size.

Amazing Leggings Especially for Thick Girls These leggings are amazing! I'm 5'3 and weight about 170lbs, and I bought an XL. I am an MMA fighter, so I have thick thighs and often can never find pants that fit me properly. Most leggings are too lose in the waist and hips, too tight in the thighs and calves, and waaaaaaay too long in the length and I trip over the ends. These are NOT the case. These fit me as if they were tailored to fit me. They don't fall down, they don't suffocate my legs, and they hit right above my ankle bone. I bought the black pair to try out, and after a few wears, I decided to buy the red, deep purple, and army green colors because I love them so much. The seams are very sturdy and do not feel like they are going to rip when you move. They are made from a thicker fabric (so they aren't see through), but still very breathable. All in all, I would highly recommend these to anyone who wants a good pair of leggings.

Flattering relaxed cut These pants are very nice for curvy girls. I have a 31" waist and 41" hips. The cut combined with the stretch is very flattering in the size 10P for me. I think that because these are cut for this specific body shape and with the stretch in the fabric, I could have sized down (normally I size up for my hips). There is a bit of excess fabric at the hips but with a bit of ironing they relax and look great with plenty of room to move. Even though they're a bit big, I love the way that they drape, looking wide and relaxed with a gently graduating boot cut. The finish is nicely tailored and professional. The length is great and measures 29" at the inseam: flats or heels appropriate for me at 5'2". I would still recommend to size down if you're in between. With them being sold as a gabardine trouser, I was expecting them to be a bit more of a classic suiting material. It still has a nice weave to the fabric so I'm pleased with the finish. In all, these Rafaella pants are good quality and excellent value and my bum feels great in them!

There is a bit of excess fabric at the hips but with a bit of ironing they relax and look great with plenty of room to move. Even though they're a bit big, I love the way that they drape, looking wide and relaxed with a gently graduating boot cut. The finish is nicely tailored and professional. The length is great and measures 29" at the inseam: flats or heels appropriate for me at 5'2". I would still recommend to size down if you're in between. With them being sold as a gabardine trouser, I was expecting them to be a bit more of a classic suiting material. It still has a nice weave to the fabric so I'm pleased with the finish. In all, these Rafaella pants are good quality and excellent value and my bum feels great in them!

Great pants, but not shipping I love the pants. They are of a good quality and true to size. I'm 5'3", and the short inseam is the perfect length. I ordered the curvy bootcut, and they fit everywhere without being too tight in the waist. The 4 stars is purely because of how it was shipped. I clearly marked the option to have the pants sent in Amazon packaging because I know manufacturer shipping materials are often lackluster at best. As you can see in the photo, that's not standard Amazon packaging. It's raining here today, so if I hadn't been here when UPS dropped the pants at my door, rain definitely would have gotten into the packaging and possibly ruined the pants. Great pants, but you're taking a risk with the shipping.

Great work pants for curvy gals! I love these pants. I'm 5'7, size 12, 33in waist, 45in hips, 170lbs and a size 12 fits perfectly, maybe a tiny bit big but I don't like things too tight. I I have a hard time finding pants that fit both my waist and hips and are not too tight. These are comfortable and fit well. Stretchy but don't look like yoga pants. The grey, however, is very dark - much darker than the photo. I wish these came in a lighter grey. I plan on buying a couple more colors.

I was skeptical but I read the reviews and glad I did! Needed some comfy - but professional- pants for an interview. Shopping for pants online is hit or miss so I was extra thankful and surprised these were a hit. I read the reviews and bought the higher end of my normal size and they fit perfectly. I am between a 14 curvy fit to16 regular fit with waist gap so I ordered the 16. They are very comfortable and flattering so I had more confidence going into my interview than I would have had with some ill-fitting, cheaply made pants. They are very good quality for the price and I’m sure I will get quite a few wears out of them. I did not have any kind of waist gap and they stretched enough to go over my curvy hips without looking like I was going to bust out of them like a can of biscuits. The length was perfect - socks weren’t showing when I sat down and I wasn’t walking on them. I could have even worn a little heel with them if I felt like breaking an ankle to make an lasting impression on my interviewers. For reference, I am 5’5 and about 190lbs.

Perfect curvy/plus pant! These are an awesome purchase!! I am curvy/plus size and have a hard time finding a pant that fits proportionally let alone is flattering! These fit me so well and are pretty comfy on top of it. I will be buying more colors. Love wearing them with a boot since I am 5’3 to give the full boot cut look. Highly recommend!!

Exactly what I was looking for! These pants are perfect for the office. They don’t show any lint or other material which is very nice. They fit perfectly and as expected. I got a size 4, I am 5’ 7” and 160lbs. It is a little short on me but I have very long legs so I’m used to it. These pants actually went longer than some of my other dress pants which was very nice! Will definitely buy more

Love these pants - I own in every color at 5'3", it is hard to find pants that are the right length. These come in short length, have a stretch to them, and can be worn with flats or a small heal/boot. They are extremely comfortable. I bought them in every color!

Cute! So I usually wear a 14 but amazon suggested a 16 and im glad i went with the 16! Perfect fit, and great for servers! I’m 5’7” and 195lbs for reference.. if you’re any taller than 5’7” i would suggest getting a longer inseam!

Size 4 is NOT a size 4 I am a size 4 jeans/pants size. 27 inch waist and I'm 5'5". I started lifting and gained that peach booty while keeping my waist pretty much the same size, but I could not fit in regular pants or jeans anymore so I started looking for curvy fits. This is NOT the usual size 4. The size 4 is more like a size 6-8 in regular pants size. The waist was too large for me.
I love these pants! The material is thick and drapes very nicely. I am 5'6 150 lbs and usually wear a size 8. The 8 was too long and big, but the 6 fits very well. (Photo is off the too large pair)

Decent fit but colors ran horrifically in the wash and even stained later loads These fit nicely, but first I want to make sure anyone reading this is aware of what an ordeal it was to wash and clean up after these pants, as the dye ran worse than anything I have ever seen in my life. Full disclosure: I am not 100% sure who is the culprit, as I washed both this pair of pants and a new pair of the Rafaella lightweight satin twill ankle dress pants (also in black) at the same time. I wouldn't have noticed how horrifically they color ran due to the them both being black, except that I had also tossed a brand new light pink bra in a white mesh delicates bag in at the same time - that one's on me, I was just thinking "wash the new stuff before wearing" and not about color bleeding. I used Woolite detergent, and when I pulled the previously white delicates bag out, it was the blue/purple color of ballpoint pen ink (fortunately the bra inside was just a slightly more mauve-y dusty pink, not completely dyed). I soaked the bag and bra, and have gotten some of the dye out, and I washed the pants through two more cycles. Out of an abundance of caution, I ran the washer again in case dye was left in the machine, and put in an old white cleaning rag as a test. It came out purple. I ran it multiple more times with a new white cleaning rag each time, and they eventually got lighter and lighter purple. I ran a load of Tide Laundry Machine cleaner through, and then bought a box of Shout Color Catcher sheets to run through, and after maybe a dozen wash cycles across two days, I'm finally confident I've gotten all the residual dye out and have run a load of regular laundry through with no problems. I'm nervous to wash either pair of pants again, as I'm not sure if I'll have to go through this whole process again or if I've gotten out all the dye that's going to bleed. I've included pictures of my purple rag progression (the mesh bag is also in the photo, but that's after soaking and running it through the wash again), as well as the tags from both pairs of pants where you can see that they are stained purple as well (although it has lightened significantly from the first wash). I'm actually rather glad I had the bra/mesh bag in that same load; even though they got stained, I would have otherwise just tossed a load of regular clothes in next and ruined nearly my entire wardrobe. I left this same warning and photos on a review for the other pants' page as well. But let's talk about the fit of the actual pants! These are very nice, but not perfect pants. I really struggle to find pants that fit my hips and thighs but don't have a horrible gap at the waist - I usually end up cinching it in paper bag style with a belt. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and ordered a size 14 curvy (sized up from the suggested sizing chart, which would have put me in a 12: 32 waist, 43 hips). These pants fit and flattered my legs nicely, and the waist is a bit loose but manageable. I'll probably move the buttons/hooks over a bit to help. They are quite long on me and will need hemmed, even to wear with heels, but I expected that. I wish the material was a bit thicker - I would describe it as almost midway between dress pant material and tee shirt material. In black, this isn't as much of an issue, but in a lighter color I think it would be somewhat clingy and probably showing off bumps and dimples in ways that I wouldn't like. Overall, I'm satisfied with the pants, but I don't think they were worth the laundry debacle.

Great dress pants! I’m a curvy girl & recently gained some weight. I remembered I had some Rafaella dress pants before that fit well. I ordered 6 of these to try. Note: these pictures are the bootcut, wider leg and come with buttons. They also have narrower legged that I liked, but I chose the wide leg. I prefer the buttons over the hooks on the waist. I tried 12’s & 14’s in straight, bootcut & short. The short length was too short. The 12’s fit perfectly. Weirdly, the black pair is tighter than the navy or vintage wine. I’m 5’6, 156# with the menopause belly & big butt. These pants fit well. I haven’t washed them yet, but I think they will be fine. With the buttons, you can move them over tighter or looser! The buttons are already getting loose on the navy pair. I see seeing in my future. Other than that, they are great. I bought them in 3 colors. Comfy & flattering. I showed you the unflattering pics so you could see the buttons, how the waist fits & the belt I added to hold the navy ones closed. These I actually bought from the manufacturer & they have belt loops. Not sure the Amazon pairs had belt loops.

Elegant, Professional, Great Fit LOVE these pants! I'm 5'2 and 130lbs, and ordered a size 6. The pants fit wonderfully around the waste and legs-- and are wonderfully professional and elegant. The leg's length were a bit too long but I'm a short person so I just wore heels and it was fine. Overall, amazing fit-- worth the money! I'll definitely be buying more pairs.

surprisingly comfortable business-casual pants I saw "supreme stretch" and thought these would be almost like pajama pants, but they're actually more like extremely-forgiving work pants :) The "stone" color is more beige than shown in Amazon's photo, and the size "medium" on me were fitted but longer than on the model (but I'm 5'2" and the model is taller-- I'll wear these with heels). The fabric is more sturdy than I had expected, and the seams are sewn in a way that give it a more-solid feel, so I'd actually be comfortable wearing these to work. Be aware that there are **no pockets**. Yes, they're pictured, but they're fake... though I appreciate that they contribute to the impression of these being nice (probably "business casual") pants rather than casual everyday clothes.

Review of: Rammus Women's Fleece Lined Dress Pants with Pockets For fitting reference: I am 5’4”, long torso, 28” around the mid rise waist band (a few inches below belly button and natural waist), 36.5” around my widest section/hips. I am wearing a size small in the black color. The inseam of the pants measured slightly over 27”. Pros: + Waist band is comfortable and stretchy + Belt loops may be handy should I loose weight + Minimal gapping at the back of the waist band with squatting down/ bending + Size chart was accurate for my size + Fleece lining does provide some warmth, but not enough to enough to make you sweaty indoors + Pockets hold my iPhone 12 mini + Washed and dried well with no signs of wear after first couple of times through the laundry + Enjoyed wearing at least 5 times in the last 2 weeks of early November in the Midwest (considering getting another pair) + Fabric does seem durable and not easy to snag + Appears to be sewn well / seams look well constructed + Easy to move in Cons - Fabric is a bit shinier than appears in the model photos - Fabric makes some noise when walking if one leg brushes against the other - They look more snug around my upper thighs than desired, but I am pear shaped and that is where I carry my weight. I do tend to wear these with a longer tunic style top to cover the extra snugness in the same way that I would with leggings. To clarify, they do not feel overly snug or constraining in any way. I want to say they are a 4.5 star pant for me. Since half stars are not an option, I rounded up because I honestly keep reaching for them. Functional pockets + comfort = worth it!

Love these! I took a chance on these pants because I need some dressy pants for winter that are actually comfortable to wear. These are perfect! I got the teal color which is very pretty, though you can't really tell the color in my photos. They have a nice sheen but not to shiny. The material weight is exactly what I was hoping - not too thick and it hangs well. The pants fit like jeans with a font zipper, top button and two side pockets. I followed the size chart measurements and got an extra large. I thought these might be too small around the wait, but the elastic makes them fit perfectly. I like that the pockets lay flat and don't stick out, which can sometimes be a problem with softer materials. The length was exactly right for me at 5'4". I expect to get plenty of use from these this winter and definitely recommend them!

LOVE the pockets and the variety of colors and lengths! So this is the third pair I have ordered because I just love these! Unfortunately the two first ones that I purchased were slightly too big (I ordered size M for both) and they kept falling down when I was running, which was super frustrating. BUT I just love how comfortable these are and I also love the pockets (where I keep my phone while running and also a taser, cause you know, just in case) so I decided to give them another chance and got this purple pair in size S and these definitely fit A LOT better.
