Perfect fit at a perfect price! Loving it! I am half way through shedding years of accumulated flab of motherhood. At 5'4" and broad hips for my otherwise in between a petite and regular frame, I am wary of shopping for any bottoms as it could be a tedious job to find a balance for my broad hips, plus longish legs for my height, but a not on the tall side build. I am also quite a novice to athletic wear and am skeptical about spending too much on it. When I found this, I ordered it based on the reasonable price and good reviews, and it is a god send. Never did I think work out wear could be so form flattering while being simultaneously so comfortable. Definitely ordering more, I could use more colours for casual wear.

Excellent leggings! I seen these on Facebook and decided to give them a try! Soft yet good support. High waisted and STAYS PUT!! After a quick 15min HIIT no adjustment needed! So far they are not see thru at all. The vent holes are a nice added flair without compromising the integrity of the fabric. All seams are well made. No camel toe for me or cellulite visible. Im 5ft 2in and a fir 150lbs. I bought a size medium.
Will definitely be getting more! These are fabulous! Super soft, passed the squat test, fit like a dream. I’m 5’6, 185lbs, and I bought a Large.

excellent fit on my tall, super plus sized frame; very comfortable I went with the size XXL because the size chart in the pictures states that XXL is size 20/22. I'm pretty much a size 20. I took my chance on this because sometimes things just don't fit right or feel right. I am SO glad that I got these- the sizing is perfect. I can tell it can stretch more (but hopefully wont need to). When they arrived, my initial thought was that they won't fit. But I put them on with ease. The polyester spandex blend is perfect. It is snug without being constricting. It is compressing without feeling like being encased like a sausage. My lower belly apron (flab) appreciates the additional support. High waist keeps the capris in place. I find that I do not need to hike them up. I can sit, bend, run around, and they aren't falling off. This is important because I am tall (5'10", 300lbs), and sometimes the way clothes are made are meant for shorter frames, or so called high waists roll, etc. So this pair of capris is rather perfect for me. Gusseted crotch lends to extra comfort, ease of movement, and not as much strain on the seams. The pockets are functional yet aren't noticeable when not in use. They lay flush. Surprisingly deep. The color gray, depending on lighting has more of a purple undertone. I like it a lot, even more so than the dull light gray I often see. I really like it paired with a black shirt. The material is thin enough to feel light and breathable, yet has plenty of coverage. I could make out my underwear line, but not my underwear. The mesh is not revealing; it looks more like a pattern. I find these to be very comfortable, and am pleased with the overall look, fit, and feel. As a plus sized person, it means a lot to find something that I feel good in. I am highly considering another pair or two in different colors. Again, very happy to have tried this, and now I have another brand I can count on.

Comfy, Thick, and Flattering! I've been wearing leggings non-stop since working from home; figure these RAYPOSE leggings will double as lounge-wear for now and I'll have them for when I finally start working out again as well, ha! The fit is comfy and not too tight: I'm 5'1", 105lbs and got a small (I'm usually an XS-S). The material is suuuuper soft and very thick; definitely squat-proof! Happy that these are actually high-waisted as well and come above my belly button. The cut is flattering, and would be more so if I was bothering to work out 🙈 Color is exactly as it appears on my screen: the dark gray is more of a taupe-y color. My only concern are the little holes in the fabric on the sides that allow for greater air flow and breathability. I'm just not sure if they will end up unraveling or tearing eventually, as they are just cut into the fabric and not finished on any way. So far they are holding up though, so I'm very happy with these super comfortable leggings! Would highly recommend! BTW- I got another pair for my mom: a medium, and she is 5'-1", 150 lbs and she says they fit great. They are snug, but this proves there is some flexibility in the sizing chart, since based on her weight she should wear a large. She said she thinks a large would fit, but they just wouldn't be as snug.

Pretty, not at all itchy I was hesitant to order these yoga pants because I've gotten several pairs recently that have large, itchy seams and itchy material. However, these looked soft and I liked the color so I decided to risk it, and I'm glad I did! They are very soft, almost like brushed fabric, and there are no large seams inside to dig into me. They're very comfortable! I like the mesh... it's cute but not too distracting or revealing. The pockets are deep and fairly vertical, so my Galaxy S10 fits (and stays put). I was also excited by the length-- they're almost long enough to come to the top of my sock (not quite, but close enough, and better than most on my 33.5" inseam). They fit well. I ordered an XL because I assumed they're Asian sizing, but they're not super tight and they have room in the waist (but I wouldn't size down because I have a booty and they might get see-through if I did). They're not see-through in this size and they feel great. They come to my bellybutton, so no muffin top but also not so high-waisted that they are up to my ribs (I hate that). These fit me pretty close to perfectly for yoga pants, and the color is really pretty. I washed them in cold and hung them to dry--they did fine. I wouldn't pay $50 for these, but as far as less expensive yoga pants go these are pretty great (no price is listed yet, so I can't factor value into my rating). I would definitely order another pair. For reference, I'm 5'10", 185 lbs, 36dd, 32 waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the XL.

Very impressed These are almost perfect, they are high rise and won’t roll down waist. They are tummy control for sure there is no stretch in the tummy area at all. They are very soft but not buttery soft. I normally don’t like mesh but with these I didn’t mind it the mesh is not as wide so it doesn’t see through as much. I weigh 150lbs 5,4 normally wear a size 8/10, in jeans. On my high end leggings I wear a size 10 received these in medium and they fit tight but not uncomfortable I think I would be a Large but will probably loose the tummy control tightness. The pocket on the side is nice but since the leggings are tight on me i couldn’t really put anything in them. These are capris but since I’m 5,4 they fit perfect ankle length. Overall I do like them and that’s coming from someone who spends $120 in leggings I will be buying a large to compare the fit.

Not flattering on me in this shade but comfortable and well made I really liked these until I had my boyfriend take a picture of the back. Oh my.. no. You can see every single dimple and flaw because these are thin. These aren't see through but they don't help hide your problem areas at all. I've been wearing these while hiking and they stay up really well. I'm only 5'1 and they are a little long but not enough to look bad. The pockets fit my Samsung S8+ in a protective case. The sizing is accurate. I ordered large and I normally wear large. This fabric is more similar to swimsuit fabric and it's stretchy and not very compressing or slimming. Overall I like them and if I had cellulite free legs I'd wear these all the time. The color I ordered didn't not help. If this is a problem for you, you might want to try a patterned pair or darker color.

Dark grey is more of a purple grey The color description for these leggings was dark grey, but in person they are a very purple-grey/dusty lavender color. My pictures I added with this review show the true color. I actually really love the color because it is very unique and I don't own any leggings this color! They fit true to size (I pot my normal size: medium and my measurements are hips: 40" and waist: 28"). The mesh material detailing on the sides adds interest and added breathability. The quality is excellent. The material is very smooth and lightweight but not see-through at all! So great for working out in. There is a good amount of stretch that make them comfortable and there is a good amount of support in the leggings so they hold you in well. The only downside to non-black colored leggings is that they tend to show any leg dimples, but don't let that defer you! Your legs are perfect and will look cute in these adorable purple leggings no matter what! If underwear lines bother you though be sure to wear the no-show kind to avoid any visible lines.

I love these pants!! Fit/Stretch/Durable I bought these pants last minute the night before we left to go hiking because I discovered that my favorite pair of prana convertible pants was missing one bottom half of the zip off leg! Ugh. Cue overnight delivery options and scurrying to pick one. Well I am very pleased!! I am 5' 5 "1/2 and approximately 128 lbs I was unsure about buying the small or the medium but I like for my hiking pants to be a little bit snug because as they stretch out during the hike they slip down. And with the seat of the pant hanging low it actually puts more stress on your thigh (quad) leg muscles to 'fight' against the garment you're wearing if you can move freely. (True!!) So I went for the small because if the medium was too big and baggy I would've had to forgo wearing them. But the small fit great with elastic waist AND they actually make me look like I have a butt which is awesome because most snug ish pants just flatten my butt!! SORRY for pics , hard to take body selfies without a mirror. HaThe material is thin, that's ok because I can put fleece leggings underneath, but be able to use them for warm weather camping too. The quality feel is nice, the pockets are great and the stretch is amazing for climbing and squatting. Haven't tested the waterproofing yet, but as long as they repell some water I'll be happy. The leg opening is just big enough to go over top of my hiking boots. If I could change anything I'd make them 1 inch longer but they are NOT too short. Happy camper here. Hope it helps.Update**** wore them 4 days in a row in Feb, north Georgia mountain weather camping and hiking. Don't look worn or dirty, no damage. They are wind resistant to a comfortable temp and in colder conditions I just wore thin leggings underneath. Great budget pant. Look good enough to wear in to town too. Comfortable, stretchy for climbing. I'm going back for more and to hopefully get a convertible pair from this company!

Updated for button issue-Impressed! Light, comfortable, cool Update: Faux button fell off after 2nd wash. It's a hook fastener, not a button. When the rivet pulls thru I'll have to fix somehow. Still like pants tho. 1st review: Bought them to mow grass in. NW FL heat index was 103° today when I tried them out. I'm impressed! Compared to my blue jeans these are light weight, COOL, stretchy, breathable, comfortable and nicely designed. The grass and dirt don't stick at all to them. I'm 5'8" 225# wear 16, sometimes 18, 2X fit best. I returned 3X they were too baggy. I put on bright underwear and I couldn't see them thru material. I was much cooler but did sweat a bit at waistband probably from elastic. My workout top made me sweat worse! The green which is slightly more sage than the photo but perfect. I'm very pleased, ordering a 2nd pair. Downsides I can live with: they wrinkle easily, can't decide if it's noticable yet. They don't have the zip off conversion to shorts I wanted. A touch short for 5'8".

Great EMS/Tactical Pant Alternative! I work for an ambulance and our uniform requires black tactical pants. For years I’ve been looking for a tactical pant that was actually comfortable or stretchy but have had no such luck (tried several brands and so far the 5.11 stryke in women’s was the best I could do). This pant is waterproof and looks almost identical to the typical EMS pant. I love that it has a zipper pocket and the rest have Velcro. What I love most about this pant is that they are super super stretchy, light weight, and comfortable! You can put a tactical belt on them and only your crazy 5.11 snob coworkers would ever know the difference :) I’m 5’4 125-130lbs and carry my weight in my hips.

Fun pants but they run snug, to me. The pants are made of good, lightweight material. The color is as pictured. They do make a swishy sound when you walk like windsuits of days gone by. I ordered the medium size because that's what I typically wear, but they are too small. The waistband lands above my belly button at my natural waist and is snug to the point of mild discomfort while standing and much more noticeable discomfort when seated. You can see in the photos that it cinches in pretty tight at the top. The opening at the ankle is tapered so my boots *fit* under the pants, but when I walk they ride up and rest on top of the boots. It's annoying trying to put them back over my boots because they rise up when I bend over to adjust them. I am ordering these in size large hoping they fit because I like the pants and hope they will work for an upcoming trip. I'll update when those arrive. I always appreciate reviewers who include their measurements. It helps me decide what size I might need so I am offering up my insights to help others :) I'm about 5'5" / 5'6" and 125-130 lbs depending on the day. For shirts, dresses, pants, shorts, etc I usually wear a medium.

The perfect pant exists! Wow, I’m so pleased with these lounge pants! My body type is short and stout with lots of curves and I didn’t know whether to get 2X or 3X. I got the 2X and the fit is perfect. I think they may be supposed to be capri length because I’m only 5’1” (but somehow I’m 260 lbs 🤷🏾‍♀️) and the legs fit me like they were tailored 😊 The waist is snug and stretchy with so much give that it kind of molds to your waist like a gentle hug. Not constricting at all, but definitely stays put so no riding up or falling down. The material is soft and ultra light without being flimsy. The pockets are wonderful. The solid color pants can easily be paired with a tunic, nice sweater, or long blouse and heels or flats to have the most comfy business casual work outfit ever. Also they are so soft, stretchy and lightweight, you can do yoga and Pilates in them. And of course they are great for lounging and sleeping. Great, no regret purchase. I will be ordering more in other colors.

Perfect Pants for Curvy Girls/Ladies! These pants fit incredibly well and boy do they accentuate your curves. I now own 6 pairs of these pants you can see most of them in my other reviews. Here I am wearing the gray ones in size 22- I could have gone smaller but I have HIPS and since these are for work, I didn’t want to go tighter. These pants have a lot of stretch to them which is nice and they fit like zippered pants! In the picture you can see how they fit with and without a 3.5 inch heel so they are long - I’m 5’7.5 and they fit me perfectly with at 3.5 inch heel. If you are shorther than 5.7 then you may need to have the pants taken up at the cleaners. I also bout this broth on Amazon and it is the perfect compliment for these pants (they have 4 belt loops) which I need with the classic coke bottle shape, small waist and larger hips and thighs. Order your regular size for dressy occasions and to impress that special someone in your life (or soon to be). Order a size up for work and get the belt ($8.00) and adjustable to like size 40 I think, and you will be as happy as I am! Oh and I included a picture of the pants laid out after I washed them in cold water. So I recommend you have them dry cleaned instead as one black pair lost their sheen after washing (on the left) and the Calais color (far left) looked a little more brown than burgundy after washing, sadly. But that doesn’t stop me from wearing them; it’s just something I noticed and want others to be aware of. I also own the dark green pair; they each fit the same and look the same. The material of the navy blue pair doesn’t seem to be the same weight as all the others - they are lighter and thinner for some reason. So there you have it! These are the perfect pants for girls and ladies with real curves!

If you are shorther than 5.7 then you may need to have the pants taken up at the cleaners. I also bout this broth on Amazon and it is the perfect compliment for these pants (they have 4 belt loops) which I need with the classic coke bottle shape, small waist and larger hips and thighs. Order your regular size for dressy occasions and to impress that special someone in your life (or soon to be). Order a size up for work and get the belt ($8.00) and adjustable to like size 40 I think, and you will be as happy as I am! Oh and I included a picture of the pants laid out after I washed them in cold water. So I recommend you have them dry cleaned instead as one black pair lost their sheen after washing (on the left) and the Calais color (far left) looked a little more brown than burgundy after washing, sadly. But that doesn’t stop me from wearing them; it’s just something I noticed and want others to be aware of. I also own the dark green pair; they each fit the same and look the same. The material of the navy blue pair doesn’t seem to be the same weight as all the others - they are lighter and thinner for some reason. So there you have it! These are the perfect pants for girls and ladies with real curves!

Thicker Material, good quality Update: The color drains tremendously, but the dark brown oatmeal color now looks more traditionally khaki. I’ve washed them twice a week, for over a month now and they do not shrink! I admit that I ordered the wrong size and that’s an error on my part. The legs are still too long and they get really lose in the waist due to the wearing in of the elasticity. Buy them a size or 2 too smaller; I will order them in a 22 regular (not wide) petite. I will be ordering more because they are very comfortable dress slacks and the material is strong and not flawed. I wear them for work in an office and I’m sitting standing, climbing stairs and they are very flexible. I purchased the Oatmeal color because Khaki was lighter than I like to wear in pants, I’m 5’4, 300 lbs and ordered 24W. I wish I would have ordered a size down and not the wide because they come under my bust. I have a large bottom and the seat is very large in these pants. I read some reviews that they shrink after wash so I’m hoping on it. The legs were long. I don’t plan to wear these with heels, only the non-slip sneakers pictured here. I didn’t purchase the petite so the 3 inches longer length is dragging the floor. I plan to wear a belt with these and I can hem the bottom. I ordered 3 but I think I’ll return them for a smaller size.

But if your looking for comfort as well as professional here ya go.

For tall women this is a no! I’m 5’10 & the first time I wore these pants the length was fine. It was a little below my ankle but I was okay with that. So I washed them according to the instructions & I HUNG them to dry & guess what?! They shrunk. They are well above my ankle now. I’m upset that I spent $30 on a pair of pants I could only wear one time.

LOVE (size 24, measurements are 55/45/55) I ordered a size 24W (USA). My measurements are 55/45/54, I'm about 5'5-5'6, I wear a size 22 pants usually, around 260ish pounds (give or take like 15 depending on the day/week). I love these a whole lot. There are real pockets but they're sewn shut, a quick snip and they're functional. Thick, quality, crunchy material, pretty color, cozy fit (maybe a tiny bit big), perfect length for me. I was super pleasantly surprised that they worked well with the vest and dark charcoal jacket I bought. Wearing them to my abuser's trial, thanks for being as advertised.

I love these so much! I'm short (5'0 ft) and plump (200+) so it's always a struggle to find pants that I don't have to hem, look good on me and are comfortable. These pants are hitting all the marks. As soon as I'm done typing this review, I'm ordering more.

Fell in love I didn’t like them much when I first tried them on. I felt they were way too long. I’m in the over 50 age range, I’m 5’1” I think my inseam is 27 (or 28”) and about 200 lbs. I bought a 16 Short. They seemed to have snugged up after the cold water wash. I did put them in the dryer with the other laundry. I lack the desire (read “patience”, lol) to hang up clothes to dry. I probably should have went up a size, but I’ve lost 30lbs and am working still working on it. I also dislike it when pants become baggy/saggy during the day; and these did not. I did feel like I had to pull them up here and there through out the day, so I may wear a belt next time. The tummy control could be better, but I could also stop eating cake, so there’s that. Overall, I’m ordering another pair as we speak because I really do like how comfortable they are. I may try an 18 and return them if they’re too big. Hahaha, you see that? I made a joke! I’m not gonna stinking return anything (said in my ‘Granny’ voice) lol. I’ll just have to eat more cake so they’ll fit better.

Silver looks like Green!! I’m 5’7 145lbs and I order a size 10. They fit as expected, they are soft and look good always. I have about 4 colors and they are consistent in size and length. I took a star off because in person the silver looks like a light olive kahki green. It doesn’t look silver. I posted a picture with my leg next to a silver bowl so you can get some sense but the picture doesn’t do it justice. These pants are more comfortable than jammies, I prefer wearing them over jeans. This same company puts out another non tummy control version of these that are exactly the same except they are actually boot cut as opposed to straight leg. After ordering about 4 pairs of each, I realized this was the only difference between them. But they don’t tell you in the description or give you an option to buy straight leg vs boot cut. The boot cut is a wider leg, but the straight leg makes me legs look more slender all the way down and fit over the top of my heels too. I just wish they had an actual very light grey in size 10. They need an option to wait list until those colors come in again. The pretty light blue is also not available in my size. But otherwise I lovse these pants!

Cute, comfortable, perfect fit! I just received these and they fit perfect! I usually wear DressBarn secret agent straight leg pants in 2 petite. They haven’t had khaki since last year when I was still losing weight and I was in petite 4. They only have black, charcoal, and navy in the similar straight leg DressBarn petite pants. I was on a mission to find comparable pants and I searched every where! I found these and I was so excited! I waited a while and I finally decided to purchase. I could probably fit a 2 but they are for work and I don’t want them skin tight. These have a nice stretch and nice thickish fabric. I ordered these in 4 short! For reference I’m 5’0 and weigh 120 lbs. I’m worried I may put them in the dryer by mistake but I’m taking the risk! I already can’t wait to order them in more colors!! EDIT: I bought these in April 2019, its now August and I've been wearing every week and washing and drying along with all of my other clothes and have not had any issues with the seams at all!! *knock on wood* I would definitely recommend these to anyone looking for comfortable pants that work with a business casual dress code.

I think I can wear them with booties and with flats. I will absolutely be ordering more. I’m sad after reading the reviews, that after 6 months the elastic in the seems shows, that’s a bummer it’s hard to find pants that I love I’m hoping they last longer then 6 months.

These pants fir nice but requires ironing. . Disappointed in these pants although the fit is nice the size was perfect and the length just right for my 5”7 150 body. The problem is the elastic in the in the waist band is very thin and after first wash it rolled needs to be ironed to straighten the waist band ,the legs were difficult to iron they don’t lay flat or straight to iron crease. So will probably return for the price I feel too much. Plus not a fan of ironing.

Great! I'm about 5'2", 135 lbs... I ordered an 8 short and they fit perfectly! The length is just past my ankle, not too long. And the cloth is just stretchy enough to hug curves without being too tight. And the fit across the butt is nice, no baggy butt! I like that the waist is fairly medium in height. I only ordered a few pair to make sure they fit ok, but I will be ordering more. I think I will get the slight boot cut next time though as the legs are a bit skinnier than I expected.

Hole on Day 1 I had such high hopes for these pants. Today was the first day I wore them, so here's my review. I ordered my regular size that I would order at any other store...the fit is okay - a bit snug in the thigh/rear area. They are also quite long. I can't remember if there is a 'long/tall' size, but I just ordered the regular inseam and found that they were quite long (I am 5'7" for reference). If you normally wear your pants with heels, then this may not be an issue for you. The material is thin, so they may not keep you very warm this winter, but I don't find that it looks too cheap or anything. The problem with these pants is that I found a small hole in the leg with the threading already coming undone (see photo). I know you get what you pay for but I was expecting them to hold up for at least a few wears. Would not order again.

Perfect fit Bought a pair for job interviews. Size 4 short. I am 5'0" and 105 lbs. They fit perfectly. The material has a good amount of stretch, so they are comfortable. They look very professional and perfect for a business casual outfit. The length is good for wearing with flats or heels. Looks flattering from behind too. Do not put in the dryer. Hang them up to dry. Very happy with my purchase, the price is fair for the quality.

Ordered a size 4...Overall A- I'm 5'5" and weigh between 115-120. I usually wear a size 2, and in some brands a size 4. Size usually 4 fits ok but gets saggy in the derriere after a few wears. With all my other pants, this is easily remedied by washing/heat drying. I'm a mommy who sits at a desk all day long and prefers eating ice cream to working out. My tiny Asian mother blessed me with a thin build but I still have all the imperfections that come with parenthood and it being the hiking off season aka the dreaded winter of the PNW. These pants definitely do a great job of hiding all flaws...self-consciousness be gone! However, the size 2 wasn't available so I thought... what the heck, perhaps the size 4 will shrink a little after drying. After reading so many reviews, I'm going to try a low heat setting then air dry as I don't want to destroy the fabric by overheating. When I first put them on, the shape was weird around my waist. They're a thick, stretchy fabric with a very light sheen, and they don't feel at all like those ultra soft jeggings (which is precisely what I was looking for). Perfect for wearing to work and true to color (really dark charcoal gray). They pull on with no button/zipper, yet have two full sized pockets up front. Without a shirt tucked in, the shape at the waist is baggy... Seems they are shaped to accommodate working up and around larger hips. Kinda bugged me at first, but I got over it. With a shirt tucked in, looks very professional and cute. The length at the ankle is a bit longer than I'd like, but I can live with it. Will definitely order the shorter pants next. These sit just halfway through my belly button and hips... SUPER COMFY! No plumber's butt (I'm not 21 anymore) and not overly high waisted. Doesn't ride up in unwanted places. I did notice a weird part in the stitching on the rear though... It's a bit shinier there and almost looks like it's coming apart. I did a minor stretch test and the threads are holding tight... Just SHINY in that spot. Even with my little complaints (borderline neurotic/OCD when it comes to clothing) I do like these pants and will try other colors, but definitely going with my usual size 2 next time around. Hope this helps!

Ordered a size 4...Overall A- I'm 5'5" and weigh between 115-120. I usually wear a size 2, and in some brands a size 4. Size usually 4 fits ok but gets saggy in the derriere after a few wears. With all my other pants, this is easily remedied by washing/heat drying. I'm a mommy who sits at a desk all day long and prefers eating ice cream to working out. My tiny Asian mother blessed me with a thin build but I still have all the imperfections that come with parenthood and it being the hiking off season aka the dreaded winter of the PNW. These pants definitely do a great job of hiding all flaws...self-consciousness be gone! However, the size 2 wasn't available so I thought... what the heck, perhaps the size 4 will shrink a little after drying. After reading so many reviews, I'm going to try a low heat setting then air dry as I don't want to destroy the fabric by overheating. When I first put them on, the shape was weird around my waist. They're a thick, stretchy fabric with a very light sheen, and they don't feel at all like those ultra soft jeggings (which is precisely what I was looking for). Perfect for wearing to work and true to color (really dark charcoal gray). They pull on with no button/zipper, yet have two full sized pockets up front. Without a shirt tucked in, the shape at the waist is baggy... Seems they are shaped to accommodate working up and around larger hips. Kinda bugged me at first, but I got over it. With a shirt tucked in, looks very professional and cute. The length at the ankle is a bit longer than I'd like, but I can live with it. Will definitely order the shorter pants next. These sit just halfway through my belly button and hips... SUPER COMFY! No plumber's butt (I'm not 21 anymore) and not overly high waisted. Doesn't ride up in unwanted places. I did notice a weird part in the stitching on the rear though... It's a bit shinier there and almost looks like it's coming apart. I did a minor stretch test and the threads are holding tight... Just SHINY in that spot. Even with my little complaints (borderline neurotic/OCD when it comes to clothing) I do like these pants and will try other colors, but definitely going with my usual size 2 next time around. Hope this helps!

Perfect business casual wardrobe staple I cannot say enough great things about these pants. They’re obviously not perfect, but for the price they’re pretty darn close. I would definitely give them a solid A. The rise is the perfect high waist to give you a great shape, and the hidden tummy control panel helps smooth lines including if you have a shirt tucked it. Keep in mind they don’t actually open at the top with a button or zipper, so it can be a little tricky to tuck in tops that are thicker or have layers, but there’s still plenty of stretch. I’m 5’5” 125 pounds and they were a little long so I just folded the bottoms under a little bit, and you honestly can’t even tell. They’re also not a complete skinny fit, but close enough. The little bit of bag at the bottom doesn’t bother me too much. I specifically waited to take this photo until I wore them for an entire work day. As you can see they stretched out a little, but not drastically. They also are prone to wrinkle, but again nothing that I would be overly concerned with. I love that they have the zip open pockets, however only in the front the back pockets are faux. I would ABSOLUTELY recommend this fit of pants. I already ordered them in 4 additional colors, and will for sure be ordering more in the future as I build my wardrobe. Note some of the colors I ordered the pictures were slightly off from what I was expecting, but still love all the vibrant colors and pattern options. I would LOVE to see these in a bright non-sheer white for summer. I did also tried some of the other fit pants by this brand. I didn’t hate any of them, this was just my preferred fit of pants. So I ultimately returned the other fits and will stick with these. As for the other review of threading issues, I did look these over pretty closely and nothing stood out as concerning. Again, for the price I’m willing to replace these as needed if they get some good wear out of them. Definitely more affordable than many brands like banana republic, jcrew, etc Hope this helps!

Best Work Pants Ever!! I’ve been buying these pants for about 3 years. They run small, so I always buy 1 size up- I’m a size 4, so a 6 is perfect. They have a little stretch to them, so even if you gain a little weight, they’re fine. They hold their shape really well. When you wash them, DO NOT PUT IN DRYER, because they will shrink in the length and sometimes fray at the seam. As long as you hang dry, they’ll be fine. They look so professional and I always get compliments on my outfit when I wear these. I’m 5’6’”, 131 lbs and they are a great length, they don’t ride up on my ankles. I have these in several colors and I will continue to buy them as long as they remain $45. I’ve looked at Loft & Ann Taylor for similar styles, but the fit and look of these are superior, especially for the price.

Looks great but difficult to get on - size up! Just received these pants today so I can't comment on my long term impressions, but I wanted to share my initial review. These pants are great! Material is thick but stretchy and seem high quality. I am 5'6", 155 lbs. generally I wear a size 6 in pants but sometimes 8. Based on the recommendation in the product description, I decided to go with an 8. These have no zipper, so they are actually pretty difficult to get on (especially over my large butt), but once they were on, they looked AMAZING. My husband was also thoroughly impressed my the way they looked and fit. Removing them was also very difficult, but hopefully they will loosen up over time. Overall though, I am very happy with this purchase. They look awesome and I'm sure I'll get used to the difficulty of getting them on/off.

Order by jeans size I have a 26” waist and usually wear a size 4 in jeans. I ordered a size 2 according to the size chart. When they came, I noticed it said UK 4- which is a 00 in US sizes. The pants fit, but I could barely get them up past my butt and they were tight in the waist. Go by your jeans size, not waist size when ordering.

Strange rubbery fabric buckles in the wrong places I wanted to LOVE these pants. They look so sleek and well-made in photos. I am 5'8" 130 lbs, hips 39", waist 27". I got the size 6, which I found to be a TINY bit tight wiggling over my hips. Once on, they FELT great! I was about to snip the tags off when I looked at myself in a mirror. I was bugeyed to see the KNEES. They were wierdly crimpled like the camel's in my childhood "Raggedy Ann & Andy" books! Nope. That's not gonna do at all. I tried smoothing them out, but the rubbery fabric just bounced right back into a wrinkled knee. It's quite possible that if I ordered up a size to an 8 the pants would lay smoother all the way down. But as it was, the length was a bit short, and I knew I didn't want the butt of the pants any bigger. So I sent them back for a refund. Ironically, the much cheaper jersy leggings I bought at Amazon fit superb - but they don't look dressy like these Rekucci pant.

Awesome I first bought these in a 16R, those were too long on me & after having lost weight I needed new ones anyways. Got these in a 14Short, I love the fit! I’m 5”6 and wear a size 14. The regular length would be perfect for taller ladies. I’ve worn them everyday for work with no issues. I love how good they make my booty look and having usable pockets!

DIFFERENT COLORS DIFFERENT SIZES First, I just came upon this brand and I absolutely love it! However I must caution you that your size may depend on the color you choose! I bought a red and black pair, both in size four. The red one "fit" but was likely too snug based on this color. The black were way too big but could be tolerated. As you can see they are "off" about 1 inch in the waist. So I ordered a size 2 in black and a size 6 in red. I am hoping the sizing difference is consistent within that color. Other than that I LOVE these pants. And for perspective I'm 5' 7" with a straight body type (which this brand is FANTASTIC with!) My measurements are about 27" inch waist and 34" hips. I clock in around 118 lbs. I've also been warned about shrinkage and wrinkles. But I've had a similar material with the same issues and I'm good with the fabric care. Well I hope so!

The perfect pair of pants DOES exist. I love these. I am 5’5” and 125 lbs. The photo is me wearing a 6 as everyone said to size up. The six fit great with a heel on as far as length but the waist was big on me. So, I bought some 2’s (and by some, I mean 6 pairs…) and they fit great, just a hair too snug for my preferred look for work as I pair these with blouses and blazers. (The 2s are great for all the non-work stuff). So, the 4s are the winner. And I will be buying at least 22 million more pairs, give or take. They’re not a super thin material which I love. They look and feel like a $90 pair of pants. They don’t wrinkle too easily and I’ve never had an issue with threads or holes or what not like some of the other reviews mentioned. If you’re debating buying these? Don’t just buy them, buy 22 million of them.

My go to brand for good fitting, quality pants. I have at least five pairs of this brand pants and they never disappoint. I can buy the same size in any style and they will fit. Perfect fit These fit like a glove! I bought a size 2 black pants. I am very long-legged and skinny with a short waist. Weight 110lbs. Height 5’4”. They do come above my belly button, which I don’t like the feeling of, so I easily rolled them down at the waist and they stay on great! The fabric is very soft, slightly thicker, and a little shimmery. Also has a stretch ability to snug over my hips and into my waistline without needing a belt. The length lays right at my heel without dragging the floor. I usually need to shop in the juniors department to get a decent fit pants for my small frame, but can never find dress pants for work because I have to shop the junior dept. This is the best pair of dress pants I’ve owned in a long time. Totally going to order more colors!

Waist doesn't have a lot of give, but they fit like a glove! I ordered these pants in a 4 Short after reading reviews about the length. I am 5'2" and typically wear shoes with at least 3" heels. The length of these pans is perfect for me, as I don't like the bottom of my pants rubbing the ground. I could still wear these with flats without worrying about the length.Be warned that there is not a lot of give in the waistband, so if you have any type of butt/hips, you may have to shimmy them up and over. Once they're up all the way though, they are super comfortable.The material of these is lovely, and they look professional with the comfort of yoga pants. I will likely look to see what other colors/patterns I can order from this brand!

Perfect fit!! I have been looking for another pair of pants with this fit. Being 5’2”, 135# and curvy, it’s tough. These are exactly what I’ve been looking for. Skimped on the sizing since last time I bought these... 60 years old, 5’3” and 200 lbs, square shaped. So I own 11 pairs of Rekucci pants, size 18, boot cut. Purchased them over a period of months back in 2016-2017. I machine wash, air dry, just like the instructions say. The colored ones have all aged fabulously, no pilling or fraying at the seams, no loss of stretchiness. The white, ivory and silver Rekuccis all wore out about a year ago (frayed at the crotch and bubbling in the fabric where the stretch gave out). Since I had other pants, I didn’t replace them...until now. I ordered the Silver, in the same boot cut size 18. As you can see from the photo, these pants are cut just a wee bit narrower...and for us full-figured ladies, that extra inch in the waist and thighs makes a BIG difference in look, fit an comfort. Feels like the manufacturer is skimping compared to the ones I purchased two years ago. So - sorry—no more 5 stars. And no more Rekuccis for me. Bummer.

Pleasantly surprised Ordered my usual size, US 4, I'm just shy of 5'8" and about 140lbs. They fit nicely however I was expecting them to be longer. I would not mind another 1/2" or so in length. Fabric is stretchy and looks professional enough to wear to work. It is not soft or remotely similar to a cotton blend yoga pant. I am skeptical on the seams and how durable they will be after several washes. I paid $29.99 with Prime delivery and do like them but feel like the price is high for the product. If they hold up for a year I will boost the rating to 5 stars.

JUNK JUNK JUNK!!! I LOVED these pants. They fit like a dream - as long as you DO NOT follow the washing instructions and have them dry cleaned. But I decided that dry cleaning them has gotten so expensive that I would follow the instructions and wash them in cold water, expecting to let them air dry and iron them. I removed them from the dryer and they were RUINED. I have a 28" waist and before washing these fit. The pictures are of the pants just out of the wash - the elastic in the waistband is completely gone and the fabric no longer has any stretch. They cannot be salvaged. I will write to the company, and if they provide any sort of solution to this, I will update this review. Until then, stay far away from this brand!

Not Perfect, But Pretty Darn Good I wasn't sure about these at first...I'm not really a "skinny pants"/"jeggings" sort of person, but the older I get, the more comfortable I want to be. I bought these thinking I would pair them with long sweaters / cardigans and wear them to work. I was looking for a comfortable, stylish, and professional look. SIZE: I'm 5'4", 120 pounds and size 2 seemed to work the best. I bought both size 2 and 4 just to test it out because I frequently find myself in-between sizes with any brand. According to the sizing chart, I am a size 2, but I didn't trust it so I bought both sizes. I should've trusted it though because it was accurate. The 4's felt nice, but were ultimately just too baggy in the crotch and around the wasteband. I tend to like more relaxed fits in pants, but I knew with the 4 I'd be hiking them up all day. The 2's were more snug...but not in an uncomfortable way. They just fit better around the butt and crotch and I didn't have to hike them up at all during the day. CONS: They're a bit high-waisted than I'm used to. I guess that's the "tummy control" part. It's not a deal-breaker though. They hit at or just under the belly button when standing, and above the belly button when sitting. Also, they're very long. I'm 5"4 and these could use a few inches lopped off the cuffs. It's not a big deal though because you can just fold the cuffs under and they'll stay all day and look perfectly normal. No alterations necessary...just fold the cuffs under. PROS: Very comfortable. When I first put them on, I really wasn't sure if I'd like them, but I thought I'd wear them to work for a day and see. I was extremely comfortable all day. The material is nice and sturdy/thick...they don't feel cheap. The zippers and pockets are stylish. I like that they don't get saggy in the knee after sitting for awhile. Overall, I would recommend these. They're not the perfect pant, but they're pretty darn close.

They hit at or just under the belly button when standing, and above the belly button when sitting. Also, they're very long. I'm 5"4 and these could use a few inches lopped off the cuffs. It's not a big deal though because you can just fold the cuffs under and they'll stay all day and look perfectly normal. No alterations necessary...just fold the cuffs under. PROS: Very comfortable. When I first put them on, I really wasn't sure if I'd like them, but I thought I'd wear them to work for a day and see. I was extremely comfortable all day. The material is nice and sturdy/thick...they don't feel cheap. The zippers and pockets are stylish. I like that they don't get saggy in the knee after sitting for awhile. Overall, I would recommend these. They're not the perfect pant, but they're pretty darn close.

Amazing fit!! These pants are amazing! I'm 5 feet tall and 116 pounds, usually wear a 2 and bought these in a 2. They come up pretty high on the waist and hold everything in! If you have a big butt and small waist you'll have a hard time pulling them up and over since there's no zipper, but once they're on the fit is great. I honestly like the fit of these better than the expensive Betabrand pants online, and the fabric is nearly identical. Wish there were even more colors and patterns! I tried the houndstooth because I thought it was a burgundy color print, but it turned out to be brown so I returned those.

Great for fuller lower body-no waist gap Ordered a size 10...very true to fit. Great for a fuller lower half...nice contour to my back and lite hold at my midsection...hits a good place...1-1.5 inches above my bellybutton. I wear a size 12 jeans...I’m 5’4...had to turn the botttoms under once but pants still have a clean line down....these are worth getting tailored when I get the chance. Very comfortable when sitting...the material is soft and thick can’t wait to get another pair.

Very long crotch The picture I included is a picture of the crotch. It came down to my mid calf. I’m 5’5”. That’s pretty average for a woman. Also they’re very thick and hot. You will sweat in these. I returned them as soon as I tried them on.

I bought these pants hoping for quality professional pants for work. I bought the burgundy pair and the black pair. I was going to buy a size up due to some reviews but decided to stick with my size. I'm glad I did. They fit perfect. There is a review someone has left regarding sizes, cut, colour, and fit and their review was SUPER . They were correct to mention the secret figure pants do taper back in at the waist band. This could bug some people. I used the size chart and I was 1 inch over the measurement for waist, prompting me to think I needed an 8 but the 6 still fits perfect and an 8 would have been too big. I'm 5'3, 150lbs, and the length of the regular was perfect. Definitely going to be ordering many more colours.

Inner thigh seam gave me a rash After wearing these for an evening, my inner thigh became red, swollen, and very painful. I realized the seam on these pants was to blame - it's very prominent. Pic attached. Also, the fit didn't work for me at all. I'm 5'1". The legs are way too long, which I usually expect. But the elastic around the ankle cuffs was huge and served no purpose at all. The cuffs slid right under my heels. I'm, admittedly, a pear-shaped, thicc woman. The front of these pants came all the way up to my boobs, but the back slid completely off my bum every time I sat down. Extremely disappointing. I'm not sure what body type these pants are designed for, but it def isn't mine.

I prefer Spanx This is the second pair of Spanx “dupes” I have tried. The first pair from an online boutique are really great but don’t quite have that faux leather look, so I decided to try these, too since I just couldn’t justify the price tag of Spanx. I ordered the small and medium in these (5’6”, 140lbs, normally small in leggings or 4 in jeans). I ended up keeping the small. These are very stretchy without much compression and they are LONG. Even at 5’6” I had to fold them under at least 2 inches. The small still has some punching behind my knees, but the medium had a lot more extra fabric. I would say these are TTS. Even though I do like these, they didn’t really scratch the Spanx itch. I kept seeing them on bloggers and finally decided to purchase a like-new pair on Poshmark. I’ve included some comparison photos. Overall, I disagree with other reviewers who said they are exactly the same and they couldn’t tell the difference. As you can see, the Retro Gongs are darker and more shiny. They have more of a crackle-glitter texture, whereas the Spanx are more of a smooth, matte with a slight sheen. For me, nothing has been able to replicate the unique look of the Spanx leggings. I’m glad I finally got a pair. If your heart is really set on Spanx but you’re trying to save money, I suggest snagging a pair of Spanx from Poshmark. If you’re not that picky and just want something similar, these might be a good option, especially if you’re tall!

Spanx vs these....I choose these Hesitated. But I have spanx and these now.....dont notice a difference. I wasn't sure if I wanted to try these due to some of the reviews saying that they received thin leggings But I had a completely different experience I was nervous I opened the package it was very nice packaging pull them out they were thicker not too shiny the same day I also received my spanx that I had ordered and honestly I cannot tell the difference. I was going to return these until I tried them on and was like these are the same there is no reason so I'm now going to keep these and telling you at least try if you receive a bad pair obviously return them but I had a good experience and these are great option if you are looking for something less than half the price of spanx. I Am 5 5 about 135 pounds in I ordered a medium they fit perfect I can't complain about anything I love the length it's not too much but it's just right. I had an option to wear these or spanx today or these and I chose these. I did not receive a free pair but I'd love to ;) I just wanted to review being I was so nervous. Hopefully it helps the next

Spanx Dup! Very similar to the Spanx faux leather leggings. They are definitely longer in length compared to the Spanx petite and the Spanx material is just slightly silkier than the Gongs. In all my pictures, the Spanx are on the left and Gongs on the right. The seams are the same too. I ordered the Gongs in medium which are true to size and the Spanx in medium petite. I don’t love the long length of the Gongs nor the price but still cheaper than Spanx. I’m 5’3 and 145lbs. Happy with my purchase overall :)

Pleasantly Surprised These faux leather leggings are a diamond in the rough!!! They are the most comfortable pair you'll ever find. To me, they are a million times more flattering than Spanx & are Way more comfortable. They are extremely soft on the inside with warm fabric which is great bc the leather doesn't stick to your skin at all. They aren't overly shiny; instead they have the perfect amount of shine to keep them classy & fashionable without being childish or costume like. I LOVE these! I will 100% be buying them in every color. Also, they are thick enough to where they are not see through whatsoever, & they Do Not show your panty lines either!!!! Seriously a 30/10. Everyone needs these! I'm 4'10" & 115 lbs with a 26 inch wait & a small fits me like a glove. There is still stretch to them too, so they aren't skin tight by any means. They are around 3-4 inches too long, but I just tuck them into my boots & you can't tell. Even if I were to wear them with sandals though it doesn't look bad at all to have them a little bunched up around my ankles. If that bothers you, just fold them inwards & they'll look like the perfect length!!

BEST SPANX DUPES U WILL FIND! I had tried SO many pairs of spanx faux leather dupes before landing on these. I refused to pay $98 for leggings (so I paid more for the Spanx Faux leather Moto style LOL). I loved the retro gong dupes so much that I just bought a second pair when I saw the price had dropped. My pics compare the first pair I got (left and with red tag) to the latest pair (white tag). In my photo with three pairs, the real Spanx is layered on bottom. The material seems similar but shinier in the current version vs new version and they changed the tag to a cheaper white tag (previously a red tag very similar to Spanx). The inside waistband lining is even identical to Spanx. I can’t recommend these enough. Hopefully the price won’t keep increasing. The fit is very comparable to Spanx but hold you in just slightly less. I can barely tell, but it may differ depending on the composition of your legs. Sizing of these is consistent to Spanx and I sized up to a medium, just like Spanx. I’m 5’6 and 132lb. The length of the leggings are slightly long but will fit pretty well if you wear pants with an inseam of 28” or longer. But there’s no petite sizing, so it is what it is. Get these before you regret it unless you have money to burn and prefer Spanx instead.

Love these!! EDIT: I came back to buy a second pair at the price I paid for them two years ago ($40), only to see that they’ve since DOUBLED in price!! I thought they were pricey but worth it at $40, over my dead body will I pay $80 for a pair of stretchy pants. No thanks. May as well buy real spanx! Took away a star from my initial review because they aren’t THAT amazing! Go get some designer pants for that price!! I’m 5’3” at 130lbs.. I went with a Medium based on reviews (I usually go for a small) and I love them! A little long but it doesn’t look bad (posted an up-close pic of the extra length at my ankles). They aren’t overly shiny, more subtle, which is what I was going for. Super comfy! Definitely a true dupe for other leggings I’ve been eyeing!! Hopefully they hold up with washing.

Perfect Spanx Dupe - Obsessed! LOVE THESE! I’m 5’7, 132 pounds, and I ordered these in black in a size Small. They fit so well and are flattering in allllllll the right places. They are just a little long so I do fold them under so they sit right above my ankle. Such a staple for your closet! You could wear them with fall/winter outfits as I have styled in my photo, or you could even do an oversized t-shirt that is knotted in the front with some white sneakers! So for me, it was hard to dress them up too much because of the waist band being so thick (although the thick waistband is nice to hold in the tummy area), they felt a little more casual in that sense, versus a pair of black leather pants that have a zipper and a button. Either way, highly recommend these, they look just like the Spanx, but half the price! Also, they have washed up very well and look just as they did when I first purchased them :)

Feels like butter! So comfy. Update to 5 stars. I LOVE these leggings. I wear them for going out, not exercise. I typically wear them with a big chunky sweater or a cute shirt. I recently wore them for a tattoo session and they were perfect. I have chunky ankles so I tend to wear them like dancer's pants around my heel. I like the look but it may be strange to others. Don't care. I love them so much!! I plan to buy more. Original review... These leggings are CRAZY comfortable! I got a Medium because that's my go-to size with leggings and though these fit like a dream once they were on, I did snap a thread on a side seam in the process of getting them on my body. For reference, I'm 5'6" and about 157lbs. Bigger lower half but I wear Medium stretchy pants and size 6 jeans. I would have given these 5 stars if not for the weak thread. (Over the last several months and multiple wears, that broken thread hasn't been an issue or unraveled, thus increasing my review to 5 stars.) I tried a few other pairs and these by far were the best. The front is oddly flat but did not bunch up or look odd like other leggings of this style have done. If you are on the fence, try them!

Best Spanx leggings dupe! I bought these after hearing wonderful things from friends and reading the reviews. JUST WOW! I was not expecting to LOVE these! I did go up 1 size (medium) from my regular size (small) to make room for my booty after reading they run small in the reviews. But I love the look, fit, feel, and especially appreciate a NO FRONT SEAM! Perfect shade of black, and I like the slight shine to them for the faux leather look! A spot on dupe for Spanx leggings! I not only enjoy wearing them for a more dressy look, but also find myself wearing them around the house to lounge in because they are THAT comfy! Perfect length to them which is a tough find for me (being 5’7) but these hit perfectly right at or just below my ankle depending on how I distribute the fabric. The high waisted band hits just right; a couple of inches above my belly button. But my one complaint is that the stitching at the top of the band is a bit tighter and occasionally I hear that dreaded sound of threads popping when I overstretch them trying to pull them on. So I’m not sure how long they will hold up. However, I’m sure I will end up buying at least one more pair anyway! For more Amazon reviews check me out on all the places @blushingray!

A solid pair of leggings I was pleasantly surprised by how well these fit. I’m tall and nothing ever fits my legs but these hit just above the ankle and look great with pumps. For reference, I’m 5’10 and 155. They have just the right amount of stretch and the fabric is shiny but not overly so. They’re very flattering. They have a little tummy control but not a ton; I think it’s just the right amount honestly. The waist height is a tad short for my taste but I am on the tall side so that might just be me. I found them very comfortable around my waist and I wore these to a play where I sat for a long time. These are an excellent pair of leggings to have in your wardrobe. Get them!

Small hole in the leg but fit/look great Overall I really really like them. They are super comfortable and they look great. I don’t think it’s noticeable enough to keep me from wearing these with shoes that show my feet but it’s not ideal.

Comfortable pants for traveling, but be aware that they stretch out I bought the black pants in a size small for a trip to South Africa, and was really pleased with them at first. They are very lightweight and comfortable, versatile, and flattering. They fit comfortably loose when I first put them on, freshly washed. The issue I've had is that they stretch out considerably throughout the course of the day. I kept trying to pull them tighter, but there's now a small rip where the cord goes through. It's incredible how much they stretched. For reference, I'm 5'8 and 127 pounds. I ordered a small rather than an xs since they are junior's pants and I was thinking they'd run a little smaller, but I wish I'd gotten the xs. I don't think I'm going to wear these pants on days when I'm active due to the stretching out issue, but I will wear them on long flights because they are comfortable.

Not flattering I feel like a frumpy old lady wearing these pants. They run large. My hip measurement is 40" and I got a size 8 (sizing down one because I read they run big) and they are still kinda loose, even after washing and drying. They are well tapered to the waist for me though, and my natural waist measurement is 25". As I walk, the pants bunch up, pulling away from my ankles. I'm 5'-5" and if the pants didn't bunch up, they would otherwise be the perfect length for wearing with flats. I think they are too short to wear with heels. The pants are extra loose at the knee. I would describe the fit as similar to relaxed fit jeans. The fabric is very stretchy, but also very thick yet lightweight at the same time, and prone to wrinkles. The fabric feels cheap. The pants are reasonably comfortable though.

The good: The pants look great. I'm excited to wear these to work. The sizing is perfect. The 10P fit me perfectly: waist=30'', hips= 40.5'', inseam= 26.5''. They feel well made. The BAD: However, with all the good, I can't give this 5/5. I'm very disappointed in how these were shipped to me. These arrived by themselves in a clear plastic bag with the shipping label holding the bag's open end "closed". The pants were totally viewable to anyone delivering or receiving the package. Not that I am ashamed of what size I bought, it's the principle of the matter. Someone else may be VERY upset that these arrived this way. Or what if these were a gift for someone in my household? Had I known that these pants would be shipped in a package that identifies what it is, I would not have purchased them. I mean a plastic flimsy bag just waiting to be ripped along the perforations? Come on! Also, the hanger is still on the pants and they were folded over the hook of the hanger (so the hanger doesn't rip through the filmy plastic bag).

Tall girls like me will love these. I'd size up!! I'm 5'9 and 150 lb. I wear a size 10 in jeans, but a 6/8 in dresses. Small on top. I'm mostly legs, and straight shaped, not hourglass. I think my actual waist measures 31" or so. I prefer a 35" inseam for anything skinny, and a 36-in inseam for anything wide leg. I ordered an extra large 34" in grey in these joggers. I like them a lot! I wanted a nice loose fit around the waist, but to be able to wear them with a bodysuit and still be cute. they have regular pockets, and then on the left they have a little hidden zipper pocket that could fit a key fob. The material is great for summer. I agree that it's similar to a t-shirt material, but my cellulite does not show through. So it's kind of the best of both worlds. I'll definitely be keeping these. I also got a size large in black. they feel great around the waist, not too tight. And they are a little slimmer around the thighs. I would say the large would be a true fit for me. I'm keeping them too :-) I put up pictures of both. Update: they bag out around the bum and get really saggy after a couple hours, and they don't regain their shape! Returned.

Nice sweats! These sweatpants (joggers) are nice. Kudos to this company for having nice packaging and the option to select your preferred inseam! I’m a tall lady, standing at a dazzling 5’11 without shoes, so I ordered the 34 inseam. It’s the perfect length for me. I could’ve gone down a size. The size I ordered seems to be too big in one particular area; it’s giving me a vagina pouch (extra fabric in the crotch). The fabric is soft! And doesn’t seem to pick up lint excessively. However, I agree with the other reviewers that these aren’t that warm/thick, moreso t-shirt material for indoors or spring wear. I’ve only worn these twice so I can’t say how long they’d hold up after washing etc. However, I would order again due to the inseam options, overall fit, and price. It’s not easy to find nice and affordable pants for taller women. Give em a try :)

Buyer beware, some styles don't last! As a 5'9 woman with long legs, I was very happy to find cotton joggers with long inseam available. I purchased three pairs last year. The first pair I bought was charcoal grey with a 34" inseam, which has been my favorite and the one that has lasted the most. I also bought a black pair with a 31" inseam, and this year, I bought a light grey pair with a 31" inseam. The black pair didn't last more than a few months. After two washes, it looked like it had been worn 10 times and the fabric started to show little holes throughout. Needless to say, I had to throw them away. I bought the light grey pair back in April this year. Unfortunately, as you can see in the pictures, it already has a hole in the right pocket area. I have only worn them 4 times. I follow the wash instructions to a T and use mild soap, I'm also not rough on my clothes. I'm going to stitch the hole in the pocket and try to fix it but sadly, I won't be purchasing more joggers from this company.

Not 100% Cotton. Otherwise, Finally Long Enough. I am returning these because although they are advertised as 100% cotton, they are not (see picture). The body is 100% cotton, but the lining is 80% cotton/20% spandex. Sellers need to be accurate with fabric descriptions. Some people (me!) are sensitive to spandex/elastic fabrics, and an otherwise deliciously nice cotton feel becomes a constant irritation. Now the fit: I am 5'8", 150 lbs. with long legs, and the 34" inseam was actually too long, and bunched at the ankle. The waist was a tiny bit snug, but OK; and the leg width runs slim, not quite leggings-like but close. Additionally, a label describes the material as UPF 50+ sun protective.

She is normally a size 2 in jeans, so I went with the smalls and they are perfect. I also went with the 34" length and she is 5'9". She loves that they are actually a little baggy and plenty long enough. I will be ordering more for her and maybe some for myself.

Tall girls buy these!! I’m 6’2” and my legs take up a majority of my body. I’ve always had trouble finding pants long enough that I didn’t have to specially order because I’m on the skinnier side, but these have been my saving grace!! I wear a size 4-6 in women’s and a small fit perfectly. Ordered a 34” and these joggers are even longer than I thought they would’ve been. Material is stretchy and good quality. As some reviews said, these are like joggers not sweatpants.

Great fit! Love these! I’m 150lbs and 5’11, and long sweats can be hard to find! I love these! I will definitely buy a second pair. They are so soft, and they are the perfect length and they aren’t too tight across the hips or but. Haven’t washed them yet, but I’m sure they should be fine since I purchased the 34” inseam. GO DAWGS

Love these pants! I’m 5’9” mostly legs, 145 pounds. I’m a true medium in everything I order. I usually wear Nike athleisure, I decided to give these a try and I love them! The length is perfect, the quality is amazing and the color grey is spot on. I ordered medium, 34 length.

Love that us tall girls can FINALLY cover the lower half of our legs! Only thing, company might want to check the spelling on the environmentally conscious bags… ooops! 😳 BTW, this was for my 6’ tall daughter.

If you're tall, these are for you! These are AWESOME. I've purchased & returned several styles from several companies plus Amazon; I'm not exceptionally tall - just 5'9ish" but apparently, in terms of pants, I'm some kind of exotic Amazonian woman because pants that are comfortably long enough are like unicorns. These are a LIGHTWEIGHT sweatpant material - not a light, thin cotton and not a fleecy-lining that you'd think "sweatshirt" with. They're thick enough to be warm and comfortable and not see thru! Not only do they have POCKETS!! they actually have a little extra zippered pocket! Please excuse my swollen belly from surgery - it's making the waist area a bit more snug. I was suggested an XL, but the 34" inseam was only available in a L, so I figured I'd risk it, and I'm glad I did. Size up for a roomier fit, go with your normal size for a more trendy fit. I'm so thrilled with the length of these, I really cannot express it. Ever since the 7/8 and "ankle length" became a style, I've struggled to find anything that doesn't look like broken capris on me hahaha I'm also very pleased with the rise of the waist. Low rise should be abolished, and frankly my mom belly, even when it's not swollen, disagrees strongly with midrise 😂 They also come packaged very nicely, which is always nice!

Happy with these overall but here are some things I would have wanted to know before ordering: The drawstring is not functional. The ties are just for show. The pink is more of a dusty rose. They are already oversized so you don’t need to size up. They wrinkle like crazy with wear so don’t bother ironing them. Give up on having the fabric look smooth like the photo and just settle in to the wrinkled vibe. They are recommended to hang dry so do size up if you’re like me and don’t have the patience to hang dry casual wear. They will mostly shrink up length wise so keep that in mind if you’re tall. They arrived smelling like they were sewn by a cigarette and the smell didn’t completely come out after the first wash. All that being said, they are cute and comfy and would medium recommend them. Probably better options available but if you see these and like the style you’ll probably be happy with your purchase. Size reference: I ordered a large and have a size 24” waist so the elastic waist is forgiving in either direction. I could probably fit another 1.5 of me in here and be comfortable.

The waist is ridicously high! I recently tried the Women's Fleece Lined Joggers, and I have mixed feelings about them. On the positive side, the feel, comfort, and warmth are fantastic! They’re perfect for lounging around or staying cozy during colder days. However, I found the high rise of the waist to be quite uncomfortable. The waistband came up almost to my chest (and I am not a short person, 5'8"), and the width of it felt a bit excessive. This made it hard to get the fit just right. While I really appreciate the warmth and comfort, the design just didn’t work for me. Overall, they’re decent joggers, but I wish the fit was more comfortable!

Comfy feel, thin fleecing, runs big I got the SANTINY Women's Fleece Lined Joggers with Pockets in Medium based on the Size Guide. I'm 5'3", 128 lbs and it definitely runs big on me and is not slim fit. The waist band is 4" which is very wide so I fold it down for a more comfortable fit (see pics). The fabric is soft on the outside with soft, thin fleecing on the inside so it's not bulky or too warm.There are 3 large side pockets, with one being zippered to keep items from falling out. All the pockets easily fit my iPhone 14 Pro with room to spare. The drawstring isn't really necessary as the waist band elastic holds in place. Overall, the jogger is quite comfortable even with the large waist band. I just wish the fit wasn't so loose.

Love these, but poor quality stitching First the good… these are warm, soft, and comfortable, perfect for spring and fall. Sizing is correct too. I’m 5’2, 135#, waist 29, hips 39, and the medium fits great, even the length. These add a little bit of bulk, so if you’re trying to look slim, these aren’t what you’re looking for. These would be my favorite sweats if they weren’t already torn on the second wear… The sewing is poor quality, and things like that should be caught before sending defective products out. It’s not just that they have a hole in them, but the stitching simply isn’t good, as you can see in my picture. I deducted a star for that, but if they were well made, they would definitely have been a 5 star pair of sweat pants!

I haven’t worn these yet because it’s been too hot. I tried them on to see if they would fit well and be warm and comfortable in winter. The answer to those questions is a definite “yes.” I am 5’ 5” and weigh about 136. The medium was perfect in length and the waist was comfortable with a very slight adjustment of the drawstrings. They washed well on a cool water cycle in my washing machine. I dried them in the dryer on a low setting and they came out beautifully. I was a little puzzled by the tag inside (see photo). I like that the outer material has cotton and polyester, but I wasn’t sure what the “other ingredients” might be. However, I like them so much that I’m going to order another pair in black for winter.

Really cute VERY comfortable and soft, must buy! I am 7 months pregnant and am recently feeling huge and uncomfortable in everything I own. I ordered these pants fully intending not to wear them til after I have this baby and to my surprise they actually fit really nicely and I could see them still fitting great different but still great after I am no longer pregnant. They are SUPER soft and feel exactly like LLR leggings but have the nice flare at the bottom and feel like better quality than some thin LLR leggings I have had. They have a nice wide waist band which for us with mom bods is important and I suspect they will be flattering on my less round body. They do look pretty good even on my bloated pregnant body tho. The pattern I got definitely draws the eyes vertically making me look taller rather than wider. Only thing I could see being an issue for some people is I am a tall lady 5’8”-5’9” and they are long which I personally love but it is something to note if you’re significantly shorter than my height you may need to cut or hem them. Otherwise a great purchase. Full disclosure I just received them and only tried them on so if they don’t hold up after a few wears I will update. Update: I wore these all the way through the end of my pregnancy and my baby is now three months old I still wear them all the time, I bought 2 other colors as well. I basically live in these pants. They are as I thought they’d be, flattering while still being comfortable. They have held up super well, still soft and not faded or ripping, after heavy use and washes on many sanitize cycle washes.

So comfortable! Wow! These are incredibly comfy! I am 5'5" 155lbs. I bought a large and they fit me perfectly! I do not have a snached waist so I get annoyed when I buy my size and everything fits except the waist squeezes me so hard all my exta bits hang over. Nope! Not with these! They are gently snug enough to stay in place but not so much to be unflattering. I am so happy with this purchase I will be buying another pair. The material is thin and so soft. Perfect for sleeping or lounging around the house.

I’m never leaving these pants Ordered size Large. These just arrived so I cannot comment on quality after some wear and washings but the moment I put them on, they’re exactly what I was looking for. They feel a lot the the lularoe leggings to touch - buttery soft. They do have a very minor see through factor if you’re bending down wearing bright colored undies through the tie dye beige parts. I’m 5’6, 185lbs and typically wear a size 12 give or take a size depending on brand/fit. I carry most of my weight in my upper half and my legs have stayed fairly thin (were “chicken legs” as a kid). I often have a hard time finding pants snug on my legs that don’t give me a muffin top. Most pants that fit my waist are saggy in butt and legs (even w high waisted styles). The thick waste band hitting right above the belly button is comfortable and forgiving and the legs are nice and snug on my thighs ❤️ I wear a 33in inseam and there’s some probably an extra 1.5 inches touching the floor.

I am 5'5", weigh about 133, but u am bottom heavy so.... last year when I bought a small, my butt was stretching the seam threads. On me, the waist goes right over my belly button. If the autumn peach wasn't so off on the pattern matching up (I expected to get a symmetrically balanced pattern instead of the awful layout I got), I would have given these pants a higher rating. These are very comfortable, but a pattern alteration that extreme is ridiculous.

IN LOVE with these! Im 5'5 and 137lbs, ordered small. I haven't worm yoga pants (or flare leggings as people have been calling them lol) in a very long time. These had good reviews and the price is fantastic, so I went for it. Im in love with them! They are so comfy and perfect for wearing around the house or for running errands. Very very soft with a perfect fit! They are a great length for my height, albeit a little long without shoes. If you are shorter than 5'5 then you may have issue with length. I've seen some photos of people dressing these up, but in my opinion the material is more of a lounge wear type. Softer than your typical leggings. So keep that in mind if you're looking for flare pants to dress up/wear with heels...these probably aren't what you want! Anyways I love them and will be buying in another color too. Don't miss out on the great price!!

Not for me. Tried to upload a review but idk if it went through. As a 5’11 girly, I thought these would be a perfect length, but they still fall short on me. best pants ever I flippin love these pants. They're super soft, comfortable, and they look awesome. I have large thighs, so I was worried they wouldn't be flattering, but nah, they look great. They make you look tall and stylish, and the amount of flare at the bottom is perfect. Get them! I'm 143 lbs, 5'6'', 28'' waist, 39'' hips, and I first got a large, but after a year or so of wearing them and washing them, they stretched out and became a bit loose and floppy, so ordered them again in a medium, which fits much snugger and better. They're very stretchy, so you can afford to go down a size, but also thin, so I'd definitely recommend wearing seamless underpants.

Breathable, comfortable, and soft, but go above the bellybutton These pants are very soft and comfortable! I took away one star because of how high they go. Some people are ok with it, however, I don't want my lounge pants above my belly button. They're fine to roll down to be below the belly button though, and it doesn't feel bulky when they're rolled. I'm 5'3" and 117 lbs and the small fits me perfectly. They're long and flowy at the bottom, but snug in all the right places. (The boyfriend loves them!) They're breathable but not see through or thin enough that I'm concerned about holes appearing sooner than they should. All in all, they're great and I'll probably order another pair in a different color!

Cheap arrived with holes The product is cheaply made with cheap fabric. It arrived with holes at the waistband. The fit and cut were nice so I gave 2 stars instead of one. I copy/pasted excerpts from the description of the product and provided my comments for each point: 'COMFY & SUPPORTIVE HIGH WAISTBAND'.......Refer to my picture. The waist band is actually flimsy, and not supportive. Even so, the pants actually fit well. I am 5'5" 150lbs. I like to hide my expanding waistline and often have trouble finding pants that don't give me muffin top, camel toe and or a wedgie. Sorry if that's TMI but I know I'm not alone on that. These pants fit great in every regard. Therefore it is so disappointing that the quality was so bad. [SOFT PEACHSKIN FABRIC - The SATINA peachskin fabric is the softest material around...] Yes. it is soft; but also thin, see through and FLIMSY. [SEJORA IS NOW SATINA - The same brand you love and trust has a new name]..Look at the picture of the label. It says "Got Style"! Neither Sejora nor Satina. So I don't think I "TRUST" this brand at all. I will get the refund since it's an Amazon prime purchase. However what a waste of my time. Almost perfect These pants are a near dupe of the old navy mid rise pixie full length pant. Great length (I'm 5'7), and four REAL (albiet small) POCKETS! I say nearly perfect because there are some minor flaws. They are not quite as stretchy and comfortable as I had hoped. They are slightly too high in the front which makes them bunch in an unflattering way when seated. They are slightly too low in the back so when you sit there is definite whale tale potential if you're not careful (might not be an issue for those with smaller butts). That said, they are better than 90% of dress pants I have tried, and cheaper than nearly 100%. If they don't fade or wear out, I'll probably buy more colors.
