Better than I expected I was pleasantly impressed with these pants. I am 5'6", 29" waist, 40" (39") hips, 136lbs. These feel a bit like parachute pants. They are 17% nylon after all. But they do have a good amount of stretch to them. I wish they were a little more supportive in the rear and that might have been achievable maybe one size down if I thought they were really stretchy enough for that sort of feature but that's just wishful thinking for a forty-something y/o whose assets aren't where they used to be. The waistband is very comfortable, the length is just where I want it, and I think they look really good on my body. As for my measurements, I am 5'6" but I have short-ish legs and a long torso - specifically my "rise". Like, high-rise pants fit me more like mid-rise because there is over eleven inches between my crotch and my bely button. I mention this because these pants are a little tight in the crotch for me and have a little camel toe effect but the waist is so forgiving, I can adjust and pull them down whenever I feel them creeping up in there;) Plus they are black and it's not noticeable. I don't think that would be the case with lighter colors though which is a bummer because I like these pants so much I'm tempted to buy them in all the colors. Anyway, lots of reviews complaining about how they don't hold up to washing but it clearly states that these pants are best dry cleaned. I never dry clean anything so I'll probably wash on cold in the gentle or hand wash cycle and hang to dry. I included front (heeled and flat footed), back, and side pics because I know that even someone with the same measurements as me may have a different shape. Hope this info helps.

Comfortable Flowy Pants After ordering several pairs of this style pant and returning for various reasons, I wasn’t getting my hopes up but these were perfect! I ordered the small and the fit is perfect (5’4” 120ish) the waistband is not tight, I’ve got a little fluff right now and you can see from my pics they hit right above my ankle, I bought these to take to Greece in the hot weather and they were perfect (some places your legs must be covered, no shorts) the fabric is not sheer like a few pairs I tried, it’s a nice weight, definitely more cotton than linen and they hang loose like a skirt, pockets are nice and roomy. These seem very well made, they do need to be steamed after laundering, I laundered in cold water wash and hung outside to dry so I didn’t find that they shrank but again I didn’t put them in the dryer. I would definitely buy these again and I’m sure I will in some of the other colors, you can style with flip flops, sandals or tennis shoes and all look cute.

Wonderfully comfy, attractively cut, pleasantly priced I ordered these to wear as comfortable pants to wear around the house and to wear out with tunic tops. They are super comfortable and stretchy. I can see already that I’m going to be living in these leggings. They aren’t compression leggings, but they are a reasonable thickness, neither thin nor thick. I find boot cut pants more flattering on me than straight legged. These have a distinct, but not weirdly huge flare. I used the size chart, figuring that if it said a small was a 26” waist, then my 28-29” waist would do fine. It does—perfectly, without being snug at all. I’m only 5’ 1” and so wasn’t surprised that I’m going to need to hem them up at least 2 ½ inches. So, I’m guessing the size small is an ideal length for someone who is 5’ 4”. I’m not sure why these leggings were advertised as “tummy control.” They aren’t, although the wide stomach band (which you can see in the ad’s photos and video) is made of a double, rather than single, layer of fabric; so maybe that’s what the manufacturer had in mind by “tummy control.” The fabric is not slick, so if you have cats, don’t be surprised if you find cat hair sticking to it. I’m really happy with these leggings, which are so comfortable I don’t want to change out of them!

A little long & a little big - very comfy I'm 5'4 and have to pull these up about 3 inches above my belly button, and they're still an inch or two too long. I'm 262lb and a size 20/22 (more 22) in pants. I ordered a 3x and I really think I could wear a 2x with no problem. I had to tighten the drawstring on these a lot, but they're very comfortable. Thin Material - See-Through for Lighter Colors -UPDATED I was excited to try on these pants since I live in Southern Texas and it gets really hot and I thought these linen pants would be a great addition to my wardrobe.They came well packaged and the stitching on them is really nice. The problem is when you put them on you can see right through them due to how thin the material is. It's unfortunate since they are cute pants (a little tight around the waist/butt area and perfect fit around the legs) and comfortable. Too bad I can't wear these out in public.Update:The company reached out to me immediately and offered to send me a pair of the darker-colored pants to help resolve the transparency issue. A pair of black pants arrived the next day and they are perfect. The fit is nice (they are a little long but I am just shy of 5' tall so that's expected), the fabric is thin and breathable so it's great in hotter weather. With the darker color there was no transparency at all in the fabric. I would highly recommend the darker colors of these pants for anyone looking for some comfortable and cute pants to wear in the summer months.

NVGTN dupes at less than half the cost! These pants are amazing! I absolutely LOVE them. I have a couple NVGTN leggings with the smile lines and these are a comparable dupe at less than half the price. I own the dark blue pair (which is actually a light blue, not sure if this was a mistake or the colors are listed incorrectly on the ad but I was hoping for the dark blue ones) and I also have the dark grey pair. The leggings have a fairly tight compressive stomach, but the material on the glutes and legs are much stretchier and fits curves well. They are not see-through, thank god! I hate that. They hold up on my heavy leg days and I feel confident and beautiful in them. They definitely accentuate the curves, the smile lines you need to line up under your cheeks for the best effect, but once you have them where you want them they don't move around too much. I am 5'10 tall, 160 lbs, 40-27-42 and I got a size large. I usually wear a medium in leggings, but I got the medium in the purple and rust orange colour and they were not the same material as the grey and blue ones. The material was much thinner and the orange were completely see-through, as well as the color of them in the ad are a beautiful rust red-orange, but the actual leggings are neon orange! Like a highlighter. I hated the color, I wanted them to be rust orange because that is so unique and gorgeous. Oh well. I returned those. I would definitely recommend these to anyone who has been eyeing the NVGTN leggings but don't want to pay $50 for one pair of leggings. 10/10

TALL GIRL FRIENDLY, SQUAT FRIENDLY I have the all black pair and I LOVE them! I have 3 pairs and plan on purchasing more. They’re not see through at all. They run slightly small (which I prefer) and they snatch my waist! Im 5’10, 180lbs and they go all the way down to my ankles (honestly they go past that but I wear long socks so no need to pull them down further) I can do a HIIT workout + weight train and dont even have to pull these leggings up! Lunges, sprints, and squats are no match for these durable leggings! I highly recommend purchasing. Im back almost 2 years later and I just wanted to say BUYER BEWARE- YOU WILL GET ATTENTION WEARING THESE lmao. Theres no scrunch on the butt (which I like!!) so if you’ve been blessed below the belt, these will emphasize what you’ve been gifted lol. Still no complaints with these leggings. I’ve probably owned 10 pair over the past 2 years and 3 of them ripped on the inner thigh (normal for thick thighed ppl so not a bad thing tbh..) and they ripped after MONTHS of wear + washing and drying with high heat (also they dont shrink!) My least favorite place to wear them at is the airport cause they’re not the thickest pair of leggings so I would still be freezing even though my legs were fully covered. In summary, great pair of leggings! I still wear mine to this day :)

Good sweats for tall gals. These are soooo overpriced for what they are. It’s just regular sweats that most people can buy for $20-$25 but of course since we are tall we have no choice but to pay the “tall tax”. They are good, comfortable sweats. Very soft, not itchy. They have nice pockets with zippers. Hopefully they hold up in the wash. At first, I ordered the charcoal color, but it was not true to color. They were much darker in person. The heather gray ones are true to color. My only other complaint aside from price is that these would be a lot better if the waistband was an inch or two lower. High-rise looks great on jeans that pull everything in. However on sweats it does not look good. Of course I can roll the waistband over, but it just flips right back up with movement. I’m mid size. 5’10” and have 43” hips

Totally different from the first pair I ordered I LOVE these joggers. LOOOOOVE. But make that lovED, past tense, because I just ordered a new pair (since my original ones were getting worn out with so much wear) and they are NOTHING like the first ones I received nine months ago. I’m 5’8, 115 lbs with a 25-inch waist and I first ordered these in navy blue and light grey in size XS with a 32-inch inseam. They were sooooo soft and comfy-cozy (they looked and felt like cotton, per the description and label), fit PERFECTLY, and were ultra flattering. This new pair, on the other hand, feels super stiff and plasticky by comparison (not cottony, at all), is way thinner, and has a freaking huge waist. (The waist is so huge I checked the label to see if maybe I’d been sent a small instead of XS, and I even ordered yet ANOTHER pair in the hope that maybe it was just a fluke.) But no, the pair that followed was just as big, synthetic-feeling, and generally disappointing as the pair that preceded it. I am massively bummed because these are (were) my All Time Absolute Favorite Joggers Ever, but now it appears the vendor has replaced what was once a high-quality product with a cheaply made substitute. I don’t know if all sizes and colors have been replaced with inferior materials, inconsistent sizing, and misleading labeling as regards fabric content, but it sure appears to me that this is the case with the navy blue version. Sigh.

comfy, seems to be made well, maybe mislabeled??? I ordered the purple and navy both in XL with 30 inch inseam. Ultimately I preferred the purple pant fit and when I laid them out on top of each other, my impressions were confirmed. I measured the inseams of both. Navy was 30" but purple was 28" even though labeled as 30. I would say the navy pant legs were 4 inches wider than the purple. Additionally, I would say the navy had a long rise from crotch-to-waist as well. I ordered based on my measurements using the provided size chart (5'9", 180 lbs, 32" waist, 43" hip). Maybe I will try ordering an L with a 28" for other colors and better fit??? I think that particular purple was mislabeled.

If you have a small waist and curvy hips like Barbie these will be good. If you’re built like an average woman who is or has gone through menopause (kind of log shaped in the middle) you might try a size bigger than the XL. They are well-made and very comfy to lounge in or do yard work. I bought another pair after the first I liked them so much!

I guess not all good things come to those who wait I had high hopes for this... only to wait three months for them to finally be delivered (I almost cancelled the order outright, probably should have). When they finally did arrive, I was excited, but wasn't thrilled to find some defects. The Pros: •Long! •More lightweight material, not itchy, but not super soft either •Fit my body type (5'8", with a 26 inch waist) I like high-waisted pants. •Comfy The Cons: •The wait •Found some loose threads •Hole in the right pocket •Pockets bulge because of the zipper •After reading the reviews, I'm scared of putting these pants through the wash and having them shrink. If you do decide to get these, then order bigger on the inseam and waist size, I guess. Overall, I'm most likely going to return the pants, because of the defects and the possibility of them shrinking too much. I just wish I didn't have to wait so long to get them only to return them again. It's a shame... If I knew they'd stay this way then I wouldn't mind fixing up the holes.

Possible miss sized I ordered the charcoal in Medium 32" inseam. I am always a medium, sometimes a small. These were too snug in the waist. The waistband is wide and holds its shape. The waist measures 14" across which would make it a small according the size chart. I am reordering a large. Hoping that it is not too loose. The material and fit are luxurious. These are beautifully designed for tall, slim women. There is no bulk. The fabric is not heavy and perfect for winters in the south. The care is a little odd. Wash cold and line dry. I am 5' 8" 145 lbs and top heavy so my bulk is not in my hips or thighs. Update: the large fits.

Almost perfect If you’re looking for sweats for tall people, you probably found these. Here is my detailed review of these sweatpants. I am in between 5’8”-5’9” for my height. I followed the instructions they gave to measure your favorite pair of pants that fit well for the inseam measurement, my pants gave me a 31” measurement. Since the guide that was posted in the product listing says for heights in between 5’6”-5’9” should order 32” inseam that was what I ordered. I was hesitant to do this because the jeans I measured for the 31” inseam can bunch at my ankles, but I don’t mind that and it allows for full coverage when I bend my knees, so I figured I would trust the seller. They ended up being too long, which was a pleasant surprise since every other sweat stops at least 4 inches above my ankle. I would personally exchange them for a 30” inseam, if I liked the quality of the sweats. Let me talk about the quality of the sweats. The seams of these sweats make me want to take them off immediately. I may just be picky with my seams, but this is my review after all. The seams, specifically on the inside of my leg are just itchy, not unbearably itchy, but enough to notice the itch and notice that you are wearing pants. I love my sweats to be soft and cozy, not itchy. The material they use for the sweats is also just kind of thin. Not thin enough to were you can see my underwear, it just feels cheaply thin. The ends of the drawstring on the waistband is also just a cheap plastic cover. I also did not notice online that the back pocket is so small and that there is only one pocket in the back. I personally only use front pockets in my sweats, I see no point for a back pocket, especially one that is so small as this one is. It doesn’t even fit my whole hand and I have small hands for someone that is 5’8”/5’9”. I think these pants would be better if they just removed the pocket in the back. I also want to point out that the sweat just kind or gathers weird on me near my crotch and you can see that in the photos that I attached. Also why include a front seam? It’s just grounds for camel toe. What are some positives of these sweats?These sweats look extremely flattering on, I just wish they were a nicer quality because I am not going to wear a sweat that itches my legs. I personally love a thicker waistband and I would describe this waistband as perfection, it’s not itchy, it’s comfortable, and it's nice and thick. The zipper for the pockets also seems to be really nice, not the nicest zipper, but good enough. The front pockets are also nice and deep. Are these the worst pants I have ever tried? No. Am I going to keep these pants? No. I personally would rather have a sweat that feels nicer on my leg than one that covers my whole leg.

Runs large, I think? So first off, I'm 5'6", 165lbs. I have long legs and a short torso, making it difficult to find comfortable pants that fit well. I first bought the 33" inseam Medium and the waist was too long and wide. It was a comfortable fit if I rolled the waist band down, so I figured I'd try the small. Great fit! I like how thick the material is, and that there are pockets. It doesn't squeeze my midsection in an unflattering way, and the length is perfect for my legs. I'm so happy I found pants that are flattering, comfortable, and sooo stretchy! ***edit- these pants ended up shrinking in the dryer (I know they're not supposed to be dried in the dryer). So they are now too short. Also, I ended up finding the pockets were a bit annoying as they are not sewn in, they bunch up on the inside. I ended up buying the bubble lime yoga pants instead and am happier with these.

Not what I expected. Not flattering but comfortable I don't feel the product in the photos is the same as what arrived. The model in the photo displays a cut/style which is flattering to her thin body, so this is what we expected. The cut was different, it seemed. I went by seller recommendation to purchase size small for someone approx 5'4" 110 lbs. The length was to the floor but if they were washed and shrank as described, they'd be too short. The upper part of the pants were bulkier than pictured so they are not at all flattering. I think if one were thin, they'd make you look out of proportion and if one was fuller figured, they'd only make one look more heavy. The fabric is soft as any jersey material would be. These are not "sweatpants" as described. They are pajama and lounge around the house pants. I find they're less flattering than scrubs even, so I will just keep wearing scrubs for now and will return these because a larger size is just going to be more bulky in the hips which we definitely don't want. I hope this helps you decide on whether or not hits is the product for you. If you found my review , please mark it as . If you know of a product like this which is more flattering, please share a link in the comments. Thank so much =)

Like Wearing Clouds I almost gave this review 4 stars because of the three pack I bought, the grey ones came not completely sewed at the crotch seam...so bummed about that. Hopefully the seller will send me a new pair of grey ones. But Im still giving these pants 5 stars because I had to really be honest with myself- These pants will change your lounge life! They are so comfortable and so accommodating to a woman’s curvy body, it’s incredible! I want to wear these pants to work they are so comfortable but alas, I cannot - that would not be a good look at my Board Meetings lol! But I don’t want to take them off either! They are cotton but super stretchy. They have pockets on both sides which are great for cell phones and they are very long; I’m 5’8 and they hit the floor on me. I will be ordering these in every color available because they fit so well. My regular size is 20/22 so I ordered the 3x size in the three pack; they are the perfect fit! They are super stretchy also so they are perfect for everyone and everyday lounge life!

Disappointing I read the reviews about this product prior to ordering. I ordered the tan, black, and white pack in size XL. I normally take a size 12. According to the size charts, a large is 12-14, and an XL is 16-18...so I took the other reviewers' advice and sized up a full size. When I received them, they looked huge and way too long. However, as the other reviewers mentioned, I tossed them in the washer and dryer on hot water and high heat. They definitely came out shorter. I took them out as soon as they were done. They are soft like a jersey t-shirt. I expected them to be flimsy. Actually, they are a little heavier weight than I expected...definitely not see-through. I wanted to use these as a combination of indoor/outdoor pants, lounge pants, and pajama bottoms while hanging out at home during the pandemic. I am 5'6" and 160 pounds. Unfortunately, these pants have no spandex or anything to help hold their shape. So, when you wear them, they stretch out on you...get baggy in the knees and wider everywhere. It is not an attractive look. I now would recommend buying your regular size if you choose to buy these and wash/dry them on LOW. They might end up being a little long, but they can always be shortened. I would wash/dry them at least a couple times before I altered them though. If you get your regular size, they will fit better and not "grow" on you as much as they stretch out. Actually, I'm sorry I purchased these pants. I am just using them for pajama bottoms.

Perfect PJ bottoms These are great! I bought an XL and I’m 5’5” and weight 190. I bought these because I find that after having a baby, you need pockets to carry stuff and the baby. Why don’t more women’s PJs have pockets? Men aren’t the only people who want to carry stuff. Fō reals. Many review say they’re long and will shrink when you wash/dry them. They did and they shrank to the perfect length! Update: still love them and they still shrunk to fit my height but for the sticklers, the length does vary from each pant about an inch. I don’t really care but I thought I’d mention it.

GREAT pants, but size up because they shrink I love these pants! They’re very comfortable - for bed, lounging, or a quick run to the store - and they have pockets big enough that my cell phone doesn’t fall out when I bend over. However, they do shrink a size. I wear an XL and bought a XXL pack. When I first put them on, they were really loose and reached all the way to my feet (I’m 5’9”). Now that they’ve been washed and dried a couple of times, they fit more like my usual XL pants and only go to my ankles. I wondered if maybe I just put on weight, but I recently bought another set and the new ones are loose like I remembered. (In the pics you’ll see my older, shrunken gray pair and the newer, unshrunken tan pair.) SO, buy a size up if you want it in your size after it shrinks; otherwise it will shrink to a size too small.

Good medium weight pants I bought a second set because I like these so much. I hadn't realized how much the first set had shrunk! Lightweight, comfortable, and cute—size down; runs large I am in love with these pajama bottoms. They run super huge. My waist is 27 inches and my hips are 37 inches; I’m 5’6 and 125 lbs. I ordered these in the smallest available size and they still puddle around my feet. However, I fully anticipated this and it doesn’t bother me. I will say the material is more polyester looking in person than in the product images but they are so absurdly comfortable. I ordered another pair in a different pattern as well. They’re lightweight so I can wear them during the summer.

Bought these knowing they would be long & I was ok with that. I’m 5’6” & they were dragging on the ground. The material is thin & it’s perfect for pajamas or just Kickin back by a camp fire. After I wash them I do hang dry them - dont want to take a chance of ruining them in the dryer. Absolutely love these pants!! Highly recommend for casual wear around the house.

HARD PASS - RETURNED Purchased these after a popular YouTuber was raving about them. Just no. No, no, no. They look SO cheap. The material is borderline shiny/metallic and feels like a Halloween costume. I am 5’5 and typically wear a size 6. I ordered a medium for reference, and they are way to short and look HORRIBLE on me. I understand they are lounge wear, but they aren’t even comfy. They are oddly fitting, they give a loose but clingy in weird spots kind of vibe. Basically the look of staticky clothing - but without the static. Bottom line, I think they are horrible. They aren’t expensive at all - so I’m not expecting some super lush high quality materials - but they aren’t even comfortable. So unless hiked up, shiny, Halloween costume feeling fabrics are your jam - it’s a hard pass.

I have purchased probably 10 over the years. BUY THESE. even if you choose the wrong size you’ll want to keep them and buy another. I LIVE in these. They are cute and comfy, and my husband calls them my “bae pants” because of how they hug my bum, lol. Not so much anymore! I’m 5’3” When I was 320 lbs I bought xxl and they hugged and curved in all the right places. As I lost weight I got xl but it got too big fast. I’m now 200 and the large looks like in the photos. I just ordered a medium even though the larges are fine, I just want them to hug my curves like the xxl did. L is perfect for lounging, I just want a tight pair too! There’s a lot of stretch and give so in my opinion don’t be afraid to size down or get your actual size. I wouldn’t size up, unless you plan on wanting them big and not form fitting. I like to even wear them out of the house, lol, so I like to size down to look less frumpy even though I’m wearing pajamas. The quality is wonderful for the price, but they do eventually wear down. I wear them constantly though! So I buy a new pair when that happens and have no regrets! I would be so sad if they ever stopped selling these.
